result.position: YES — an unsafe escape hatch exists, in exactly one form: block-scoped unsafe regions, each carrying a machine-readable obligation manifest that names the specific proof obligations (from the language's per-fact soundness ledger) the block claims to discharge, with a rationale, full generation provenance, and content-addressed identity. The AI writes them; the toolchain treats every block as a first-class auditable artifact; debug builds dynamically check every declared obligation for which a check exists, trapping into the mandated trap-with-report channel. A bare, manifest-free unsafe block is not representable.
result.coreArgument:
1) EXISTENCE IS ALREADY ENTAILED BY THE DECIDED CORPUS; ONLY THE FORM IS OPEN. The unsound-optimization threat model's own verifiability column reads 'sometimes no' (raw pointer casts), 'often no' (int-ptr casts), and 'no' (FFI callbacks), with 'unsafe boundary' listed as the remedy — the compiler provably cannot verify everything the periphery must do (implementation/unsound-optimization-threat-model.md). Round 2's modules verdict makes this concrete language surface: 'fact-boundary declarations (FFI, unsafe, reflection restriction...) must exist as core constructs even with modules gone' and its follow-up orders the design of 'fact-boundary declaration constructs (unsafe regions, FFI extern regions, reflection capability grants) as core-language surface' (debates/round2-modules.md, lines 15 and 42); the round-2 synthesis carries this into its replacements list verbatim (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md line 59). Round-1 ir-strategy independently orders 'the fact-boundary/escape-hatch representation (unsafe regions, interior mutability, FFI)' as an architecture-invariant artifact 'needed under any outcome' (round1-ir-strategy.md line 62). The no-hatch alternative does not eliminate this code, it relocates it: round-1 concurrency-model found every position 'bootstraps on permanently-trusted unsafe shared-memory code' — 'trusted-base placement, not elimination' (round1-concurrency-model.md line 40). A language with no hatch ships the same trusted code as compiler intrinsics or a foreign-language runtime kernel, where this language's verifier, provenance system, and debug checkers cannot see it. Under D1's own make-bad-states-unrepresentable logic, the manifest form makes UNAUDITED trust unrepresentable.

2) D1 IS SATISFIED, NOT NEGOTIATED, BECAUSE D1'S OWN REFERENCE BAR IS ARCHITECTED THIS WAY. D1 names Rust-class static enforcement as the bar, and the corpus's adversarially-verified exemplar of that bar, F001, guarantees exclusivity for SAFE Rust with a negative scope that explicitly excludes 'unsafe/raw-pointer/UnsafeCell details.' The Rust-class guarantee IS the conditional guarantee: safe code cannot exhibit the D1 bug classes provided the confined unsafe fragment discharges its obligations. Round-1 aliasing confirmed the confinement architecture as best-attested (F001, F006) and the round-1 aliasing critique explicitly preferred 'localized greppable unsafe blocks' over P2's 'every ordinary signature becomes a UB-bearing trust point' (round1-aliasing-model.md, P2 weaknesses). My form strengthens the bar: D1 violations remain unrepresentable in the entire safe fragment; in the unsafe fragment, each potential violation is converted from ambient silent UB into a NAMED obligation that is build-rejected if the manifest is absent or malformed, debug-trapped where dynamically checkable, and enumerated for audit where not.

3) THE MANIFEST IS THE ONLY HATCH FORM COMPATIBLE WITH THE ROUND-2 VERIFICATION SPINE. The spine rule is: the verifier 'rejects any optimizer fact not originating from a stated-and-checked or checker-derived source; law/effect annotations must be checked obligations, not trusted assertions — there is no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed' (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md line 33). A bare Rust-style `unsafe {}` — an unnamed ambient license — is precisely the banned third state. The manifest converts the hatch into STATED obligations: each fact the verifier will consume across the block boundary (a noalias claim per F003/F004, an overflow-freedom claim per N002, a range/inbounds claim per N004/N005, a Send/Sync capability claim per C004) appears as a named ledger entry with the per-fact truth conditions F003's and F004's negative scopes demand ('only sound if frontend obligations are true'). This is the ir-strategy 'soundness-obligation ledger' instantiated at the boundary where round-1 says real-world noalias miscompiles historically arise (round1-ir-strategy.md lines 50, 62).

4) THE FORM IS THE DIRECT INSTANTIATION OF THE ROUND-1 VIOLATION-SEMANTICS LEADING CANDIDATE. Position 3 (amended) — the named leading candidate — requires asserted contracts 'only in per-fact explicitly marked contexts, dynamically checkable in debug builds,' with conservative no-contract fallback at unverifiable boundaries (round1-violation-semantics.md line 7). Per-fact marked context = the obligation manifest; debug dynamic checking = the checked tier; conservative fallback = obligations that cannot be named against the ledger force conservative emission (no fact crosses the boundary). The checkable subset has carded mechanisms: A005 runtime pointer-range checks + versioning for loop-bounded aliasing obligations; N002/N004/N005 per-operation channels for overflow/range/inbounds; C002's Go race-detection halts as the carded trap-with-report precedent, landing in the trap=abort + machine-readable trap report channel round 2 already mandates.

5) EVERY CLASSIC OBJECTION TO MANIFESTS IS PRICED AT ZERO OR ALREADY PAID UNDER D0 AND ROUND-2 LAW. Annotation burden — the historical killer per F006's negative scope ('usability costs') — is inadmissible: D0 makes verbosity free and machine-writer costs admissible. Toolchain novelty is marginal: round 2 already mandates provenance-checked generation (consistency-layer mechanism 2), content-addressed canonical artifacts, machine-readable diagnostics with node-path references, and contract-edit gating; the manifest rides all four. Contract-edit gating applies identically to manifests: an AI writer cannot launder a hallucinated block by rewriting its manifest to match, any more than it can rewrite a signature (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md line 71). Manifests are also a checkable-redundancy channel of exactly the kind round 2 requires 'since same-pass writer output is error-correlated with itself' (line 68): the block body and its manifest are two statements of intent that debug checks and audits can diff. Auditability is not a human-ergonomics luxury smuggled past D0 — it is the machine-consumable interface for the fuzz/verification harness round 2 already requires, and the only carded route to post-violation observability (C002 precedent) at the one place D1's static guarantee is conditional.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Adversarially verified (3-0): safe Rust's &mut exclusivity makes overlapping mutation impossible — but its negative scope explicitly excludes unsafe/raw pointers/UnsafeCell. D1's own reference bar is a conditional guarantee architected around a confined hatch; my position preserves that architecture and hardens the condition.

noalias attributes and alias.scope/noalias metadata are UB-if-false optimizer contracts, 'only sound if frontend obligations are true.' The obligation manifest is the named, machine-readable home for exactly those per-fact truth conditions at the one boundary where the checker cannot derive them.

Return-noalias is allocation-grade strength but 'source languages need lifetime/provenance/escape rules before emitting it' — an unsafe allocator/FFI shim emitting this fact needs a named discharge obligation, which the manifest supplies and the ledger types.

SPARK proves the pattern: facts true by construction only because a checking toolchain enforces DECLARED restrictions — declared, checked obligations rather than ambient trust. Its negative scope (usability costs) is the historical anti-manifest argument, and D0 voids it: verbosity is free for an AI writer.

Go's race-detection halts are the corpus's only carded post-violation observability precedent; the debug tier's obligation checks trap into the same trap-with-report channel round 2 already mandates. C002 also leaves residue race semantics open — which my form names per-block rather than resolves.

Send/Sync + exclusivity is the carded static route to data-race freedom; in Rust practice, manual Send/Sync impls are unsafe obligations. Manifest entries for capability claims are how that route's trusted edge stays auditable. (Provisional card, not workflow-confirmed — inherited weakness, conceded.)

LLVM's atomic-ordering grading and undef-for-racing-loads show defined middle points exist below full UB; the manifest's per-obligation granularity lets the residue semantics question (UB vs bounded) be decided per obligation class later without changing the hatch's form.
nuw/nsw are per-operation, natively checkable contract channels — overflow-freedom obligations declared in a manifest have a direct debug-check lowering with a sound (unflagged) fallback.
range metadata is a per-value fact channel with poison-on-violation — a manifest-declared range obligation is dynamically checkable at the block boundary in debug builds.
inbounds GEP contracts give pointer-arithmetic obligations a named IR target; non-inbounds is the conservative fallback when a manifest declines to claim the obligation.

The refinement lattice shows a false contract has unbounded blast radius by construction — the strongest argument that unsafe assertions must be localized, named, and probed rather than ambient: blast radius is bounded socially (audit surface) even where it cannot be bounded semantically.

Runtime pointer checks + loop versioning are LLVM vectorizers' standard shipped legality strategy — the carded existence proof that declared aliasing obligations CAN be dynamically checked (in loop-bounded form), i.e., the debug tier is not hypothetical for the highest-value obligation class.

The leading candidate (Position 3 amended) — asserted contracts only in per-fact explicitly marked, debug-checkable contexts with conservative fallback at unverifiable boundaries — IS my position instantiated at the unsafe boundary; its named evidence gaps (lines 48, 54, 62) are my evidence gaps.

Leading candidate is checked ownership with trust residue in 'localized greppable unsafe blocks,' judged superior to making every signature a UB-bearing trust point (P2 weaknesses); the frontier's existence (pointer-rich structures, callbacks, FFI) is affirmed while its size is flagged unmeasured.

Lines 40 and 47-49: every position 'bootstraps on permanently-trusted unsafe shared-memory code' — trusted-base placement, not elimination. Refutes 'no hatch' as an option: it merely exports the trusted base beyond the verifier's, provenance system's, and debug checkers' reach.

Lines 50 and 62: no card covers fact boundaries/escape hatches, and the 'soundness-obligation ledger (per-fact truth conditions per F003/F004 negative scopes)' plus 'fact-boundary/escape-hatch representation (unsafe regions...)' are ordered as artifacts needed under ANY outcome — the manifest is that ledger made surface syntax.

Line 15 rules fact-boundary declarations '(FFI, unsafe, reflection restriction...) must exist as core constructs even with modules gone'; line 42 orders 'unsafe regions' specified as core-language surface. The existence question is thus already presupposed by round-2 verdict text; the debate's live content is form.

The verification spine (line 33: no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed) bans bare unsafe blocks and forces the stated-obligation form; lines 59, 68, 71, 73, 76 supply the machinery the manifest rides: fact-boundary constructs as core surface, checkable redundancy channels, contract-edit gating, machine-readable trap reports, and provenance infrastructure.

The verifiability column ('sometimes no' / 'often no' / 'no' for raw pointer casts, int-ptr casts, FFI callbacks) with 'unsafe boundary' as the listed remedy is the corpus's direct statement that a compiler-unverifiable residue exists and must be fenced, not verified.

Standing rules honored: Rust findings 'must carry their caveats: unsafe escape hatches...'; the Nomicon is explanatory, not a complete formal aliasing model; and any proposal must state what remains unproven — which this case does in its concessions and gaps.

concessions: The dynamic-check tier may be thin exactly where the payoff is highest: A005 covers only loop-bounded pointer-range checks; general scoped-noalias disjointness is a whole-execution property with NO carded runtime check, and purity has no dynamic-check evidence at all (round1-violation-semantics.md lines 48-49, 62-63). 'Debug builds check declared obligations where checkable' is honest but the checkable subset's coverage is unmeasured — for aliasing-class obligations the debug tier could currently be mostly vacuous, degrading those manifest entries to audited-but-unprobed assertions.

Every claim that AI writers will produce honest, accurate obligation manifests — rather than plausible-sounding manifest theater — is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED; the corpus has zero AI-codegen evidence (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md line 3). Contract-edit gating and redundancy diffing mitigate laundering but cannot detect a coherently-wrong body+manifest pair where no dynamic check exists.

A strict reading of D1 ('impossible in accepted programs', unconditionally) would exclude any assertion-conditional guarantee. My position holds D1 in the conditional form its own named reference bar (Rust-class enforcement, F001) actually provides — safe fragment unconditionally clean, unsafe fragment obligation-conditional with the obligations named and probed. If the project owner intends the stronger unconditional reading, the hatch must shrink to the threat model's other remedies (prohibit + conservative fallback) plus a foreign-language runtime kernel, and I concede that is a coherent, more restrictive resolution — at the cost of moving the identical trusted code outside this language's audit machinery.

The manifest form does not resolve the open residue race-semantics question (UB vs bounded outcomes for the unsafe fragment, round1-concurrency-model.md line 7 residue; C002 negative scope) — it only names and localizes where that decision applies. The form is compatible with either answer, but the answer is still owed.

C004, the static route to the D1 race-freedom half, is a provisional Nomicon-sourced extraction, not workflow-confirmed (corrected in round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md line 83); no RustBelt-class formal safe-fragment DRF theorem is carded. The manifest boundary inherits this uncertainty: obligations are named against a fact vocabulary whose formal model is unsettled.

The named-obligation ledger cannot be finalized before memory-object-model.md's prerequisite questions (object identity, provenance, interior-pointer stability, uninitialized bytes, FFI invalidation) are answered — you cannot name a provenance obligation before the provenance model exists. The manifest's vocabulary is downstream of undesigned artifacts, including the FFI fact-attenuation design round 2 flags as an UNDESIGNED soundness precondition.
risks: Manifest theater at scale: an AI writer under performance pressure emits syntactically valid, semantically vacuous discharge rationales; where no dynamic check exists (whole-execution noalias, purity) nothing in the toolchain falsifies them, and N001's lattice means one false obligation still licenses unbounded silent miscompilation in release builds. The audit artifact could create unwarranted confidence — worse than a bare block that at least advertises its own opacity.

Unsafe-region proliferation / marking metastasis: no empirical evidence exists on whether block discipline holds under performance pressure or on defect density inside audited assertion regions (round1-violation-semantics.md line 54). If the optimizer-first workload's frontier is large, manifests multiply until the audit surface is the program.

Debug/release divergence: the debug tier tests semantics that are not shipped; divergence-escape rates are completely unevidenced (round1-violation-semantics.md line 40). A passed debug suite over checked obligations may systematically miss release-only interleavings — especially since trap meaning under racing threads is itself unspecified (round2 constraint on violation-semantics, line 82).

Ledger versioning rot: as the fact vocabulary evolves (memory-object-model decisions, aliasing-model round 2, FFI wall design), previously accepted manifests become mis-specified against renamed or re-scoped obligations; without mandatory re-validation on ledger change, the provenance system preserves stale claims with high apparent authority.

Cost concentration at the checked tier: trap-intrinsic costs, check+flag co-emission soundness (dominance ordering), and whether passed checks recover assumption-grade optimization are all uncarded (round1-violation-semantics.md lines 47, 61); if debug obligation checks are too slow, the AI iteration loop routes around debug builds and the observability story silently dies — the same failure mode as the multi-process trap=abort harness risk round 2 already accepts (line 72).
evidenceGaps: Debug-mode dynamic checkability uncarded: no cards on Miri/UBSan/TSan-class detection of false noalias/inbounds/nsw/purity assertions or their cost — this single gap decides whether the manifest's dynamic tier exists for the obligations that matter most (round1-violation-semantics.md lines 48, 62).

Frontier size unmeasured: unsafe/UnsafeCell/raw-pointer density in performance-critical Rust code and SPARK ownership-discharge rates (round1-aliasing-model.md lines 45, 59; round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md line 99) — the variable that decides whether manifests are a manageable audit surface or the whole program.

Zero cards on manifest-like precedents in the wild: Rust's SAFETY-comment convention and clippy's undocumented_unsafe_blocks lint, Rust unsafe defect-density studies, and the RustBelt semantic-obligation formalization are the closest real-world exemplars of obligation-annotated unsafe, and all are uncarded background knowledge requiring adversarial verification before this form can pass a decision gate.

AI-writer manifest fidelity: whether an AI writer can reliably emit accurate obligation discharges, and whether manifest-vs-body redundancy actually catches correlated hallucination, is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED with zero corpus evidence — needs a dedicated experiment alongside the round-2 flagship drift experiment (inject known-unsound blocks; measure manifest-mismatch detection rate and vacuous-rationale rate).

Checked-tier mechanics uncarded: trap-intrinsic cost, soundness of co-emitting checks with nsw/range/inbounds flags, and whether llvm.assume after a passed check recovers assumption-grade optimization (round1-violation-semantics.md line 47) — prices the debug tier and the A005-style release-mode versioning option.

The obligation ledger's target vocabulary is blocked on undesigned artifacts: memory-object-model.md prerequisites (provenance, object identity, uninitialized bytes) and the FFI fact-attenuation design (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md lines 23, 34) — the manifest cannot name obligations that have no defined truth conditions yet.

Rust noalias enable/disable/miscompile incident history uncarded (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md line 102) — the strongest available natural experiment on what false boundary obligations actually cost in production, directly sizing the blast radius the manifest form is meant to bound.

result.position: Declared trust contracts at the single FFI wall: every foreign function carries a writer-stated, machine-readable effect/aliasing/lifetime/thread contract (reads/writes/escapes/threads/no-unwind/re-entry), with the maximally conservative contract as the undeclared default. Internal facts attenuate mechanically and exactly per declaration; every optimizer fact derived through a declaration carries its identity in the verifier's provenance chain. Wrong declarations are the accepted, explicitly marked residual unsoundness — quarantined to the enumerable declared surface and auditable by taint, which is precisely the Rust-class soundness shape D1 names as the bar.
result.coreArgument: The fact-attenuation problem is that internal proofs (checked ownership, noalias, effect rows, Send/Sync-class capabilities) are conditional theorems: they quantify over all behaviors of code the checker saw. Foreign code was not seen, so at every wall crossing the compiler must decide which theorems survive. The wall prevents external falsification in two moves. FIRST, by construction it eliminates every non-call channel: the round-2 closed-world floor kills interposition (D008), separate ABI (D004/D007), dynamic loading, and reflection (D005 negative scope, per-construct capability grants); foreign unwinds abort at the boundary (round2-error-handling); so the ONLY channel through which external behavior can touch internal state is a wall crossing. SECOND, each crossing is mediated by a declared frame. This is not one option among several — the corpus's own threat model already prescribes it: the FFI row lists exactly 'effects/alias/lifetime', marks compiler verification 'no', and names 'boundary contracts / conservative fallback' as the remedy. Declared trust contracts ARE that remedy, unified: the undeclared default is the worst-case frame (callee may write all reachable memory, retain every passed pointer, publish to other threads, re-enter), which is the round-1 consensus conservative fallback all three violation-semantics positions endorsed at unverifiable boundaries; every declared relaxation (this callee only reads arg1, escapes nothing, touches no threads) is an explicit, named, per-function trust extension. Attenuation is then mechanical: kill or downgrade every internal fact whose support intersects the declared writes/escapes/threads sets; facts disjoint from the frame survive and may still lower to F003-class noalias/alias.scope metadata, whose omission-is-always-sound structure is exactly the attenuation primitive needed. Three arguments make this the strongest position. (1) NO ZERO-TRUST OPTION EXISTS. Even the purely conservative wall trusts the foreign callee to respect the base ABI, not scribble over memory it was never given, and not unwind through internal frames — an ambient trust layer no rival design escapes. And the decided round-2 floors already force declarations: checked ownership plus explicit storage contracts mean a pointer cannot even be passed outward without stating the ownership/lifetime consequence (does it escape or not?) — otherwise the borrow state after the call is undefined and the call cannot be typed. Pure conservatism without declarations is therefore not merely slow (every passed &mut permanently poisoned, every call a full optimization and threading barrier, contradicting D0 extreme performance); under the ownership floor it is inexpressible. The design freedom was never trust-vs-no-trust; it is whether the trusted surface is implicit, monolithic, and unauditable, or explicit, minimal, per-function, and enumerable. (2) IT IS THE REFERENCE BAR'S OWN SHAPE. D1 names Rust-class static enforcement as the bar, and Rust's soundness statement is exactly this position's: safe code is UB-free provided declared unsafe surfaces (unsafe impl Send/Sync, extern blocks) uphold their stated invariants — C004 cards both the mechanism (writer-declared capability predicates the optimizer consumes to treat non-atomic memory as unshared) and the residual (incorrect manual impls are UB). F005/F001 card the wider precedent family (Fortran dummy-argument conformance, __restrict__, SPARK declared restrictions): writer-declared non-interference is the best-attested optimizer enabler in the corpus, and round-1 ruled the safe-layer-proves/unsafe-asserts confinement split its best-attested architecture (F001/F006, with unconfined F005 as the cautionary contrast). D008 even cards a declared trust regime at a linkage boundary (-fno-semantic-interposition = 'assume interposed definitions preserve semantics'). Declared FFI contracts are the confined form of this family: assertion permitted only at the one marked wall, conservative default everywhere else. (3) IT IS THE ONLY POSITION THAT MAKES THE RESIDUAL AUDITABLE. The round-2 verification spine rejects any optimizer fact not stated-and-checked or checker-derived; the wall adds exactly one sanctioned third class — stated-and-trusted — provided every fact derived through it carries the declaration's identity in its provenance chain (infrastructure round-2 already mandates for diagnostics). The closed world plus single wall makes the complete trusted surface enumerable: the audit for any suspected miscompile is 'list the declarations whose taint reaches this code', a computable set. Each declaration is a small, testable proposition an AI toolchain can lint against foreign headers, fuzz under sanitizer builds, version-pin to the foreign library it describes, and protect with the already-mandated contract-edit gating so a writer cannot launder a convenient wall contract. Internal proofs are thus never falsified silently from the side (no channel exists) and never falsified anonymously through the wall (every trust extension is a named, dated, machine-readable artifact whose falsity is the unique root cause of any resulting violation). D1's envelope statement becomes: memory and thread safety are impossible in accepted programs, conditional on the finitely many declared wall contracts — the same conditional Rust ships under, made stricter by having exactly one wall and mandatory conservative defaults.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: The corpus's own threat model marks FFI as breaking effects/alias/lifetime facts, compiler-verifiable 'no', with remedy 'boundary contracts / conservative fallback' — naming both halves of this position (declared contracts + conservative default) as the prescribed remedy and fixing the fact vocabulary the declaration must cover.

Rules that the FFI wall's fact-attenuation must be designed and carded BEFORE the closed-world commitment hardens, that fact-boundary declaration constructs (FFI extern regions) must exist as core surface, and that function-level contract-bearing signatures carrying the fact vocabulary (ownership, effects, aliasing) are non-optional — the wall contract is the same signature vocabulary with discharge switched from checked-against-body to declared-trusted, since no body exists.

Cards the exact declared-trust structure in the reference-bar language: Send/Sync are writer-declared capability predicates the optimizer consumes (treat non-atomic memory as unshared unless types say otherwise), with negative scope stating incorrect manual impls are UB — proving Rust-class enforcement, D1's named bar, already carries declared-surface residual unsoundness of precisely this shape.

LLVM noalias/alias.scope are the IR channels declared facts lower to; violation is UB and metadata omission is always sound — omission-by-default is the mechanical attenuation primitive: the compiler simply suppresses emission of every fact the declaration does not preserve.

Fortran dummy-argument rules are the carded precedent for writer-declared, compiler-assumed conformance contracts as optimization enablers — evidence the mechanism produces real optimizer permission, while its unconfined form supplies the cautionary contrast motivating confinement to one wall.

Safe-Rust &mut exclusivity plus SPARK anti-aliasing establish the checked interior the wall protects; F001's negative scope (depends on programmer truthfulness in the unchecked regime) honestly prices what a declaration is: the same non-interference fact minus the checker.

Semantic interposition shows boundary semantics can silently falsify optimizer assumptions about statically-known code, and -fno-semantic-interposition is a carded precedent for a declared trust regime at a linkage boundary; the closed-world cut eliminates this channel internally, concentrating all attenuation at the one declared wall.

Every carded compilation/linkage boundary is a fact-loss pathology or its expensive cure — the reason round 2 collapsed all boundary load onto a single wall, which is what makes the trusted surface small enough to enumerate and audit.

Negative scope (reflection metadata constrains closed-world assumptions unless restricted) grounds the per-construct capability-declaration pattern the wall generalizes: open-world abilities exist only where explicitly granted.

All three positions converged on conservative no-contract fallback at unverifiable boundaries (FFI, per the threat model), and the verdict names safe-layer-proves/unsafe-asserts confinement the best-attested split (F001/F006 vs F005) — this position instantiates both: conservative default plus confined, marked assertion at the wall only.

Names the single explicit FFI wall with declared fact-attenuation as a soundness precondition (currently UNDESIGNED), mandates the verifier's fact-provenance rule and contract-edit gating, and supplies the provenance/diagnostics infrastructure that makes declaration-taint tracking and gated wall edits implementable rather than aspirational.

C003 lists the optimizer freedoms lost whenever memory may be shared — the concrete payoff of a declared 'threads: none' frame letting facts about unescaped memory survive a call; C002 marks race semantics open, bounding what the thread component of the contract can currently promise.

IR-level gradation (racing non-atomic loads are undef, not instant UB) shows the substrate offers graduated blast-radius vocabulary for lowering calls whose thread declarations are weak, so attenuation need not be all-or-nothing at the IR level.

The abort-at-boundary rule for foreign unwinds (provisional, flagged evidence-free) is the no-unwind component of the wall contract: one dimension of foreign behavior already ruled to be declared and enforced by shim rather than trusted ambiently.

Remands open-set dispatch (FFI callbacks, D008-class boundaries) to the periphery and names its fact-attenuation an undesigned soundness precondition — the callback direction of the wall must carry re-entry declarations, and this verdict already assigns that design to the declared-boundary mechanism.

'Trust-boundary placement, not existence: all sound variants converge on checked discharge; what differs is where the unchecked residue lives' — establishing that some unchecked residue at FFI/callback boundaries is common to every admissible design, so the debate is whether the residue is declared and localized (this position) or implicit and diffuse.

SPARK's toolchain-enforced declared restrictions on subprograms are the carded precedent for contracts as first-class checked artifacts; at the wall the same declaration form persists with the checker's discharge replaced by trust, keeping one fact vocabulary across the whole program.

concessions: 'Quarantined' means attributable, not consequence-contained: round-1's strongest surviving semantic point (N001 refinement-lattice reasoning) is that a false UB-bearing contract has unbounded, silent, non-local miscompilation blast radius. A wrong wall declaration can corrupt arbitrary internal state; what the position bounds is the audit surface (the enumerable, provenance-tainted set of declarations that could be the root cause), not the damage.

The position is only defensible with the maximally conservative frame as the undeclared default and every relaxation explicit; a permissive-default variant fails the round-1 consensus (conservative fallback at unverifiable boundaries) and I do not defend it.

D1 must be restated honestly as conditional: memory/thread safety is impossible in accepted programs GIVEN the truth of finitely many declared wall contracts. This is exactly Rust's shape (C004 negative scope) and hence within the stated bar, but it is weaker than an unconditional reading of 'impossible', and the user directive explicitly delegates this envelope question to round 3 rather than treating it as settled.

Process/SFI isolation with copy-in/copy-out genuinely enforces the memory dimension rather than trusting it, and plausibly dominates for untrusted third-party libraries; I concede it should be expressible as the bottom tier of the declaration vocabulary (an 'isolated' contract) and composable with this design, at unmeasured performance cost against D0.

There is an ambient trust floor no declaration expresses (ABI conformance, no writes to never-passed memory, callee-save discipline): 'facts attenuate exactly per declaration' is true only above that floor, which every rival design shares but none can audit.

Per the corpus's own decision gates, this entire area is below design_candidate status: ffi_abi_runtime is missing_primary_source (priority-high), so this position is a design lean for round 3, not promotable to law until that backlog is discharged.
risks: AI-writer declaration hallucination (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED): the wall is the one place with no body to re-check against, so the coherent-hallucination failure mode round2-modules warns about has no redundancy channel by definition; contract-edit gating, header-derivation tooling, and sanitizer-build conformance fuzzing are mitigations with zero corpus evidence.

Declaration drift under foreign-library evolution: a contract true of libfoo v1 may be false of v2; without version-pinned, provenance-carrying wall contracts an upgrade silently falsifies internal proofs. Toolchain design exists on paper (round-2 provenance infrastructure) but is untested.

Optimism metastasis under performance pressure: round-1 explicitly lists 'whether explicit-marking discipline holds under performance pressure' as unevidenced; an AI writer optimizing for benchmarks may systematically declare the strongest admissible frames.

Vocabulary insufficiency: reads/writes/escapes/threads may not capture provenance subtleties (int-ptr casts: 'often no' per threat model), interior pointers, or the memory-object-model open questions (backlog item 14 blocks lifetime-fact emission design); a too-coarse vocabulary forces either unsound declarations or conservative collapse.

The thread component is capped by an undecided prior: concurrency-model is research_needed, C002 marks race semantics open, and C004 is provisional (not workflow-confirmed, per the synthesis correction); if concurrency resolves toward foreign threads/runtime-loaded components, round2-modules says the closed-world commitment itself must be reopened, changing what the wall even is.

Facts derived from trusted declarations must survive lowering to remain sound (misleading-comparisons #5: IR metadata is a conditional carrier); the Rust noalias enable/disable/miscompile incident history — the closest real-world evidence on declared-aliasing-fact fragility — is uncarded, and could cut against emitting assumption-grade metadata from wall declarations at all.
evidenceGaps: ffi_abi_runtime primary sources: missing_primary_source, priority-high — zero carded evidence on ABI, unwinding boundaries, GC statepoints, or C/C++/Rust/Fortran interop; the single wall now carries all boundary load with no cards under it (round2-modules follow-up, backlog items 6 and 49).

No cards on existing FFI contract-declaration systems: SPARK Global/Depends on imported subprograms, Rust extern-block unsafe attributes, C prototypes with restrict, JNI/ctypes trust models — the position's closest direct precedents are all uncarded.

No performance quantification anywhere (caveats.md standing limitation): the delta between conservative-wall calls and declared-relaxation calls — the entire optimizer payoff of the mechanism — is structural conjecture with no benchmark.

Callback direction undesigned: the env-struct borrow to noalias/alias.scope story is a blocking obligation from the aliasing-model amendment; re-entry frames (foreign code calling back into internal code mid-attenuation) have no carded semantics.

Dynamic checkability of declarations: no cards on whether Miri/UBSan/TSan-class instrumentation can falsify a wrong reads/writes/escapes/threads declaration in checked builds, or at what cost (round-1 gap, unresolved) — this decides whether wall contracts have any conformance-testing story.

AI-writer declaration accuracy: the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence; the rates at which an AI writer states correct foreign contracts from headers/docs, and whether gating prevents laundering, are the flagship-experiment class of unknowns (all behavioral claims here EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED).

Whole-execution properties (escape, thread-publication) have no carded runtime-check mechanism (A005 covers only bounded pointer-range checks in loops), so the position's residual cannot currently be downgraded from trusted to checked even in debug builds.

No card addresses fact boundaries and escape hatches directly (round1-ir-strategy gap: 'how each strategy represents where facts do NOT hold and suppresses emission there — historically the locus of real noalias miscompiles'); the attenuation algorithm itself is undesigned and uncarded.

result.position: Prove-or-reject with explicit checked ops as the only opt-in: for array bounds, integer overflow, and runtime alias disjointness, the compiler rejects any program whose safety-relevant fact it cannot statically discharge, unless the writer spells the residual as a visible, first-class checked operation (checked_get, checked-mode arithmetic node, disjointness-check/split op) whose failure semantics are the already-decided trap-with-report=abort. No compiler-inserted check exists anywhere in the language. SPARK direction, priced for a machine writer under D0.
result.coreArgument:
First, framing: prove-or-reject is NOT prove-or-die. The set of accepted programs is identical to an implicit-checking rival — only the spelling differs: every residual runtime check must be a named node in the canonical artifact. So the standard coverage objection ("you reject too much") collapses into a spelling question, and spelling explicitness is already decided law here.

(1) THE POLICY IS THE FIXED POINT OF DECIDED ROUND-2 LAW. The round-2 floors outlaw every other implicit, contract-bearing operation the compiler could add on the writer's behalf: zero implicit value-changing conversions ("every representation change is an explicit, named, mode-carrying operation"), no invisible drop, no implicit dispatch, one spelling per semantic construct, fully-elaborated stored artifact as the mandatory re-read surface (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md, SURFACE/NUMERICS/MEMORY floors). A compiler-inserted bounds check is a branch + trap edge + optimization barrier (N003: guards block speculation and pin code motion) that never appears in the artifact — an unspelled semantic construct that makes the mandatory re-read artifact lie about control flow and cost to the very AI that must re-read it. Proven-unchecked access and checked access are semantically different constructs; canonical form therefore requires different spellings; therefore checks are explicit and everything unchecked is proven. For overflow this is nearly a corollary of decided law: round-2 already requires each arithmetic node to name exactly one mode, so a "checked" node IS the visible opt-in; my position adds only that the assume-no-overflow mode is proof-gated — which round-1 numerics' own critique demanded ("unchecked-assume-no-overflow as an ordinary operator with no unsafe/proof gating is a standing soundness hazard") — and that no ambient default silently binds bare '+'. The genuinely contested residue is bounds and alias checks, where the same logic applies.

(2) IT MAXIMIZES THE PROGRAM FRACTION LIVING IN THE CORPUS'S ONLY WELL-ATTESTED TIER. Round-1 violation-semantics records the post-critique convergence of all three positions: compiler-proven facts are assumable in every build at zero runtime cost, and every carded win (F001 safe-Rust exclusivity, F005 Fortran, F006 SPARK) lives in that proven tier; it also records that the prove-gated position is "the only position under which the false-contract state is unrepresentable for covered code" and its fallback "is always sound." Prove-or-reject maximizes membership in that tier by construction: every unchecked access ships with a checker-derived fact, so the F003/N005 frontend obligation is discharged rather than promised — inbounds GEP (N005), nsw/nuw (N002), and range facts from range types (N004) all lower truthfully, exactly satisfying the round-2 verification-spine rule ("no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed") and misleading-comparisons #5 ("IR mechanisms are conditional carriers of source facts").

(3) THE RIVALS' PERFORMANCE MECHANISMS ARE UNCARDED PRECISELY WHERE MINE IS CARDED. Implicit-checks-everywhere rests on two mechanisms: check elision and post-check fact recovery. Round-1 numerics confirmed by direct card inspection that no card shows LLVM recovering nsw-grade facts from dominating checks or llvm.assume, and round-1 violation-semantics lists check overhead after elision as unmeasured. The assume/UB rival rests on an unquantified freedom delta plus unbounded blast radius (N001 lattice) and carries the refuted "forced by logic" claims. My payoff mechanism — statically proven facts lowered truthfully into per-operation contract channels — is the best-verified structure in the corpus (N001/N002/N004/N005 all 3-0). Where proof fails, my explicit opt-in inherits the one carded middle mechanism: A005 runtime pointer checks plus loop versioning, where a passed check legitimizes assume-grade facts on dominated paths; making the check explicit puts that versioning/cost decision in the artifact instead of in pass ordering.

(4) PRICED FOR A MACHINE WRITER (D0). Every cost that historically killed SPARK-style prove-or-reject for humans (F006 negative scope: usability costs; round-1 violation-semantics: "annotation burden, prover time, and rejection rates... historically adoption-limiting") sits in D0's explicitly admissible cost channel: tokens, restructure iterations, compile-loop latency. The round-2 AI-writer additions are exactly the machinery that makes rejection cheap for a machine: node-path machine-readable diagnostics, site-enumerated mechanical edit lists (a rejection reads "cannot prove i < n at node X: insert checked_get or strengthen bound to range<0,n>" — a to-do item, not a wall), redundancy channels where writer-stated facts are cross-checked against inference with mismatch as hard error, and content-addressed memoized checking. Rejection converts hallucinated bounds reasoning into a compile-time diagnostic — the only failure point with a mechanical repair path — instead of silent runtime cost (implicit checks) or silent miscompilation (assume). All claims about how AI writers respond to this pressure are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

(5) RESTRUCTURE PRESSURE IS CO-OPTIMIZATION, NOT OVERHEAD. Forcing range types and iterator/affine-provable loops pushes generated code into exactly the fragment round-1 arrays-loops named leading (structured/affine-checked core over a contract-bearing substrate) and that A003 attests as where dependence analysis is "efficient and reliable"; the same debate found every checked precedent in the corpus (Rust/F001, SPARK/F006, Futhark/A008) achieved checkability by restricting the model. Provability and optimizability are the same restriction — prove-or-reject makes the writer pay for it once, visibly, and the payment simultaneously feeds the vectorizer's documented legality inputs (A005). The contrast class is carded too: J001/J002 show that facts not known statically must be recovered with guards plus invalidation machinery; static proof is the only regime with neither guards nor deopt.

(6) PER-CLASS FIT. Bounds: range/size types plus provable loops (A008 direction); residual = explicit checked access, truthful N005 lowering on the proven side, decided trap=abort on the checked side. Overflow: named modes are already law; no-overflow is proof-gated; residual = explicit checked or wrapping nodes (N002 truthful lowering). Alias disjointness: the decided checked-ownership floor (F001, C004) already proves the bulk; the residual (slice splits, arena indices) becomes explicit split/disjointness ops returning checker-visible disjoint views, and the loop-shaped residual is A005 versioning made explicit. Observability for every opt-in check is the already-mandated machine-readable trap report with stack attribution — the corpus's only carded post-violation story (C002-adjacent).

citedEvidence.howItSupports: SPARK is the existence proof that a shipping language can reject unproven aliasing/ownership/side-effect facts via prover-enforced restrictions; its negative scope (usability costs) prices exactly the cost channel D0 declares admissible for a machine writer.

Safe Rust &mut exclusivity is the corpus's best-attested zero-runtime-cost win and the template for 'restructure until the fact is true by construction' — the proven tier my policy maximizes; also (with C004) it is the decided ownership floor that already discharges most alias-disjointness obligations.

noalias/alias.scope facts are UB-class contracts sound only if frontend obligations are true; prove-or-reject is the only policy that discharges those obligations for every emitted fact rather than promising them (with misleading-comparisons #5).

nsw/nuw make overflow poison — the strongest integer-optimization channel is intrinsically a contract, so it may be emitted only where no-overflow is proven; unflagged wrap and explicit checked modes are the truthful lowerings of the visible opt-ins.
inbounds GEP makes out-of-object results poison: a proven bounds fact has a truthful, high-value IR carrier; an unproven one is a miscompile channel — the exact asymmetry my policy enforces.
range metadata gives range types a truthful IR carrier for proven value ranges, so the 'restructure into range types' path yields optimizer fuel, not just proof bookkeeping.

Checks/guards block speculation and pin code motion — the real, per-site cost of every runtime check, which argues both for proving checks away and for making the remaining ones visible where the cost is incurred.
The definedness lattice shows a false assumed contract has unbounded blast radius, disqualifying unproven-assume as the opt-in and leaving explicit-check as the only sound residual.

Runtime pointer checks + loop versioning is the carded mechanism showing a passed check legitimizes assume-grade facts on dominated paths — the performance story the explicit checked opt-in inherits for residual alias disjointness.
Affine restriction makes dependence analysis efficient and reliable — evidence that restructure-to-provable pressure pushes code into the fragment that also optimizes best (co-optimization claim).
Futhark size-in-types and consumption rules are direction evidence for the range/size-type restructuring path (flagged: unverified direct extraction).
Type-level Send/Sync-class sharing facts (decided core kernel component) mean most alias disjointness is ownership-proven, shrinking the residual my checked opt-in must cover.
With J002: runtimes that lack static facts must recover them via guards plus deoptimization/invalidation machinery — the carded cost of every not-proven fact, which static proof alone avoids.

Records the three-way convergence that proven facts are assumable everywhere (the tier my policy maximizes), Position 2's 'false-contract state unrepresentable / fallback always sound' strength, A005 as the carded middle mechanism, and the unmeasured-check-elision gap that undermines the implicit-check rival.

Its critique demands proof-gating of assume-no-overflow; it confirms by card inspection that post-check fact recovery is uncarded (the implicit-check rival's load-bearing mechanism); its leading candidate — named truthfully-lowered modes with the default binding open — is what my per-node explicit policy completes.

Supplies the decided floors my policy is the fixed point of (zero implicit conversions, one-mode-per-node, no invisible drop, canonical single spelling, verification-spine fact-provenance rule, trap=abort) and the AI-writer diagnostic machinery that prices rejection for a machine.

Leading candidate is a structured/affine-checked core over a contract substrate, and its finding that every checked precedent restricted the model supports restructure-to-provable as the default posture; its failure-mode-taxonomy gap is the question this debate answers.

D0 makes tokens, restructure iterations, and prover latency admissible costs while excluding human ergonomics — neutralizing the historical SPARK-adoption objection; D1 delegates exactly this runtime-check policy to this debate.

concessions: Static discharge rates are unknown: SPARK overflow-proof discharge rates are a named missing card (round1-numeric-semantics follow-ups), and no card measures what fraction of realistic bounds/overflow/disjointness obligations a decidable checker discharges on performance kernels. If discharge rates on generated code are low, the policy degenerates to explicit-checks-almost-everywhere — semantically equivalent to the implicit-check rival, at extra token cost and with the differentiating performance win largely gone (a floor, not a cliff, but a real deflation).

Every claim about AI-writer behavior under rejection pressure — that writers restructure toward provable idioms rather than spam checked ops, that they act correctly on prover diagnostics, that iterations-to-green stay bounded — is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED; the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence (round-2 header rule).

General runtime alias disjointness beyond loop-bounded pointer ranges has no carded check mechanism (A005's scope limit, echoed in round1-violation-semantics): my residual story there leans on ownership coverage that is itself conditional on the aliasing round-2 landing substantially as described.

The decidable proof fragment is undesigned: flow-/range-sensitive reasoning strong enough to matter versus deterministic and explainable enough to honor the machine-diagnostic contract is an unresolved tension, and putting a full SMT prover in the trust path sits uneasily with the round-2 preference for closed, decidable checking. No card covers prover-in-loop determinism or latency.

For the explicit checked residual I inherit the same uncarded mechanics I criticize in the rival — post-check fact recovery, trap lowering cost, combined check+flag emission soundness — though prove-or-reject minimizes the surface area over which those gaps matter.

Round-1 violation-semantics named graduated obligations (Position 3, amended) leading, not pure prove-gating; my position is a hardening of that verdict for these three per-operation-checkable fact classes, justified by the later round-2 explicitness floors and trap=abort constraint — but it does go beyond the recorded round-1 leaning, and honesty requires saying so.
risks: Coverage-miss risk: the workload census is absent (round1-arrays-loops gap); if the provable/affine fragment misses the performance-critical center of real generated code, writers face unbounded restructure loops or blanket checked-op fallback, and the policy optimizes the wrong center.

Escape-hatch pressure: F006's negative scope and round-1 violation-semantics both record that proof burden is historically adoption-limiting; under performance pressure, demand for an unproven assume-unchecked op will recur, and granting it ad hoc reopens UB-class violation semantics. The policy must keep any assume op behind the proof/unsafe boundary per the round-1 numerics critique, and there is no empirical evidence marking discipline holds under pressure.

Checked-op metastasis: an AI writer can satisfy the compiler by wrapping every access in checked ops, silently converting a statically-proven language into a dynamically-checked one; containment needs a lint-class check-density/budget channel (round-2 lint machinery) and whether writers respond to non-error feedback is untested.

Prover instability under closed-world context: whole-program checking means distant edits can flip local provability, producing nonmonotonic repair loops and cache-defeating recheck costs; closed-world compile-loop pricing is already flagged as the biggest unpriced cost in round 2.

Trap=abort loop economics: every explicit checked op is a potential process-death site in test/fuzz runs; round-2 already flags multi-process harness wall-clock as a reopener, and this policy's residual checks add sites to that surface.

Visible-cost erosion: if the backend A005-versions loops around explicit checks or moves them, part of the 'the artifact tells the truth about cost' claim erodes; the policy needs an explicit stance on compiler motion of writer-spelled checks, which intersects the unresolved predictable-cost-model vs guaranteed-optimization-floor charter question.
evidenceGaps: SPARK/Ada/GNAT proof discharge rates and overflow/bounds-check overhead cards — explicitly named missing in round1-numeric-semantics follow-ups; the single most decision-capable artifact for pricing prove-or-reject coverage.

Post-check fact recovery: whether llvm.assume or dominating-branch reasoning after a passed explicit check recovers assumption-grade optimization — uncarded (confirmed by card inspection in round-1 numerics); decisive for the checked opt-in's residual cost and equally damaging to the implicit-check rival.
Check elision rates and checked-access overhead after modern optimization — unmeasured (round1-violation-semantics gap; caveats.md rule that documentation evidence never quantifies magnitude).

No card on any static bounds-proof system's coverage: range types, dependent-index typing, or iterator-provable loop discharge rates on performance kernels; A008 (Futhark size types) is the closest card and is unverified direct extraction.

AI-writer regime experiment missing: prove-or-reject vs implicit-check vs assume on the same kernels across edit/regeneration cycles — iterations-to-green, tokens, residual check density, restructure-convergence rate, and hallucinated-bound catch rate (extends the round-2 flagship drift experiment; all such behavior currently EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED).
Workload census: fraction of intended performance-critical generated code fitting the provable/affine fragment (round1-arrays-loops gap) — directly determines rejection-rate exposure.

Interaction of bounds checking and overflow checking (subsumption or duplication) and of trap/check semantics with N005 inbounds contracts — raised by the round-1 numerics critic, analyzed by no advocate.
Prover-in-loop latency and determinism at AI-scale program sizes under closed-world whole-program checking — the round-2 unpriced cost, aggravated here by arithmetic proof obligations.

Trap lowering cost and soundness of co-emitting checks with nsw/range/inbounds flags (dominance ordering) — uncarded per round1-violation-semantics; needed before the explicit checked op's lowering can be specified.

result.position: Always-check with aggressive elimination: every checkable-but-unproven bound, overflow, and runtime-alias-disjointness fact carries a mandatory runtime check lowering to compare+branch+trap(=abort) in ALL build modes; the compiler may remove a check only by proving it redundant (range types, dominance, closed-world range analysis, A005-style check+versioning); debug and release have byte-identical semantics. An unproven fact is never assumed; a proven fact is never checked; there is no third state.
result.coreArgument:
The decided floors of this project do not merely permit always-check — three of them structurally entail it, and the fourth (extreme performance) has zero carded evidence against it.

(1) For array bounds, the policy is forced by D1, not chosen. An out-of-bounds write IS memory unsafety — the bug class D1 makes non-representable in accepted programs. Checked ownership (F001, C004) discharges most disjointness statically but cannot prove all index facts; whatever residue remains must be checked at the access or the shipped binary can perform a wild write. Bounds have no conservative fallback the way noalias does: omitting the N005 inbounds fact does not make an OOB access safe, it only changes which wrong memory is touched, and the round-1 numeric verdict confirmed the freeze-hardening escape fails here too — a frozen out-of-range value flowing into index arithmetic 'still yields memory unsafety unless independently bounds-checked.' Rust, D1's explicit reference bar, ships release-mode bounds checks. So every rival policy already concedes always-check-in-all-modes at the memory boundary; the debate is really only about overflow and alias residue, where my policy is the uniform extension of semantics opponents must already accept, rather than a per-fact-class mode matrix with unevidenced seams.

(2) The round-2 verification spine is this position stated as law: the verifier 'rejects any optimizer fact not originating from a stated-and-checked or checker-derived source... there is no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed.' A release mode that strips the check but keeps the assumption (nsw/inbounds/noalias emission on an unproven fact) is precisely the forbidden third state — a trusted assertion. Rival policies must amend a decided floor; mine instantiates it. Likewise the round-2 constraint on this very debate: trap=abort is the mandated release-mode failure semantics, and graduated obligations must be designed 'without assuming unwinding' — my checks are compare+branch to a noreturn abort with a machine-readable report: pure branch-based dataflow, no unwind edges, no landingpad/funclet costs (F008 inapplicable), exactly the lowering shape the error-handling verdict blessed for Result and the shape A005 documents LLVM's own vectorizers already emitting.

(3) Identical debug/release semantics is the only policy coherent with the D0 writer. The AI's correctness instrument is the test/fuzz loop (round 2 mandates a multi-process harness engineered around trap=abort). A debug-checked/release-assumed split ships IR contracts that testing never executed — the round-1 numeric verdict names this exactly: the split 'ships exactly the IR contracts ordinary testing never executes and doubles the N006-style frontend verification burden' — and divergence-escape rates are 'completely unevidenced' (round-1 violation-semantics). Always-check gives one semantics, one lowering regime, one Alive2 validation surface (N006), and makes check elimination a pure refinement: an eliminated check is a proven-never-fires check, so no optimization level can ever change observable behavior. That is the strongest predictability property available to a machine writer that must reason about behavior from source alone. It also makes trap-with-report — the ONLY carded post-violation observability story in the corpus (C002 Go race-detection halts; round 2 requires machine-readable trap reports with stack attribution) — valid in production, whereas under release-assume a false fact is a silent poison miscompile with a discovery path the corpus explicitly lists as an unclosed gap, and an unbounded blast radius by construction of the N001 lattice.

(4) The forfeiture objection is refuted on the corpus's own record. Round-1 violation-semantics confirmed that A005 refutes the 'checking forfeits the payoff' structure: runtime pointer checks plus loop versioning are LLVM vectorizers' standard, documented legality strategy, and a passed guard legitimizes assume-grade facts on all dominated paths. The elimination half compounds under the decided floors: closed-world whole-program compilation (no interposition/D008, no separate-compilation fact loss/D004) gives range and dominance analysis maximal scope; mono-only generics make every instantiation's types concrete; the canonical fully-elaborated artifact plus writer-stated-and-cross-checked facts (round-2 redundancy channels) let range types feed checker-derived N004-class facts, which satisfy the fact-provenance rule because they are checked, not trusted. Ownership already proves the bulk of noalias for free, so mandatory runtime alias checks pay only on the loop-shaped residue that A005 covers. Guard-then-specialize is additionally the universally precedented industry mechanism for exactly this fact class (J001), and J002 records that guards are 'not purely a cost' — they enable optimizations static compilers cannot legally perform.

(5) Burden of proof. caveats.md's standing rule and N006 (LLVM applies maximal ceremony — negative tests, Alive2 proofs — precisely to poison-generating flag transforms) place the burden on relaxations. No card quantifies the assume-over-check benefit; no card quantifies the check cost after elision; both sides of the delta are unmeasured. Under a no-UB envelope with those priors, the default must be the policy whose worst case is a clean, attributed abort rather than the policy whose worst case is unbounded silent miscompilation — and the three-way benchmark already in the backlog is the instrument for carving proof-gated exceptions later, from evidence rather than fear.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: LLVM loop/SLP vectorizers use runtime pointer checks + multi-versioning as their standard legality strategy — the carded proof that mandatory checks are a payoff-RECOVERY mechanism, not forfeiture; a passed guard legitimizes assume-grade facts on dominated paths.

inbounds GEP violation is poison and licenses provenance-based miscompilation — shows what an unproven bounds fact assumed without a check costs; conversely non-inbounds omission does not make an OOB access safe, so bounds admit no conservative fallback, only check-or-prove.

nsw/nuw make overflow poison: assuming an unproven overflow fact is a UB-class contract by the substrate's own definition; my policy never emits these flags on unproven facts, only after a dominating check or proof.

The definedness lattice makes a false assumed fact's blast radius unbounded and silent by construction — the worst case my policy structurally excludes; freeze is not a rescue (arbitrary value still flows into indexing).
Honestly cited as my cost mechanism: guards pin speculation and code motion. But it also shows immediate-UB semantics block speculation too — check-free is not motion-barrier-free.

range metadata is the checker-derived fact channel range types feed after checks/proofs; round 1 confirmed it does NOT apply to post-check arithmetic results, which I carry as a concession, not a claim.
One semantics = one flag-emission regime = one Alive2 validation surface; LLVM itself applies maximal ceremony exactly to poison-generating flag transforms, supporting burden-on-relaxation.
Go's race-detection halts are the only carded post-violation observability precedent — trap-with-report in all modes generalizes it; also flags trap-under-races as an open dependency I concede.

Send/Sync + &mut exclusivity prove non-sharing statically — the proven tier that discharges most alias facts at zero cost, shrinking the residue my mandatory checks must pay for; also makes check-then-use TOCTOU-sound in accepted programs.
Safe-layer static exclusivity (Rust/Fortran/SPARK) is the best-attested win in the corpus and sits in my proven tier untouched — always-check applies only to the unproven residue.
The payload is non-interference around writes; an eliminated-or-passed check delivers the same payload on dominated paths without any unsound assumption.
SPARK's prover-discharged checks are the carded precedent for my elimination tier: proof removes the check, restriction enables the proof — the retreat path if check density is too high.
EH representation costs (funclet regalloc constraints) do NOT apply to my checks: compare+branch to noreturn abort has no unwind edges, per the trap=abort floor.
Guard-then-specialize is the convergent industry mechanism (CPython/V8/PyPy) for checkable-but-unprovable facts — cheap checks guarding specialized fast paths are precedented at scale.
Guards are 'not purely a cost': deopt-guarded speculation enables optimizations static compilers cannot legally do — carded support that passed checks generate optimization fuel.

Verdict confirms A005 refutes Position 2's forfeiture claim; names poison-silent release-binary observability as an unclosed gap; names debug/release divergence-escape rates 'completely unevidenced'; names trap-with-report the only diagnosability story.

P3 assessment: trap-in-all-modes is 'the only default with unconditionally defined behavior in every shipped build... one lowering regime and one verification surface'; also sources my concessions (N004 misuse, unquantified trap cost, freeze-into-indexing unsoundness, relaxed-trap-ordering hazard).

Decided law I instantiate: verification-spine 'no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed'; trap=abort with machine-readable stack-attributed reports; closed-world whole-program + mono-only generics maximizing elimination scope; redundancy channels feeding checker-derived range facts.

Trap=abort with descriptive (non-executing) CFI metadata is decided; branch-based failure handling is the blessed lowering shape; constrains this debate to design checked semantics under abort — which my policy does natively.

Names failure-mode taxonomy per fact family as the undebated decision variable my policy answers uniformly; also sources my concession that A005 checks are vectorizer-internal speculation, not yet a carded user-contract validation mechanism.

concessions: The cost mechanism is real and carded: N003 shows guards pin speculation and code motion; A005 versioning pays code size; per-element checks in vectorizable inner loops are my highest-cost site and vectorized checked-overflow lowering (llvm.*.with.overflow) has zero corpus pricing.

The aggressive-elimination story is only half carded: A005 covers runtime alias checks in loop-shaped code, but NO card shows LLVM recovering nsw-grade optimization from dominating checks or llvm.assume, and N004 was confirmed in round 1 to exclude post-check arithmetic — my 'passed check legitimizes the fact' claim for overflow is currently mechanism-plausible, not evidenced.

Rust — the D1 reference bar — does NOT always-check overflow in release (debug-panic/release-wrap); precedent for the overflow fact class specifically cuts against me, and all language-precedent cards (Rust field experience, Swift trap-always, Ada/GNAT overhead, SPARK discharge rates) are absent from the corpus.

General scoped noalias is a whole-execution property with no carded runtime-check form (A005 covers only bounded pointer-range checks in loops); there my policy degrades to conservative non-emission, forfeiting payoff wherever ownership cannot prove disjointness and the code is not loop-shaped.

Elimination-dependent performance is fragile: whether a check is removed is optimizer-dependent, in tension with the dispatch verdict's hostility to silent optimizer-dependence and the unresolved predictable-cost-model vs guaranteed-optimization-floor charter question — my semantic predictability is bought with performance unpredictability unless elision is made a reportable, guaranteed floor.

Any relaxed trap ordering (hoisting/coalescing checks to recover speed) reintroduces exactly the cross-optimization-level behavioral divergence my position exists to eliminate; I must either forbid it (paying the cost) or produce a sound specification that does not exist in any card.

Trap semantics under racing threads is unspecified (round-2 explicitly assigns this debt to this debate; C002's negative scope leaves race semantics open) — 'identical semantics in all modes' is only fully defined once that is settled.

All AI-writer behavioral claims on my side (AI writers assert false facts at material rates; AI writers act effectively on trap reports; identical-mode semantics measurably improves the repair loop) are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus has zero AI-codegen evidence.
risks: Peak-throughput regression in exactly the hot kernels the language exists for: if the measured always-check delta is large, D0 'extreme performance' pressure will force a proof-gated or unsafe assume tier, and round-1's flagged marking-metastasis dynamic could erode the policy into de-facto release-assume with extra steps.
Vectorization blocking: where elimination and A005-style versioning both fail, per-element bounds/overflow checks can forbid vector legality outright, producing cliff-shaped (not marginal) slowdowns.

Low check-elision rates without heavy range-type annotation would shift cost onto writer-side annotation volume and compile-time whole-program analysis — both admissible under D0 but entirely unpriced, and closed-world compile-loop latency is already round 2's biggest unpriced cost.

If the mandated multi-process trap=abort test harness fails its wall-clock measurement, the checked-tier question reopens at the toolchain level and could smuggle mode divergence back in through toolchain-internal containment builds.

TOCTOU soundness of check-then-use rests on D1 race-freedom; the undesigned FFI fact-attenuation wall is a named soundness precondition — foreign threads or attenuation holes that reintroduce races would silently invalidate passed checks.

A005's checks are vectorizer-internal speculation, not user-contract validation; transferring the mechanism to a frontend-mandated check discipline (dominance ordering, co-emission with nsw/range/inbounds flags) is an uncarded soundness surface where F003-negative-scope-style frontend bugs live.
evidenceGaps: The three-way benchmark (UB-asserted vs runtime-checked+versioned vs proof-gated on aliasing/bounds/overflow kernels: speed, code size, check-elision rates) — already in the backlog as the single most decision-capable missing artifact; my position's admissibility under 'extreme performance' turns on it.
Post-check fact recovery: whether LLVM regains nsw/inbounds-grade optimization from dominating checks or llvm.assume after a passed check — backlog item, decisive for my elimination tier on overflow.

The compiler-inferred-facts fourth channel (nsw/known-bits/ranges from types and guards, no frontend flags): uncarded in both directions; if inference recovers most of the payoff, the always-check vs release-assume delta shrinks and every rival is overpriced.

Achievable check-elision rates under the decided floors (range types + closed-world whole-program + mono-only generics): no card measures what fraction of bounds/overflow checks such a compiler eliminates — the load-bearing quantity behind 'aggressive elimination'.
Whether bounds-checked indexing subsumes or duplicates overflow checks in index arithmetic (round-1 numeric gap, analyzed by no advocate) — determines my real check density.

Sound specification of relaxed/coalescible trap ordering against LLVM's speculation and side-effect model, and trap semantics under racing threads — both required before 'identical semantics in all modes' is fully defined.

Language-precedent cards: Rust debug-panic/release-wrap field experience, Swift trap-always costs, Ada/GNAT overflow-check overhead, SPARK proof-discharge rates — the direct real-world tests of always-check economics, all absent.

Checked-tier lowering mechanics (trap intrinsic cost, co-emitting checks with nsw/range/inbounds flags, dominance ordering soundness) — carded nowhere; named as an open follow-up by round-1 violation-semantics.

AI-writer behavioral evidence: false-fact assertion rates in generated code, repair-loop effectiveness on machine-readable trap reports vs poison-silent failures, and multi-process harness wall-clock — all EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED per the round-2 standing flag.

result.position: Memory quarantine at the single FFI wall: foreign code never receives pointers into language-managaged memory. Every crossing is either a by-value copy or an opaque, unforgeable handle denoting an object in a foreign arena that lives outside the proved heap and carries zero optimizer facts. Internal proofs quantify only over language-managed memory (LangHeap), foreign-reachable memory is disjoint from LangHeap by type-level construction, so the fact-attenuation function is identically zero — its domain is empty. Pay the copies; where copies are unaffordable, allocate that data in the foreign arena as its primary home, by explicit writer choice under the existing per-allocation storage-contract floor.
result.coreArgument: (1) WHAT ATTENUATION IS. Every high-value internal fact in this corpus is a non-interference claim that quantifies over ALL access paths reaching a location: F001-class exclusivity, F002 ('the value of alias information is proving a location is not modified'), F003 noalias/alias.scope whose violation is UB. The closed-world commitment (round2-modules) bought exactly one thing: 'all access paths' became a checkable, finite set, because every carded boundary was a fact-loss pathology (D004 unit boundaries, D007 conditional/revertible recovery, D008 interposition blocking IPA even for statically-known code). An FFI call that receives a pointer into LangHeap adds access paths no checker can see — the threat model's FFI-callback row is categorical: effects/alias/lifetime, 'compiler can verify? NO'. Because retention is unverifiable, attenuation is monotone and permanent: after one escape, the only sound fallback is to void every fact about everything reachable from that pointer at every subsequent extern call, forever — D008 re-imported at every call site of the one language that cut modules to escape it. And under D1 this is not merely an optimization loss: a foreign thread retaining a LangHeap pointer makes data races and use-after-free REPRESENTABLE in the composed program, which the fixed constraint forbids outright. (2) THE QUARANTINE INVARIANT. Enforce at the type level that no language-managed pointer type appears in any extern signature or in any value transitively crossing the wall. Crossings are (a) by-value copies through declared buffers, or (b) opaque handles into a foreign arena allocated outside the proved heap. The arena carries no optimizer facts by the corpus's own emission rule (memory-object-model.md: never emit noalias/lifetime/invariant metadata without a matching verified contract — the arena has no contracts, so nothing is emitted, so nothing is falsifiable). Reach_foreign ∩ LangHeap = ∅ becomes a checked bipartition, not a trusted claim. Nothing attenuates because internal proofs never quantified over foreign-reachable memory in the first place. (3) THIS IS THE CORPUS'S OWN LOGIC AT ITS LIMIT. Round-1 violation-semantics' leading candidate mandates 'conservative no-contract fallback at unverifiable boundaries (FFI, int-ptr casts, per the threat model)'; quarantine is that fallback's limit case — instead of attenuating facts over a foreign-reachable set that is precisely what cannot be computed, it makes the set statically empty. It is also Position 2's strongest surviving move ('make the false-contract state unrepresentable; the fallback is always sound') applied at exactly the row where the threat model says verification fails. (4) WHY IT BEATS DECLARED ATTENUATION CONTRACTS. The rival design — per-extern annotations declaring which facts the callee may invalidate over which reachable set — is a trusted assertion by a fallible AI writer about code no checker ever sees. The round-2 verification spine forbids exactly this ('no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed'); contract-edit gating exists because writer-declared facts get laundered; and round 1's N001-lattice argument shows one false 'does-not-retain' annotation licenses unbounded, silent, non-local miscompilation. Quarantine converts the boundary obligation from an unverifiable semantic claim into a syntactic type check ('no lang pointer crosses'), machine-checkable with zero trust. Under D0 this is the right trade: marshalling verbosity and mechanical copy code are free for a machine writer; an unauditable trust surface is not, because there is no human auditor in the loop. (5) OPTIMIZER PAYOFF. With quarantine, an extern call's effect row is precise and small: reads/writes the foreign arena plus declared copy buffers only. Every F002-class non-interference fact about LangHeap remains true ACROSS FFI calls — code motion, register caching, and vectorization over call sites stay legal, instead of every extern call becoming a C003-style may-touch-everything motion barrier. Concurrency composes: C001 DRF-SC and C004 Send/Sync-class non-sharing facts quantify over language threads only; foreign threads can only touch the arena, so D1's race-freedom needs no foreign happens-before modeling, and round1-concurrency's 'residual FFI-survivor policing' objection is discharged — there are no survivors. Race semantics for arena memory shared with foreign threads (C002's open UB-vs-bounded question) is confined to a region about which the optimizer assumes nothing, mediated by checked accessor ops at read-back. (6) PAY THE COPIES — AND CHOOSE RESIDENCY WHERE YOU CAN'T. Hot interop data (device buffers, in-place BLAS operands, mmap regions) may live in the foreign arena as its primary home, accessed through explicit effect-typed accessors; the round-2 memory floor already requires a writer-stated storage contract on every allocation, so world-residency is one more contract field, not new machinery. This matches the shape of the highest-performance interop in actual existence — accelerator device memory with explicit transfers and opaque device handles (uncarded precedent; GPU/heterogeneous is an admitted corpus gap). The periphery also stays small: with no shared-fact calculus to attenuate, the remanded open-set-dispatch/FFI periphery (round2-dynamic-dispatch) needs no attenuation vocabulary — a direct answer to the round-2 synthesis tension that the periphery is 'accreting into a second, evidence-free language'.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Defines what attenuates: alias-fact value is proving non-interference around writes; these proofs quantify over all access paths, which is exactly what an escaped foreign pointer destroys and what quarantine preserves across every FFI call.

noalias/alias.scope facts are UB-if-false promises 'only sound if frontend obligations are true' — a foreign retainer silently falsifies them; quarantine keeps their quantification domain foreign-free so they stay true by construction.

The confirmed central enabler (exclusivity/non-interference rules) is precisely the fact class at stake; its negative scope flags programmer-truthfulness dependence, which declared-attenuation FFI contracts would reintroduce and quarantine eliminates.

States compiler verification of FFI effects/alias/lifetime is 'no' with remedy 'boundary contracts / conservative fallback'; quarantine is the limit case of conservative fallback — the foreign-reachable set made statically empty; the int-ptr row supplies the required companion ban on laundering LangHeap pointers through integers.
