Carded precedent that a boundary where unseen definitions may observe/replace what the compiler knows blocks IPA/inlining/propagation even for statically-known code — a leaky FFI wall is D008 at every extern call; quarantine keeps the closed world actually closed.
Every carded compilation/linkage boundary is a pathology or its expensive cure; these underwrote the modules cut whose entire payoff a fact-leaking FFI wall would forfeit.

Rules the FFI wall's fact-attenuation design 'a soundness precondition, not an optimization detail', notes the single wall 'now carries ALL boundary load', and demands the mechanism be designed before the closed world hardens — this position is a candidate discharge of that named precondition.

Leading candidate mandates 'conservative no-contract fallback at unverifiable boundaries (FFI, int-ptr casts)'; also supplies the N001-lattice blast-radius argument against trusted 'does-not-retain' annotations and Position 2's 'unrepresentable false-contract state has an always-sound fallback' logic that quarantine instantiates at the wall.

Send/Sync-class capabilities prove non-sharing over language threads only; quarantine ensures foreign threads never reach LangHeap, so D1 race-freedom composes without modeling foreign happens-before; its negative scope (incorrect manual impls are UB) warns against any trusted foreign-side equivalent.

DRF-SC quantifies over the proved world (C001); racy semantics for foreign-shared memory is an open UB-vs-bounded question (C002) that quarantine confines to a fact-free arena; C003 lists the optimizer freedoms lost when memory may be shared — losses quarantine avoids for all LangHeap across extern calls.
IR-level violation semantics (undef racing loads) exist but only govern code the compiler lowers; foreign code offers no such vocabulary, so containment must be spatial (quarantine), not semantic.

Its emission rule (no noalias/lifetime/invariant metadata without matching verified contract) makes the foreign arena fact-free by construction — nothing emitted, nothing falsifiable; its open question 'what can FFI invalidate?' gets the answer 'nothing in LangHeap'.

Names FFI fact-attenuation UNDESIGNED and a pending hole; verification spine ('no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed') and contract-edit gating rule out trusted attenuation annotations, leaving unrepresentability as the compliant design family.

Critique of message-passing position shows policing 'residual alias-free moves (raw pointers, FFI survivors)' requires borrow-checker-shaped machinery; quarantine dissolves the FFI-survivor half of that residue — no survivors exist.

Remands open-set dispatch to the FFI periphery and names fact-attenuation an undesigned precondition; under quarantine the periphery dyn construct operates over handles/copies and needs no attenuation calculus, keeping the periphery small.

The provisional foreign-unwind abort-at-boundary rule (flagged evidence-free) shows the wall already firewalls control-flow; quarantine is the matching firewall for data, and both await the same ffi_abi_runtime card.

Requires exact-card citation and explicit statement of what remains unproven; this position complies by flagging that no copy-cost, pinning-alternative, or ABI primary sources exist and that design_candidate status is blocked until ffi_abi_runtime is discharged.

concessions: Copy costs are completely unquantified (caveats.md: no performance magnitudes anywhere in the corpus) and could collide head-on with D0's extreme-performance charter at hot boundaries — zero-copy networking, mmap'd files, in-place vendor BLAS. The arena-residency mitigation is real but not free: arena-resident data forfeits ALL interior optimizer facts (it lives outside the proved heap), so quarantine does not eliminate the attenuation tradeoff for such data — it relocates it, prices it per-object, and makes it an explicit writer choice instead of an ambient global decay. That reframing is the honest core of the position, not a claim that the tradeoff vanishes.

'Nothing attenuates' is precisely true only for MEMORY facts. Callback reentrancy still attenuates effect/ordering facts: a foreign callee that re-enters language code at unexpected points constrains code motion across the extern call on the language side even though no pointer crossed. The threat-model FFI row covers effects, not just aliasing; quarantine narrows the boundary problem to the (checkable) language side of callbacks but does not close it.

Quarantine is an interface discipline, not a hardware boundary. In-process foreign code shares the address space and can scribble on LangHeap out-of-contract. The defensible claim is: D1 holds relative to ABI-well-behaved foreign code — the same envelope every Rust program linked against C already accepts. Any stronger claim requires hardware/OS enforcement (separate processes, MPK, CHERI), on which the corpus has zero cards.

Handle safety is not free: stale-handle/ABA protection needs generation-checked table lookups — a per-access runtime check, unpriced and uncarded. Handle leaks are possible; permitted under D1 (leaks are not on the banned list, and the round-2 memory verdict already downgraded 'cleanup always runs'), with lint-class reclamation diagnostics as the existing mitigation channel.

The type-level 'no lang pointer crosses' check is only sound if pointer-to-integer conversion for LangHeap pointers is banned or provenance-tracked (threat-model int-ptr row: 'often no'); otherwise quarantine is laundered through an integer. This couples the position to unresolved provenance questions in memory-object-model.md.

Historical erosion pressure is real, though uncarded: every mainstream runtime that started with copy/handle FFI grew pinning escape hatches under performance pressure (JNI critical regions, .NET pinned GCHandles, cgo pointer rules). 'Pay the copies' has historically been unstable; the position's survival depends on arena-residency genuinely covering the hot cases so pinning pressure never targets LangHeap.
risks: Charter collision: if a workload census shows dominant interop is large-buffer in-place (numerics on vendor kernels being the likely worst case for an optimizer-first language), quarantine either pays prohibitive copies or pushes the program's hottest data wholesale into the fact-free arena — at which point the language's confirmed payoff cards (F001/F002/F003) no longer apply to the data that matters and the design undercuts its own reason to exist.

Periphery accretion (round-2 synthesis open tension): the wall now hosts open-set dispatch, foreign unwind aborts, reflection grants, arena accessors, and handle tables. Quarantine keeps the SEMANTICS minimal but concentrates all interop machinery in one evidence-free zone; if that zone grows its own object model it becomes the second language the synthesis warns about.

Closed-world reopen trigger: round2-modules explicitly flags that a concurrency-model resolution requiring runtime-loaded components or foreign threads reopens the closed-world verdict. Quarantine's 'foreign threads only touch the arena' story must survive that redesign; accelerator runtimes calling back into language schedulers are the stress case, and GPU/heterogeneous is an admitted corpus gap.

Marshalling-layer drift: quarantine multiplies generated copy/serialize/accessor code; AI-writer drift rates in exactly this kind of repetitive boundary code are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED (corpus has zero AI-codegen evidence). The consistency layer's provenance-checked generation is the designed mitigation but is itself untested.

Deep-copy semantics for object graphs crossing the wall are undesigned (identity, cycles, partial graphs); hidden cost cliffs and subtle semantic divergence (snapshot-vs-live) live there — the same divergence class the closures verdict fights internally.

Verification-asymmetry hazard: because quarantine makes the boundary rule easy to state, there is a temptation to declare it design-decided before ffi_abi_runtime primary sources exist; the standing rule and the modules verdict both block that, and this position must not be allowed to harden by rhetorical simplicity alone.
evidenceGaps: ffi_abi_runtime backlog item is missing_primary_source, priority-high — the single named blocker: no ABI, unwinding-boundary, GC-statepoint, pinning, or C/C++/Rust/Fortran/Python-extension interop primary sources are carded. Per caveats.md's standing rule and the modules verdict, quarantine cannot pass design_candidate until this is discharged.

Zero quantified copy/marshalling costs anywhere in the corpus, and no census of how much real-world FFI in optimizer-first workloads is large-buffer/in-place vs thin-edge — the single most decision-capable missing measurement for this position.

No cards on real quarantine-adjacent precedents: JNI local/global references and GetPrimitiveArrayCritical, .NET GCHandle pinning, Go cgo pointer-passing rules, Erlang NIFs/ports, Wasm linear-memory sandboxing, CUDA/HIP device-handle interop. These are both the position's best precedents and its best counter-evidence (pinning escape hatches), and none are carded.
Handle-table mechanics uncarded: generation-check costs, arena accessor lowering, whether checked arena access pins optimization in surrounding language code (N003-adjacent question).
Callback reentrancy effect modeling uncarded: what effect row an extern call with callback capability must carry, and its optimization cost on the language side.
Provenance/int-ptr rules unanswered (memory-object-model.md open questions) — quarantine's type-level enforceability depends on them.
GPU/heterogeneous sync scopes, MMIO, and signal-handler-visible memory untouched by any position (round1-concurrency gap); the accelerator case is load-bearing for the arena-residency mitigation.
All AI-writer behavioral claims herein (marshalling verbosity is acceptable, trusted annotations would be hallucinated, drift in boundary code) are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED per the round-2 standing flag.

Race semantics for arena memory shared with foreign threads (C002's open UB-vs-bounded question) still needs a decided answer for the accessor ops themselves, even though quarantine confines its blast radius.

result.position: Toolchain-gated unsafe: an unsafe escape hatch EXISTS and is core language surface with fully defined semantics and per-fact obligations, but AUTHORING or EDITING unsafe regions is a privileged toolchain operation outside normal generation — a separate reviewed channel with quotas and content-addressed authorization artifacts, generalizing the already-decided round-2 contract-edit gating. The steady-state AI writer is structurally incapable of emitting UB-capable code: the verifier rejects any asserted (non-proven, non-checker-derived) fact that lacks a gate-authorization provenance record bound to the canonical bytes of its region.
result.coreArgument:
STEP 1 — A hatch must exist somewhere; 'no unsafe at all' is incoherent with D1's own bar. D1 names Rust-class enforcement (checked ownership + Send/Sync-class capabilities) as the reference, and the round-2 synthesis promotes C004-class capability predicates to a REQUIRED core kernel component. But C004's negative scope is explicit: 'Unsafe code and incorrect manual Send/Sync impls are UB; does not eliminate the need for an atomics model.' Send/Sync-class capabilities are only useful because base impls (mutexes, atomics, channels) ASSERT them — those assertions are UB-capable authorship by definition, and someone must write them. The corpus's own threat model lists 'unsafe boundary' and 'prohibit/unsafe/conservative fallback' as remedies for raw-pointer and int-ptr casts, and its FFI row is verify='no' with 'boundary contracts' as remedy — declaring FFI fact-attenuation (round-2: the single FFI wall, UNDESIGNED, a named soundness precondition) is itself an unproven assertion. A language with zero in-language hatch does not eliminate this trust; it relocates it to compiler intrinsics or foreign code behind the FFI wall, where the language can state NO obligations, downgrading threat-model rows from 'sometimes no' (in-language raw pointers, partially verifiable) to 'no' (FFI, unverifiable). Round-1 aliasing confirms the frontier is real though unmeasured: pointer-rich structures (graphs, intrusive lists, callback-heavy code) sit past the ownership discipline's edge (misleading-comparisons #2; frontier-sizing is a standing backlog item). Every sound road runs through a trusted base; the design question is only WHERE the residue lives and WHO may write it — exactly the round-1 aliasing 'trust-boundary placement, not existence' tradeoff.

STEP 2 — Rust-style freely-writable unsafe blocks fail D1's spirit for a steady-state AI writer. A free unsafe block makes program acceptance conditional on the writer's own assertion being true. The blast radius of a false assertion is maximal and silent: by the N001 refinement lattice a false optimizer fact licenses unbounded non-local miscompilation (the one Position-2 argument round-1 violation-semantics records as surviving critique intact), F003 violations are 'UB, silently, with no diagnostic,' and under the decided trap=abort floor there is no unwinding-based containment and no carded post-deployment observability for poison-silent binaries (round-1 violation-semantics evidence gaps). Meanwhile the corpus explicitly records ZERO evidence that unsafe-marking discipline holds under performance pressure or what defect density audited assertion regions carry (round-1 violation-semantics gap: 'unsafe/marked-region proliferation'), and the round-2 synthesis's rationale for redundancy channels — 'same-pass writer output is error-correlated with itself' — applies with full force: the writer that hallucinated the body will coherently hallucinate the unsafe justification. This is precisely the laundering failure round 2 already closed for contracts: 'editing a declared contract is a privileged, gated toolchain operation, so a writer cannot launder a hallucinated body through the checker by rewriting the declaration to match it.' An unsafe assertion is the MAXIMAL contract edit — it rewrites the safety declaration itself, asserting facts (noalias, Send, lifetime) the checker would otherwise have to prove. A fortiori: if signature edits are gated, unsafe authorship must be at least as gated. Round-1's cautionary contrast supports confinement generally (F005 Fortran's unconfined ambient contracts vs F001/F006's confined checked regimes); gating is confinement carried to its D0-consistent conclusion.

STEP 3 — Gating is the only form that reconciles the decided floors with the necessary hatch. The round-2 verification spine rules: the verifier rejects any optimizer fact not 'stated-and-checked or checker-derived — there is no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed.' A free unsafe block IS the forbidden third state. Toolchain gating restores two-state discipline at system level: a gate-authorized assertion becomes 'stated-and-checked' where the checker is the gated channel itself — review, per-fact obligation discharge, and a provenance artifact — and the in-language verifier mechanically rejects any assertion lacking the artifact. Crucially, the infrastructure is already specified, not new: the byte-canonical fully-elaborated artifact makes region identity syntactic, and consistency-layer mechanism 2 (provenance-checked generation: generator identity, canonical inputs, content hash, build-time re-run and diff) is the exact substrate authorization records ride on — one edited byte in a gated region invalidates its hash and fails the build. The closed-world/whole-program/no-interposition floor (D004/D007/D008 grounds) makes the gate enforceable in a way it never was for C or Rust: there is no separate compilation or link-time side door, and the only other entry — the FFI wall — is itself gated surface. And the language still owns the semantics, satisfying my position's defining clause: round-1 ir-strategy names the 'soundness-obligation ledger (per-fact truth conditions per F003/F004 negative scopes)' and the 'fact-boundary/escape-hatch representation... specifying where emission must be suppressed' as architecture-invariant artifacts needed under ANY outcome, and round-2 modules independently requires 'fact-boundary declaration constructs (unsafe regions, FFI extern regions, reflection capability grants) as core surface.' Gating changes WHO may write an unsafe region, never WHAT it means: inside the channel, the round-1 violation-semantics leading candidate (graduated obligations) applies unchanged — proven where provable, dynamically checked in debug where checkable (A005 runtime-alias-check + versioning is the carded middle mechanism; Miri/UBSan-class checking is an acknowledged gap), conservative fallback where neither works.

STEP 4 — The costs are admissible and the classic counterargument is not. Gate friction — review latency, quota bookkeeping, two-channel toolchain complexity — is machine-writer/toolchain cost, explicitly admissible under D0. The historical objection to heavyweight unsafe governance is a human-ergonomics/ecosystem-adoption argument (F006's negative scope on SPARK usability; round-1's 'escape-hatch pressure... historically adoption-limiting') and is therefore INADMISSIBLE under D0: in a single-writer, closed-world toolchain there is no ecosystem to revolt — frontier pressure surfaces as a logged, reviewable, quota-bounded gate request instead of a silently-emitted unsafe block. Quotas additionally convert the corpus's most decisive missing measurement — unsafe-density frontier sizing, a standing backlog item — from an unknown into a governed budget whose ledger produces exactly that data as an operational byproduct. The steady-state writer cannot emit UB-capable code at all, which is the strongest constructive reading of D1's 'impossible in accepted programs' available once Step 1 shows a trusted base is unavoidable: unsafety becomes not a thing the writer avoids, but a thing the writer cannot say.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Positive scope grounds the Send/Sync-class capability route D1 mandates; negative scope ('unsafe code and incorrect manual Send/Sync impls are UB') proves the reference bar itself rests on a trusted asserted base — so a hatch must exist somewhere, and the design question is its governance. (notes/phase2-concurrency-findings.jsonl)

Safe-layer-proves / unsafe-asserts is the best-attested split in the corpus (safe Rust &mut exclusivity; restrict-like contracts 'depend on programmer truthfulness'); gating keeps the attested split but moves the asserting party out of the steady-state writer. (notes/workflow-confirmed-findings.jsonl)

Violating noalias promises is UB 'silently, with no diagnostic' — establishes that a false gated assertion is undetectable post-hoc under trap=abort, so minimizing and auditing the assertion-generation channel is the only lever left. Its negative scope also seeds the per-fact truth-condition ledger the gated channel enforces.

The refinement-lattice card behind the surviving round-1 argument that a false optimizer fact has unbounded blast radius by construction — the quantitative-free but structural case for why UB-capable authorship must be the scarcest, most-audited operation in the system. (notes/phase2-numeric-findings.jsonl)
Fortran's ambient unconfined contract regime is round-1's cautionary contrast to confined checked regimes — evidence that WHERE the unchecked residue lives matters; gating is maximal confinement.

SPARK proves the restrict-the-normal-channel architecture ships (prover-enforced anti-aliasing/ownership atop a permissive base language); its negative scope (usability costs) is the classic anti-gating argument, which D0 rules inadmissible — human ergonomics are not a cost channel.

Runtime alias checks + loop versioning is the carded middle mechanism the gated channel can require as obligation-discharge where static proof fails — showing gated obligations are not all-or-nothing. (cited via round1-violation-semantics.md; notes/phase2-arrays-findings.jsonl)

Go's race-detection halts are the only carded post-violation observability precedent, and race semantics is an open prior — supporting both the trap-with-report shape of gated debug obligations and the concession that gated-region race behavior is undecided.

Leading candidate (graduated obligations: human-asserted contracts only in per-fact explicitly marked contexts) defines the semantics INSIDE the gated channel; evidence gaps §'unsafe/marked-region proliferation' (no evidence marking discipline holds under performance pressure, no defect-density data for audited regions) and §'violation observability post-deployment' are the direct case against freely-writable unsafe. (/Users/bytedance/Dev/xlang/optimizer-language-research/debates/round1-violation-semantics.md)

'Trust-boundary placement, not existence: all sound variants converge on checked discharge; what differs is where the unchecked residue lives' — frames the debate as governance of an unavoidable residue; also carries the unmeasured-frontier gap and misleading-comparisons #2 (ownership does not map everywhere), proving the frontier is non-empty. (/Users/bytedance/Dev/xlang/optimizer-language-research/debates/round1-aliasing-model.md)

Three load-bearing precedents: (1) contract-edit gating already decided as 'a privileged, gated toolchain operation' to prevent laundering — the exact architecture, extended a fortiori to unsafe; (2) verification spine's 'no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed' — free unsafe is that third state, gate-authorization restores two-state discipline; (3) consistency-layer mechanism 2 (provenance-checked generation over the byte-canonical artifact) is the already-specified substrate for tamper-evident authorization records. Also: FFI wall UNDESIGNED as named soundness precondition; periphery-accretion tension. (/Users/bytedance/Dev/xlang/optimizer-language-research/debates/round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md)

Requires 'fact-boundary declaration constructs (unsafe regions, FFI extern regions, reflection capability grants) as core-language surface' — the language defines the hatch's semantics even though modules were cut; and lists 'whether contract-edit gating actually prevents laundering' as an explicit experiment, which I inherit as my primary EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED flag. (/Users/bytedance/Dev/xlang/optimizer-language-research/debates/round2-modules.md, lines 32/38/42)

Names the fact-boundary/escape-hatch representation and the soundness-obligation ledger (per-fact truth conditions) as architecture-invariant artifacts needed under ANY outcome — i.e., the language must define obligations for the hatch regardless of who writes it, exactly my position's semantic clause. (/Users/bytedance/Dev/xlang/optimizer-language-research/debates/round1-ir-strategy.md, lines 50/62)

Remedy column lists 'unsafe boundary' (raw pointer casts, verify='sometimes no') and 'prohibit/unsafe/conservative fallback' (int-ptr casts, verify='often no'); FFI callbacks row is verify='no' — keeping the frontier in-language under a gate preserves partial verifiability that FFI relocation forfeits. (/Users/bytedance/Dev/xlang/optimizer-language-research/implementation/unsound-optimization-threat-model.md)

D1 delegates 'existence/form of any unsafe escape hatch' to this debate and fixes the Rust-class bar; D0 makes gate friction admissible (machine-writer cost) and the usability counterargument inadmissible (human ergonomics). (/Users/bytedance/Dev/xlang/optimizer-language-research/notes/user-directives.md)

Frontier-sizing item (unsafe/UnsafeCell/raw-pointer density in performance-critical Rust; SPARK discharge rates) is the decisive unknown for gate throughput; the numeric-semantics item demands a project-level ruling that any unchecked mode 'must be unsafe/proof-gated (it should be regardless)' — independent corpus support for gating the unchecked tier. (/Users/bytedance/Dev/xlang/optimizer-language-research/notes/missing-research-backlog.jsonl)

concessions: Trust relocation, not elimination: for gated regions, D1's 'impossible' is conditional on gate-discharged obligations — the same conditionality as Rust's own bar (C004 negative scope). I read D1 as 'the steady-state writer cannot produce UB-capable code and all residual trust is confined, audited, and obligation-bearing'; that is a reading, and the backlog's numeric-semantics item already demands an explicit project-owner ruling on what 'soundness may not be sacrificed' means for exactly this tier.

Every behavioral claim about the gate is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: whether gating prevents laundering is itself a listed untested experiment (round2-modules follow-up d); whether a quota-blocked AI writer routes around the gate by contorting safe code or spamming gate requests has zero evidence; corpus has zero AI-codegen evidence of any kind (round-2 synthesis header).

The gate is a toolchain invariant, not a type-system invariant: a modified toolchain accepts the same artifact without review. The verifier's rejection of authorization-less assertions makes bypass detectable in-band, but the root of trust is gate policy plus provenance infrastructure, not the checker's soundness proof — a strictly weaker artifact-level guarantee than 'unrepresentable', which the pure no-hatch position can claim for covered code (at the price shown in Step 1).

Reviewer identity is unresolved: an AI reviewer may share the writer's correlated errors (the same correlation argument I use against free unsafe cuts against same-model review); a human reviewer strains D0's 'all code AI-written' framing (review is not writing, but this is a boundary case needing an owner ruling). Review-channel independence has no card in either direction.

Quota policy is unevidenced and blunt: too tight pushes the frontier through the FFI wall (threat-model verify='no' — strictly worse), too loose recreates free unsafe with extra steps. No card supports any particular quota mechanism; the equilibrium is unknown.

If the frontier turns out large (unmeasured — the standing backlog item), the gated channel becomes the throughput bottleneck for exactly the performance-critical code the language exists for, and D0's 'extreme performance' pulls against the gate's latency; frontier sizing is therefore a blocking measurement for this position, not a nicety.

Rust aliasing-model caveats apply to the obligations themselves: the corpus's unsafe-Rust/UnsafeCell coverage is explicitly flagged incomplete (caveats.md), and Rust's aliasing model is unfinalized — so the per-fact obligation ledger the gate enforces cannot yet be fully written; the gate governs a semantics that is itself partially undesigned (memory-object-model prerequisites, FFI fact-attenuation).
risks: Obligation dilution under sustained gate pressure: the process analog of the unevidenced marking-metastasis risk (round-1 violation-semantics gap) — review standards erode precisely when performance pressure is highest, and unlike a type rule, a process standard degrades silently.

Periphery concentration: everything already remanded to the undesigned FFI periphery (open-set dispatch, foreign unwinds, dynamic loading, reflection grants — round-2 open tension) plus all unsafe authorship now lands in ONE evidence-free channel; the gated channel risks becoming 'a second, evidence-free language' holding all soundness load with the least corpus coverage (ffi_abi_runtime: missing_primary_source, priority-high).

Provenance/authorization infrastructure becomes the attack and defect surface: a hash-checking or keyring bug silently authorizes edits to UB-capable regions; the consistency layer's mechanism-2 substrate is itself uncarded and unprototyped.

Context-dependence of frozen assertions: whole-program regeneration continuously rewrites the safe code AROUND a gated region whose assertions (e.g., noalias, non-escape) may depend on caller context; the review validated the region against a program state that no longer exists — the closed-world analog of D004/D008 linkage-boundary contract falsification, and no card covers re-validation triggers.

Two-channel toolchain complexity may itself breed the near-duplicate-machinery drift the charter forbids (round-2's consistency-layer-proliferation tension): the gate needs its own diagnostics, ledger, quota state, and replay logic, all coupled to the canonical-artifact substrate.

If debug-mode dynamic checkers for asserted facts turn out infeasible or unaffordable (round-1 gap: no Miri/UBSan/TSan-class cards), the gated channel's obligations collapse to pure review — the weakest tier — and the position's 'defined obligations' clause loses most of its teeth.
evidenceGaps: Frontier size (decisive): unsafe/UnsafeCell/raw-pointer density in performance-critical Rust and SPARK ownership-discharge rates — determines gate throughput requirements and whether quotas are viable at all (missing-research-backlog item; round-1 aliasing follow-up).

Gating efficacy: whether contract-edit gating prevents laundering is an explicitly listed untested experiment (round2-modules); no experiment exists for the strictly harder unsafe-authorship case, and no card covers ANY historical gated-unsafe process precedent (e.g., cert-review or proof-carrying-code governance) — the corpus has zero process-governance evidence.

Defect density inside audited assertion regions and whether marking/audit discipline survives performance pressure — round-1 violation-semantics names this gap for humans; it is doubly open for AI writers and AI reviewers (correlated-error independence unmeasured).

Debug-mode dynamic checkability of asserted contracts (Miri/UBSan/TSan-class detection of false noalias/inbounds/nsw/Send assertions and its cost) — decides whether gated obligations are mechanically dischargeable or review-only (round-1 violation-semantics gap).

FFI fact-attenuation design (UNDESIGNED, named soundness precondition) — the gated channel's largest client has no primary-source cards (ffi_abi_runtime backlog, priority-high); until it exists, the gate's obligation ledger for the FFI wall cannot be written.

Race semantics for gated regions: C002 leaves UB-vs-bounded open; what a false Send/Sync-class assertion means under racing threads (trap meaning under races is a round-2 constraint on violation-semantics) is undecided and caps every claim about gated-region blast radius.

Quota mechanism design space: no evidence on budget granularity (per-fact, per-region, per-build), replenishment policy, or the behavioral response of a quota-constrained AI writer — all EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED and needing a dedicated experiment alongside the flagship drift experiment.

All AI-writer behavioral claims herein (writer cannot be trusted with free unsafe; writer will attempt laundering; gate requests remain rare) are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED per the round-2 standing flag — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence in either direction.

result.position: No unsafe escape hatch exists in the language. The accepted-program envelope is totally UB-free: every optimizer-facing fact in writer-emittable source is (a) compiler-proven (checked ownership, Send/Sync-class capabilities, types), (b) established by a runtime check with trap=abort on failure, or (c) not emitted (conservative fallback). The residue Rust covers with `unsafe` — core data structures, allocators, atomics internals, FFI glue — is relocated outside the language into human-maintained, safe-signatured, versioned trusted-toolchain primitives/intrinsics plus the single audited FFI wall. The AI writer can call these; it can never write, extend, or assert them. There is no source construct whose semantics is "believe me."
result.coreArgument: (1) D1's word is IMPOSSIBLE, and only no-hatch makes it a theorem rather than a statistic. The corpus's only adversarially-verified exemplars of static memory/thread safety are hatch-free subsets: F001's verified claim is about SAFE Rust exclusivity and its negative scope explicitly excludes unsafe/raw-pointer/UnsafeCell; C004's negative scope says "unsafe code and incorrect manual Send/Sync impls are UB." The verified guarantee stops exactly at the hatch, per the cards' own text. A program containing one wrong unsafe block is an ACCEPTED program containing use-after-free — so any in-language hatch converts D1 from a property of the language into a claim about writer discipline, which the corpus flags as unevidenced ("no empirical evidence on whether explicit-marking or unsafe-block discipline holds under performance pressure," round1-violation-semantics evidence gaps). (2) D0 deletes the only justification a hatch ever had. Rust's unsafe is a channel for a trusted human expert to discharge obligations the checker cannot. Here the writer is by charter the least-trusted component: round-2 decided law already treats writer assertions as radioactive — the verification spine states "law/effect annotations must be checked obligations, not trusted assertions — there is no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed," and contract-edit gating exists precisely so a writer cannot launder a hallucinated body past the checker. An unsafe block is the terminal laundering construct: it does not sneak past the check, it waives it. No-hatch is the only round-3 answer consistent with the round-2 floors as written. (3) Blast radius is carded: by the N001 lattice a false contract refines to anything (round-1 records this as "the debate's most solid semantic point"), and F003 makes violated noalias promises silent UB. Round-1 violation-semantics records Position 2's unique surviving strength as being "the only position under which the false-contract state is unrepresentable for covered code"; no-hatch extends unrepresentability to all writer-emittable code, and it inherits the all-position convergence round-1 already found: proven facts assumable everywhere, unproven assertion never ambient, unverifiable boundaries conservative. The contested middle tier — asserted-but-unproven — is exactly the tier D0 delegitimizes, because assertion presupposes a trustworthy asserter. (4) The performance story does not require a hatch. A005 cards runtime pointer checks + multi-versioning as LLVM vectorizers' STANDARD legality strategy, and round-1 confirms it refutes "checking forfeits the payoff"; trap=abort (decided law) gives checks a cheap failure semantics; J001/J002 show even a third sound channel (speculate/deopt) exists in principle. Meanwhile the round-2 core already amputated the main fact-destroyers before the hatch question arises: closed-world whole-program (no interposition/stale-summary miscompiles), mono-only generics discharging F003 obligations per-instance like concrete code, no hidden call targets, explicit storage contracts on every allocation. What residue remains — allocators, intrusive structures, lock-free internals — is small in kind, stable in form, and maximally reusable: precisely the profile of a toolchain-primitive problem, not a language-surface problem. The threat model's own remedy column (prohibit / conservative fallback / boundary contracts) never requires in-language unsafe on any row. (5) F006 SPARK is the carded precedent that a shipped language answers unprovability with restriction plus a declared boundary rather than an internal UB license — and its historically adoption-limiting proof burden is re-priced to near zero by D0, which makes verbosity free and machine-writer costs admissible. (6) Audit economics invert under AI authorship: humans audit a closed, slowly-changing primitive set once, at the toolchain where human trust is already spent (checker, codegen); a hatch instead demands audit of an unbounded stream of writer-emitted unsafe regions produced by a component whose same-pass output is error-correlated with itself (round-2's stated rationale for redundancy channels). No-hatch adds no new KIND of trust; a hatch adds the worst kind.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: The corpus's flagship verified safety claim quantifies over SAFE Rust only; its negative scope ('excludes unsafe/raw-pointer/UnsafeCell details... restrict-like contracts depend on programmer truthfulness') shows the verified guarantee dies exactly at the hatch — evidence that the hatch-free subset is where D1-grade enforcement actually holds.

Type-system route to data-race freedom, but negative scope states unsafe code and incorrect manual Send/Sync impls are UB — the hatch is the documented breach in the only carded static race-freedom mechanism; removing it closes the breach.

SPARK is the carded precedent for answering unprovable constructs with restriction (ownership on access types, banned side effects, anti-aliasing) enforced by a checking toolchain, with escape meaning leaving the verified language at a declared boundary — the no-hatch architecture, shipped.

Violating noalias promises is silent UB at the IR contract level and soundness is conditional on frontend obligations being TRUE — a writer-assertable hatch makes obligation truth rest on the least-trusted component; no-hatch makes every emitted F003 fact stated-and-checked or checker-derived.

The immediate-UB > poison > undef > concrete lattice makes a false contract refinable to anything — carded basis for the unbounded-blast-radius argument round-1 called the debate's most solid semantic point, and thus for making false-contract states unrepresentable rather than merely discouraged.

Runtime pointer checks + multi-versioning are LLVM vectorizers' standard legality strategy — the carded payoff-recovery mechanism that refutes 'no hatch means forfeiting optimization' for the loop-shaped cases where the payoff concentrates.

Speculation with guards/deopt is a second sound no-source-UB channel (even if rejected for AOT on J005 grounds) — evidence that sound alternatives to trusted assertion exist across the design space; unchecked UB assertion is never the only road to performance.

Languages split UB-races vs bounded-races, and the escape-hatch fragment is where UB-race semantics would live; with no hatch, the C002 residue question collapses to the FFI wall and toolchain primitives only, shrinking the unspecifiable surface round-1 concurrency flagged.

Conceded against my position honestly: Fortran's unconfined promise-based regime (POINTER/TARGET exceptions, truthfulness-dependent) is the cautionary contrast round-1 used — every studied static channel historically grew escape exceptions, which is the pressure my position must absorb via primitives instead.

Records the all-position convergence (proven facts assumable, unproven assertion never ambient, unverifiable boundaries conservative), Position 2's surviving strength (false-contract state unrepresentable, omission always sound), A005's refutation of the forfeiture claim, and the uncarded status of unsafe-discipline-under-pressure — the skeleton of the no-hatch case.

Decided floors do the heavy lifting: verification spine ('no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed'), contract-edit gating against laundering, closed-world compilation, mono-only generics, explicit storage contracts, trap=abort — a hatch is the one construct that contradicts all of them at once; also supplies my concessions (periphery accreting into a second evidence-free language, EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED flag).

Checked ownership leads because every sound road runs through checked discharge; names frontier size (unsafe/UnsafeCell/raw-pointer density) as the decisive unmeasured variable and trust-boundary PLACEMENT, not existence, as the real question — my position places it at the toolchain, the only locus with a standing human audit.

Every threat row (raw pointer casts, int-ptr casts, FFI callbacks, races, finalizers) lists a sound remedy — prohibit, conservative fallback, boundary contracts — that never requires in-language unsafe; the no-hatch position is choosing the always-available sound column on every row.

D1's 'impossible in programs accepted by the language as shipped' is satisfiable as a theorem only if no accepted program can contain an unchecked assertion; D0 makes the proof/verbosity burden admissible and human ergonomics irrelevant, deleting the classic anti-restriction argument (F006's usability negative scope).

concessions: Relocation, not elimination: the unsafety moves into the trusted computing base. D1 becomes 'impossible modulo toolchain correctness' — the same epistemic status as trusting the borrow checker and code generator, but the TCB grows by every primitive body. Critics will fairly call the 'total no-UB' claim partly definitional: every design draws this line somewhere (Rust trusts rustc + std internals); mine draws it at a closed human-audited set rather than an open writer-emitted set, but it is a line, not an elimination. Checker/TCB implementation-defect history is uncarded (round1-aliasing evidence gap).

Frontier size is the decisive unmeasured variable, by the corpus's own repeated statement (round1-aliasing evidence gaps; round2-memory-automation's explicit reopening clause if the statically-unprovable-lifetime frontier measures large; backlog frontier-sizing experiment). If unsafe/Arc/arena-index density in performance-critical code is high, the primitive set balloons toward exactly the 'second, evidence-free language' the round-2 synthesis already fears for the periphery.

The performance forfeit is unquantified in both directions. A005 covers only bounded pointer-range checks in loop-shaped code; general scoped-noalias disjointness and purity have NO carded runtime check (round-1: 'A005 covers only bounded pointer-range checks in loops'; purity has no dynamic-check evidence at all). Where proof and check both fail, no-hatch forfeits the contract via conservative fallback, and caveats.md confirms zero performance quantification exists anywhere — the delta versus a disciplined-hatch rival could be large on exactly the extreme-performance workloads D0 privileges.

Human-maintained primitives are a development-speed bottleneck that sits awkwardly with an AI-speed loop: a new lock-free structure or allocator not in the set cannot be written in-language at full performance until a human toolchain release. And 'maintained by humans, never emitted by the AI writer' is a policy fence, not a technical one — nothing technically prevents an organization from pointing an AI at the toolchain repository, silently degrading the guarantee to Rust's status quo without Rust's ecosystem review norms.

Every AI-writer behavioral claim in my case — hallucinated unsafe assertions at meaningful rates, unreliable proof-obligation discharge, error-correlated same-pass output — is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED per the round-2 standing flag (corpus has zero AI-codegen evidence). If future AI writers plus proof assistants discharge obligations more reliably than human auditors, a proof-carrying hatch (obligations emitted and machine-checked, not waived) would dominate my position; my case then reduces to 'no UNPROVEN-assertion hatch,' which is a weaker and different claim.

Round-2 text leans against me in one place: the modules verdict's follow-up and the synthesis core list 'unsafe regions' among fact-boundary declaration constructs needing a home in core surface. I read these as placeholders — D1 explicitly delegates 'existence/form of any unsafe escape hatch' to this round, so no-hatch is admissible — but I concede the drafted round-2 surface anticipated some in-language boundary construct, and my position must formally amend that follow-up to exactly two constructs: FFI extern regions and toolchain-primitive imports, neither writer-assertable.

My Rust-ecosystem analogy (unsafe concentrated in vetted core libraries behind safe APIs, e.g. Vec/Rc/Mutex, so the primitive layer is a proven pattern) is background knowledge, not carded — F001 supports only the safe/unsafe split itself. I flag it as uncarded per the standing rule.
risks: Pressure reroutes to the FFI wall — the worst possible relocation. If the checked/proven envelope rejects hot code and no primitive exists, the writer's remaining move is wrapping code as 'foreign,' escaping into the zero-fact, zero-check territory where the threat model says verification is 'no' and facts attenuate to nothing. A no-hatch language with a permissive, undesigned wall can be LESS safe end-to-end than a hatch language with strong discipline. Mitigation (FFI declarations gated like contract edits, human-approved) is essential and currently undesigned — the wall has zero primary-source cards (ffi_abi_runtime: missing_primary_source, priority-high).

Escape-hatch pressure is historically adoption-limiting (F006 negative scope; round-1 violation-semantics names 'escape-hatch pressure... unquantified and historically adoption-limiting'), and in this project adoption pressure IS performance pressure from the charter itself. If the primitive-request loop is slow, the failure mode is not abandonment but circumvention: toolchain forks, vendored intrinsics, and de facto hatches without the audit discipline my position depends on.

The check tier may underprice: checks pin code motion (N003 guard example), cost cycles and code size (A005 versioning), and trap=abort already carries the multi-process test-harness cost round-2 knowingly accepted. If the measured check overhead is high where proofs fail, the reopening pressure lands simultaneously on primitive-set expansion and FFI leakage — both erosion channels for the no-hatch guarantee.

Concurrency hole beneath everything: non-interference proofs quantify over race-free executions, race semantics for any residue is open (C002), and C004 — the load-bearing capability card — is provisional, not workflow-confirmed (round-2 correction). If the Send/Sync-class capability system has holes, 'total no-UB' fails one layer down regardless of hatch policy; my position inherits this risk equally but advertises a stronger headline, so the reputational blast radius of a soundness bug is larger.

Primitive-layer defects produce exactly D1's bug classes inside the TCB, and there is no card comparing defect density of centralized vetted primitive layers versus distributed audited unsafe blocks — my audit-economics argument is structural reasoning, not measured evidence. If centralized primitives turn out to concentrate rather than reduce defects (monoculture, stale human expertise), the core selling point weakens.
evidenceGaps: Frontier sizing — the single most decision-relevant missing measurement (carded as backlog and as round2-memory-automation's reopening condition): unsafe/UnsafeCell/Arc/arena-index density in performance-critical Rust and SPARK ownership/contract discharge rates. This decides whether the trusted-primitive set is a standard library or a second language.

Zero AI-codegen evidence (round-2 standing flag). The D0-decisive empirical — AI writers' hallucinated-assertion rate inside unsafe-like regions versus their proof-obligation discharge ability with prover assistance — is completely untested; it arbitrates no-hatch versus proof-carrying-hatch directly.

The three-way benchmark (UB-asserted vs runtime-checked+versioned vs proof-gated/conservative) named by round-1 as the most decision-capable missing artifact does not exist; it is the only thing that can price what no-hatch forfeits on aliasing/bounds/overflow kernels.

The FFI wall — where my position concentrates ALL residual risk — has no primary-source cards (ffi_abi_runtime missing, priority-high) and its fact-attenuation semantics are flagged UNDESIGNED in the round-2 core as a named soundness precondition. My position is not evaluable end-to-end until it is.

No exemplar card exists for the trusted-primitive/intrinsics architecture itself: caveats.md lists Zig, Go, Julia, and every intrinsics-heavy design as uncovered; F006 (SPARK) is the nearest precedent and is restriction-based, not primitive-based. The load-bearing architectural pattern of my position is currently precedent-free in the corpus.

No card on runtime-checkable forms of general (non-loop-bounded) disjointness or purity; no cards on the region/arena verifier family (Cyclone, MLKit, Koka, DPJ — round-2 blocking obligation); no card on checker/toolchain implementation-defect history to price the enlarged TCB; no card on defect density in vetted primitive layers versus distributed unsafe blocks.

result.position: Graduated check policy, instantiating the round-1 violation-semantics leading candidate (Position 3, amended): (T1) compiler/prover-PROVEN facts are assumed in every build mode at zero runtime cost; (T2) writer-marked `assume` facts are trusted trap-free in release ONLY where a static-proof attempt was made, failed/was inconclusive, and was logged as a machine-readable per-site record (fact id, site provenance, prover version, failure reason), and these sites remain dynamically checked in debug/fuzz builds; (T3) every UNMARKED unproven checkable fact — array bounds, integer overflow in checked modes, A005-style runtime alias-disjointness guards — keeps its check in release too, lowering to the round-2-decided trap-with-report=abort; (T0) conservative no-contract fallback at unverifiable boundaries (FFI, int-ptr casts) per the threat model. Prover success at a T2 site automatically promotes it to T1; the assume set is therefore by construction the machine-enumerated residue the prover actually failed on.
result.coreArgument:
First, the default is the strongest admissible one, and it is already half-decided law. T3 means unmarked unproven facts ship checked: the tested artifact equals the shipped artifact everywhere except an enumerable, logged list of marked sites. This kills the strongest standing critique of wholesale debug-check/release-assume regimes — 'you ship IR contracts testing never executed' (round1-numeric-semantics, P2 weaknesses) — by confining debug/release divergence to an auditable per-fact set instead of the whole program. Round-2 law already fixes the failure semantics (trap-with-report=abort, no unwinding) and the round-2 synthesis explicitly TASKS this debate to 'design graduated obligations' under abort — the rival policies are answering a question round 2 already reframed. Note also that the D1 reference bar itself, Rust, ships exactly this shape: release bounds checks stay on by default, and the only trap-free path is a marked get_unchecked-class site inside an audited unsafe region. My policy is that shape plus a stricter gate.

Second, D1 becoming fixed law repairs the proven tier's one round-1 hole. Every round-1 position shared the weakness that 'proven facts stay true at runtime' quantifies only over race-free executions (C002 open). With checked ownership + Send/Sync-class capabilities now mandatory (C004, D1), race freedom is statically guaranteed in accepted programs, so T1 — the corpus's best-attested wins (F001 safe-Rust exclusivity, F006 SPARK prover discharge) assumable at zero cost in all builds — is now sound without further rulings.

Third, the middle of the ladder is the only carded route to recovering assume-grade optimization where proof fails. A005 documents runtime pointer checks + loop versioning as LLVM vectorizers' STANDARD legality strategy: a passed guard legitimizes emitting the same noalias-grade facts on dominated paths. This is the carded refutation (recorded in the round-1 verdict) of proof-or-nothing's structural claim that 'checking degenerates into don't-emit-the-contract.' And every fact class at issue has a truthful graduated lowering with a sound fallback: overflow — unflagged add wraps (defined) vs nsw/nuw poison for the assume tier (N002); bounds/indexing — non-inbounds GEP fallback vs inbounds-poison assume channel (N005); proven value ranges — range metadata (N004); alias facts — noalias/alias.scope (F003) behind A005 guards or T2 marks. The graduation is native to the substrate: no new IR semantics at any tier.

Fourth, the proof-attempt-and-log gate is not ornament; it is demanded by the corpus's own verdicts and is uniquely affordable under this project's charter. The round-1 numeric verdict states that an unchecked assume-no-overflow mode 'with no unsafe/proof gating is a standing soundness hazard... it should be [gated] regardless.' The gate operationalizes two round-2 mandates: the verifier's fact-provenance rule (no optimizer fact without a stated-and-checked or checker-derived source — the log IS the provenance record for the third, explicitly-quarantined source) and contract-edit gating (a writer cannot launder an unproven fact into trap-free release without leaving a gated, machine-readable trace). It also yields the only post-deployment attribution story on the table: a suspect release binary has a finite, enumerated assume set; canary/debug builds re-arm exactly those checks; trap reports with stack attribution (round-2 addition, C002 Go-halt precedent) close the loop. The classic objection to proof-attempt regimes — annotation and ceremony burden, F006's negative scope — is priced in HUMAN ergonomics, which D0 zeroes out: prover invocations, logs, and per-site marks are machine-writer costs the charter declares admissible. D1's own text delegates precisely 'runtime-check policy for bounds/overflow... proof burden placed on AI writers' and 'existence/form of any unsafe escape hatch' to this round; T2-with-logged-attempt answers all three jointly, with the burden set at 'attempt required, success not required' — heavier than Rust's bare unsafe, lighter than SPARK's proof-or-reject.

Fifth, only the graduated policy degrades gracefully across the open envelope ruling and decomposes per fact class. If the safety-envelope ruling forbids any escape hatch for D1-critical classes, T2 simply empties for bounds/alias and the policy remains coherent (proven-else-checked); pure-assume fails closed as inadmissible under D1, and proof-or-nothing rejects exactly the dynamic-aliasing/FFI rows where the threat model says verification is 'sometimes no / often no / no.' Per-class: overflow is per-operation checkable (N002); bounds is per-operation checkable (N005); alias disjointness is checkable only in loop-bounded pointer-range form (A005), so its ladder is proven / A005-guarded / T2-logged-assume / conservative no-emit. The round-1 verdict's tradeoff analysis says one uniform policy across these classes 'may be a category error only Position 3 can even express' — this policy expresses it. The speculate-and-deoptimize fourth alternative (J001/J002) is enumerated and rejected for this AOT, closed-world design on J005 warmup/memory grounds and round-2's no-JIT-shaped-runtime floors, not ignored. And the honest reason a trap-free tier must exist at all is carded: checks pin speculation and code motion (N003) — that cost is real in mechanism even though its magnitude is unmeasured, which is exactly why removal must be earned (proof) or quarantined (marked+logged), never ambient.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Names graduated obligations (Position 3, amended) the leading candidate — 'compiler-proven contracts assumable in every build mode; human-asserted contracts only in per-fact explicitly marked contexts, dynamically checkable in debug builds; A005-style runtime-check-plus-code-versioning as the carded middle mechanism; conservative no-contract fallback at unverifiable boundaries.' My position is its direct instantiation. Also records the refutations of both rivals: P1's UB-only trichotomy refuted by C005/N001-freeze, P2's 'checking forfeits the payoff' refuted by A005.

Decided law constrains and directs this exact design: 'round 2 must take trap=abort as release-mode failure semantics... and design graduated obligations without assuming unwinding.' Also mandates the infrastructure my T2 log rides on: machine-readable trap reports with stack attribution, contract-edit gating, provenance/generator infrastructure, canonical elaborated artifact — so the marginal toolchain cost of attempt-logging is low. Honestly cited against me too: the verification-spine 'no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed' line requires a formal carve-out for T2.

Round-2 floors this policy composes with (trap=abort, checked ownership, mono-only generics, closed-world) are recorded as decided law; also records that violation-semantics is research_needed with a leading candidate — supporting 'leading candidate, instantiated' framing while capping how much I may claim.

D1 explicitly delegates 'runtime-check policy for bounds/overflow', 'existence/form of any unsafe escape hatch', and 'proof burden placed on AI writers' to this round — T2 is the proposed escape-hatch form at the Rust-class bar D1 itself names. D0 makes the gate's ceremony (prover runs, logs, per-site marks) admissible machine-writer cost, voiding the F006-negative-scope usability objection.

Truthful graduated lowering for overflow: unflagged add is defined modulo arithmetic (sound fallback/checked-mode substrate), nsw/nuw make overflow poison (the T2 assume channel). Verified 3-0: overflow facts are optimization contracts, not annotations — so the policy question is precisely who may emit them and when.

Same graduated pair for bounds/indexing: non-inbounds GEP is the sound fallback; inbounds makes out-of-object results poison — the T2 channel for assumed bounds facts, with provenance-like no-cross-object dereference rules optimizers rely on.

The definedness lattice shows a false assumed fact has unbounded, silent blast radius by construction — the reason T2 must be per-fact, marked, logged, and debug-checked rather than ambient; freeze is a carded containment point for value facts (but, per the numeric-round critique, NOT a safety fallback once values flow into indexing).

The honest cost of the checked tiers: guards block speculation/hoisting (udiv cannot be hoisted above its zero-check). This is why a trap-free tier must exist at all — and why its entry must be earned via proof or gated assume.

Range metadata is the proven-fact channel for integer loads/calls; the numeric round CONFIRMED its scope excludes post-check arithmetic — an honest limit I inherit on claims that a passed check feeds optimizer facts beyond A005's pattern.

The carded existence proof of the checked middle tier: LLVM loop/SLP vectorizers use runtime pointer checks + multiversioning as standard legality strategy, recovering assume-grade inner loops from unproven alias facts — checking does not forfeit the payoff. Scope honestly noted: bounded pointer-range checks in loops only.

Send/Sync + &mut exclusivity make non-sharing a type-level fact — now fixed law via D1 — closing round-1's universal hole that proofs quantify only over race-free executions; T1 'proven facts assumable everywhere' is sound under D1. (Flagged: C004 is provisional per round-2 correction, not workflow-confirmed 3-0.)

Go's race-detection halts are the only carded precedent for trap-with-report as violation observability — the mechanism T3 and debug-mode T2 checks ship. Also documents the UB-vs-bounded race split that D1 now resolves for accepted programs.

LLVM gives racing non-atomic loads undef, not instant UB — carded proof that defined violation semantics without runtime checks exist, refuting the pure-assume rival's 'UB is forced by logic' claim and supporting a graduated envelope.

Safe Rust &mut exclusivity as the best-attested zero-runtime-cost proven-tier win; Rust is also D1's named reference bar, and its shipped policy (release bounds checks on, marked unchecked escape) is structurally my T3+T2.

SPARK's prover-gated regime is the T1 existence proof for prover-discharged obligations (anti-aliasing, side-effect-freedom); its negative scope (usability cost, historically adoption-limiting) is the classic objection to proof gates — repriced to admissible by D0.

noalias/alias.scope metadata is what T2 emits for alias facts, with UB on violation — and its negative scope ('sound only if frontend obligations are true and metadata survives lowering', misleading-comparisons #5) is exactly why unproven emission must be gated and logged, never ambient.

InstCombine's flag-sensitive folds carry Alive2-proof and negative-test discipline — the validation standard every graduated lowering path (check+flag co-emission, assume channels) must meet; sizes the frontend audit burden my mode matrix creates.

Verifiability rows 'sometimes no / often no / no' (raw pointer casts, int-ptr casts, FFI callbacks) ground the T0 conservative no-contract fallback and show proof-or-nothing rejects exactly the code where the payoff concentrates.

Verdict-file support for the gate itself: unchecked assume-no-overflow 'with no unsafe/proof gating is a standing soundness hazard... it should be [gated] regardless'; the leading truthful-lowering mode architecture is what my tiers instantiate; also records the unpriced costs (nsw benefit, trap overhead, freeze cost) I concede.

Speculate-and-deoptimize is the enumerated fourth policy (sound, no source UB) — acknowledged and rejected for this AOT closed-world design on J005 warmup/code-cache-memory grounds and round-2 floors, so the graduated enumeration is complete rather than conveniently narrow.

result.position: Rust-division of proof burden: the AI writer states INTENT — ownership/aliasing capability, effect row, numeric mode, and ABI-visible layout at every signature, interface, and instantiation, plus explicit storage/lifetime contracts at allocations (all already round-2 decided floors). The COMPILER carries the entire derivation burden: interiors are discharged by deterministic, borrow-checking-class analysis, and every optimizer-visible fact is either stated-and-checked or checker-derived. Writer-stated facts beyond the intent floor are exactly the round-2 optional checked-redundancy channel (mismatch = hard error), never trusted, and never load-bearing for soundness. The writer never writes a proof; it writes claims the compiler proves or rejects.
result.coreArgument: The question "how much proof burden can the writer carry?" dissolves once the round-2 verification spine is taken seriously: there is NO THIRD STATE between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed (round2-synthesis, VERIFICATION SPINE; grounded in F003's negative scope — IR contracts are sound only if frontend obligations are true, so unchecked writer promises are a miscompilation channel, and N001's refinement lattice gives a single false fact unbounded, silent, non-local blast radius). Whatever the writer 'proves', the compiler must independently re-derive before the optimizer may consume it — under D1 there is no admissible design in which a writer-supplied derivation is trusted. Therefore mandatory writer proof burden can NEVER buy trust; it can only buy redundancy. The debate is thus not 'how much proof can we extract from the writer' but 'how much redundancy is worth its error surface' — and round 2 already answered that: mandatory where redundancy is independent, optional where it is correlated. (1) EVIDENCE ASYMMETRY. The corpus's only adversarially-verified exemplars of D1-grade static enforcement are exactly this division: F001 (3-0 verified) — safe Rust's &mut exclusivity makes overlapping compute(&x, &mut x) unrepresentable while the writer states only &-vs-&mut intent at signatures; C004 (provisional) — Send/Sync are compiler-DERIVED auto traits, with manual assertion explicitly the UB channel in its negative scope. The round-1 aliasing verdict names checked ownership the leading candidate on a convergence argument that is decisive here: P2's checked boundary-contract regime imports ownership machinery at every heap-pointer case (F006), and P3's region-verifier family has zero exemplar cards and concedes ownership fallback at its hard frontier — every sound road runs through compiler-side ownership discharge. The higher-writer-burden alternative that DOES have a card, SPARK (F006), carries a negative scope warning that its restrictions 'may not map to a general-purpose performance language without usability costs', and round-1 violation-semantics records proof burden, rejection rates, and escape-hatch pressure as 'unquantified and historically adoption-limiting'. D1 itself names Rust-class enforcement — this division — as the bar. (2) EPISTEMICS OF A CORRELATED WRITER. All code is same-source (D0). The round-2 type-inference trial's sharpest mechanism point is that same-pass writer annotations are error-correlated with the code they annotate via autoregressive conditioning — an AI that hallucinated the body can hallucinate a coherent wrong 'proof' of it, and correlated wrongness typechecks (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED, both directions; the redundancy-independence experiment is the gate). Compiler derivation is the only fact source independent of the writer's error process BY CONSTRUCTION. The one place writer statements do have independence is boundaries: signatures are re-verified against bodies on every build as 'the redundant second statement of intent against coherently-hallucinated caller/callee pairs' (round2-synthesis, modules replacement) — caller and callee are generated in different contexts, so signature-vs-body checking catches cross-context hallucination. That is precisely why the division puts MANDATORY intent at boundaries and only OPTIONAL redundancy inside: it maximizes independence-per-stated-fact. (3) LAUNDERING SURFACE. Round 2's contract-edit gating (a writer cannot rewrite a declaration to match a hallucinated body) is tractable exactly because contracts live on a small, privileged surface — signatures, storage contracts, law bundles. Every additional mandatory interior obligation is another site where the writer can adjust the claim to fit the bug; interior-heavy proof regimes multiply the laundering channel that gating exists to close. (4) MACHINE-LOOP DETERMINISM. Round 2's type-inference floor requires interior synthesis be deterministic, non-backtracking, defaulting-free, ambiguity-a-hard-error — which is what disarms D009's silent-degradation failure mode. Borrow-checking-class analysis has this shape: stable accept/reject, localized machine-readable blame, compatible with content-addressed memoized checking (an explicitly priced AI-cost win). Prover-discharged writer obligations (the F006 direction) inject solver heuristics and timeout nondeterminism into the accept/reject decision — the same canonical input can flake — which poisons both the memoization substrate and the unattended repair loop that round 2's machine-diagnostics contract is designed around. (5) 'VERBOSITY IS FREE' DOES NOT RESCUE WRITER PROOFS. D0 makes tokens cheap, but the round-2 type-inference verdict shows the costs of dense mandatory writer-stated facts are not token costs: edit cascades scaling with program size, structurally-sound O(k^2) blowup in generic pipelines, unnameable types needing new naming machinery, annotation-vocabulary churn as systemic rewrite risk, and — critically — full annotation MAXIMIZES the count of wrong-but-well-typed silent semantic decision sites (let x: u8 = 255). More mandatory writer claims = more error surface, zero added trust (by point 1). The verdict refused to rule this trade without evidence; the division is the unique configuration consistent with that refusal: it keeps the decided floors, leaves interiors to the experiment, and loses nothing if the experiment later mandates interior annotations — they would simply move from the optional to the mandatory redundancy channel, with the derivation burden still in the compiler. (6) FRONTIER POLICY WITHOUT WRITER PROOFS. Where deterministic analysis cannot discharge (dynamic aliasing, loop-bounded disjointness), the carded recovery mechanism is A005 runtime checks + versioning — a compiler/toolchain mechanism, consistent with round-1 violation-semantics' amended leading candidate — not writer-authored proofs; and F009's architecture (exploit ownership facts in a high-level IR before conservative lowering) is the carded pipeline for consuming exactly the two fact classes this division produces. The division is therefore not one position among equals: it is the fixed point of the round-2 floors, the only regime with verified existence proofs at the D1 bar, and the only one whose failure mode under a fallible writer is a deterministic compile error rather than a trusted false fact.

Its negative scope (restrict-like contracts 'depend on programmer truthfulness') is also the direct indictment of trusted writer assertions.

Send/Sync auto traits are compiler-DERIVED capability facts — the carded pattern of checker-derived trust, with manual assertion as the UB channel; supports 'derived-fact production belongs in the checker under a least-trusted-writer charter'. FLAGGED: provisional direct extraction, not workflow-confirmed (round-2 synthesis correction).

SPARK is the carded higher-writer-burden alternative; its negative scope warns of usability costs mapping to a general-purpose performance language, and round-1 violation-semantics records its proof burden/rejection rates as unquantified and historically adoption-limiting. Also shows checked boundary contracts import ownership machinery at every heap-pointer case (round-1 aliasing P2 weakness) — the convergence argument for compiler-side ownership discharge.

(Via round-1 violation-semantics, both positions' surviving semantic point) The refinement lattice gives a false optimizer fact unbounded, silent, non-local blast radius by construction — why nothing writer-trusted may ever enter the optimizer, hence writer 'proofs' cannot be load-bearing.

Race semantics is an open prior and non-interference proofs quantify over race-free executions — making C004-style type-level race freedom (compiler-derived, this division's mechanism) load-bearing for optimizer soundness, which D1 has since fixed as law.

Verification spine (facts must be stated-and-checked or checker-derived; no third state), optional writer-redundancy channel cross-checked against inference, contract-edit gating, and signature re-verification as the independent second statement against coherently-hallucinated caller/callee pairs — the position is the fixed point of these floors.

Post-critique convergence: proven facts assumable everywhere, unproven assertion never ambient, conservative fallback at unverifiable boundaries — the division realizes the convergence point while its rivals' distinctive tiers (trusted assertion, universal prover obligations) are the refuted or uncarded residue; also records F006-style proof burden as historically adoption-limiting.

concessions: Every behavioral claim about AI writers in this case — correlated-annotation wrongness, laundering behavior, repair-loop performance on prover vs type-checker diagnostics — is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED; the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence cards. The redundancy-independence experiment could show same-pass interior annotations catch real errors at rates justifying their costs, which would flip the interior channel from optional to mandatory (the prosecutor's regime minus layout). The division's boundary/derivation split survives that outcome, but its 'optional' half does not.

The 'signature' framing is idealized: round-2 floors already require writer-stated storage/lifetime contracts at every allocation and explicit instantiation arguments — more than literal signatures. The honest statement of the division is intent-vs-derivation, not signature-vs-interior; the writer's intent surface is nontrivial and will likely grow (lifetime/region parameters, env-struct capture modes as field types).

The borrow-checkable frontier is unmeasured (round-1 aliasing evidence gap: unsafe/UnsafeCell/raw-pointer/callback density is 'the decisive variable' and no card touches it). Past that frontier the position has no carded answer except conservative rejection or A005-style loop-bounded runtime checks; graphs, intrusive lists, and callback-heavy code may force either escape hatches (round-3 open per D1) or exactly the richer writer-stated contract regime this position resists.

Concentrating all derivation in the compiler makes the checker the single trusted computing base, and the corpus has no cards on borrow-checker implementation defect history or compile-time cost; Rust's aliasing model is itself unfinalized (caveats.md: Nomicon is explanatory, not a formal model). 'Deterministic' is a design property claim, not yet a verified-implementation claim.

Load-bearing cards are provisional: C004 is a direct extraction wrongly argued as 3-0 confirmed (round-2 synthesis correction); D009, A005, D001 and the rest of the A/D/C/J series carry no adversarial verification votes. Round-2 synthesis flags checked ownership itself as a single point of failure for five verdicts simultaneously, with the aliasing round-2 re-litigation not yet run.

Effect rows are a mandatory intent floor, but the corpus has ZERO exemplar cards for region/effect verifiers (Cyclone/MLKit/Koka/DPJ — a named blocking obligation); whether compiler-side effect checking achieves borrow-checker-class determinism and coverage is unevidenced, so one leg of the 'compiler can carry the derivation' claim is currently promise, not proof.

Conservative rejection has its own loop cost the position cannot price: when deterministic analysis fails on correct code, the AI writer must restructure the program rather than discharge an obligation — plausibly a longer repair loop than answering a prover. No card compares restructure-loops vs proof-loops for any writer, human or machine.
risks: Compiler-soundness concentration: a borrow-checker or effect-checker defect is a silent D1 violation across every accepted program, with no writer-side redundancy required that could catch it; the position deliberately removes the second derivation the prover-obligation regime would force.

If AI writers empirically iterate well against prover feedback (SPARK-style obligations), the adoption-limiting-proof-burden argument — which is a human-ergonomics observation being extrapolated to machines — collapses, and a higher-writer-burden regime could dominate at the expressiveness frontier where borrow-checking rejects correct code. F006's usability negative scope is about humans; D0 rules human ergonomics inadmissible, so the position's strongest historical evidence is partially off-charter and must be re-established for machine writers.

The optional redundancy channel may be dead weight: an unattended writer optimizing for iterations-to-green has no incentive to emit optional cross-checked facts, so the hallucination-checksum value may never materialize; conversely, if policy effectively mandates it, the regime becomes mandatory-annotation without having run the gating experiment.

Escape-hatch metastasis at the unmeasured frontier: round-1 violation-semantics records zero empirical evidence that marking discipline holds under performance pressure; if the borrow-checkable subset is too small for extreme-performance code, unsafe-region proliferation relocates proof burden to audit processes nobody has designed.

Interior elision plus the mandatory compiler-elaborated re-read artifact creates the codec/second-spelling tension round-2 flags: the elaborator becomes a write-path codec that must meet bijectivity/fixpoint rules, and no verdict says which artifact is the source of record during a repair loop.

The division's determinism claim degrades if interior analysis needs to grow toward flow-sensitive or prover-assisted precision to keep frontier rejection rates acceptable — precision and deterministic ambiguity-rejection pull in opposite directions, and the position has pre-committed to the latter.
