result.position: Maximal proof burden on the AI writer: proof-carrying source. The writer emits SPARK-grade checked annotations everywhere — loop invariants, value ranges, ownership/lifetime transfers, effect rows — directly in the fully-elaborated canonical form, and the compiler is a checker only: it never infers optimizer-consumable facts, it verifies writer-stated ones and rejects the program otherwise. False facts are compile errors, never UB, so the D1 envelope is preserved by construction; the debate-relevant claim is that this regime maximizes the volume of top-tier (proven, assumable-in-every-build-mode) optimizer facts while pricing all writer fallibility in admissible machine currency.
result.coreArgument: Four load-bearing arguments, all grounded in decided law and carded evidence. (1) THE NO-THIRD-STATE RULE MAKES PROOF BURDEN THE ONLY ROUTE TO THE HIGHEST-VALUE FACTS. The round-2 verification spine (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md, VERIFICATION SPINE) is already law: every optimizer fact must be stated-and-checked or checker-derived — "there is no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed" (round2-type-inference.md, Verification role (a), from F001/F003). Checker derivation is capped by decidability, and the facts the backend actually consumes for the biggest wins — dependence-freedom, disjointness, trip counts, ranges, reduction structure (A005; A003; N004) — are exactly the facts general inference cannot synthesize. Under a checks-only compiler, a fact the writer does not state does not exist and the optimizer runs conservative. Given D0's extreme-performance mandate, every fact left unstated is a permanent performance forfeit; every fact stated costs only tokens, which D0 prices at zero. Proof-carrying source is therefore not one option among peers — it is the unique regime in which the reachable fact set equals the optimizer-consumable fact set. (2) THE CORPUS'S ONLY OBJECTION TO SPARK-GRADE BURDEN IS PRICED IN A CURRENCY THIS PROJECT DOES NOT PAY. F006 is a workflow-confirmed (3-0) existence proof that prover-enforced anti-aliasing, access-type ownership, and side-effect-free expressions work in a shipped language. Its carded failure mode — round1-violation-semantics.md, Position 2 weaknesses: "annotation burden, prover time, and rejection rates are... historically adoption-limiting (F006 negative scope)" — is a HUMAN-adoption failure mode. D0 explicitly rules human ergonomics inadmissible. The strongest evidence against maximal proof burden in this corpus dissolves under the charter's own cost model; what remains (writer error-rate, token volume, compile latency) is admissible, measurable, and EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED rather than adverse. (3) PROOF-CARRYING SOURCE COLLAPSES THE VIOLATION-SEMANTICS PROBLEM INSTEAD OF ANSWERING IT. Round-1's only convergence point across all three positions is that compiler-proven contracts are assumable in every build mode (round1-violation-semantics.md, Rationale); the entire residual dispute lives in the asserted-but-unproven middle tier. A writer-stated, checker-verified invariant IS a proven contract: check-then-emit discharges the frontend obligation that F003's negative scope and misleading-comparisons #5 warn about, so every emitted noalias/nsw/range/inbounds channel (F003, N002, N004, N005) is truthful by construction and grants maximal transform freedom on the N001 lattice with zero unchecked assumptions. Simultaneously it minimizes runtime-check residue: N003 shows guards pin speculation and A005's runtime alias checks + versioning cost code size — every obligation discharged statically deletes a check and a motion barrier. Maximal proof burden is thus the extreme-performance answer to the open round-3 question, and it shrinks the contested middle tier toward the FFI wall rather than legislating it. (4) UNDER ZERO AI-CODEGEN EVIDENCE, CHOOSE THE REGIME WHOSE UNKNOWN-ERROR-RATE FAILURE MODE IS RECOVERABLE. We do not know the writer's per-fact hallucination rate ε (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED, corpus has zero AI-codegen cards). Proof-carrying source is the only regime whose soundness is independent of ε: a bad writer produces compile failures and repair iterations — admissible D0 costs — never miscompiles. Low-burden regimes do not trust the writer more safely; under the spine nothing is trusted anyway — they simply cap fact density, converting uncertainty about ε into a certain, permanent performance ceiling. Decision-theoretically, tokens and iterations are recoverable currency; forfeited facts are not. Supporting synergies: the syntax verdict already mandates that the stored artifact be fully elaborated with every type/mode/effect/contract explicit — proof-carrying source has the writer author what the store already requires, dissolving the elaborator-as-codec second-spelling tension flagged as an open tension in round2-synthesis; the drift-check machinery (elaboration diffing, redundancy-as-hallucination-checksum, round2-synthesis Additions) gets maximal content-addressed material; and unlike type restatement, invariants/ranges/effect rows are semantically richer than the code they annotate, so checking them is a second derivation rather than an echo — the correlated-wrongness objection (round2-type-inference defender horn b) is structurally weakest exactly where this position adds burden. Round-1's Position-2 critique that "static proof required" conflated two regimes without choosing is answered: this position chooses writer-authored, deterministically-checked, prover-discharged obligations, with the checker deterministic and resource-bounded per canonical-form doctrine and proof hints stored as source artifacts when discharge needs help.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Workflow-confirmed (3-0) existence proof that prover-enforced anti-aliasing, ownership-transfer access types, and side-effect-free expressions are achievable in a shipped language — the closest carded exemplar of SPARK-grade proof burden; its negative scope (usability costs) is the human-ergonomics cost class D0 rules inadmissible.

Establishes that restrict-like contracts 'depend on programmer truthfulness or unsafe assumptions' (negative scope) — the reason writer-stated facts must be checked, which is this position's defining mechanism; also carded existence of machine-checked non-interference (safe Rust, SPARK).

noalias/alias.scope are UB-on-violation IR channels sound 'only if frontend obligations are true' — check-then-emit is the only regime that discharges this obligation for every emitted fact; instruction-level metadata granularity also shows the fact vocabulary is dense, favoring a dense annotation source.

The definedness lattice shows proven facts grant maximal transform freedom while a false contract has unbounded blast radius by construction — proof-carrying source takes the maximal-freedom end while making the false-contract state unrepresentable in accepted programs.
nsw/nuw are per-operation contracts, not hints; a writer-stated, checker-verified overflow-freedom annotation lowers to them truthfully, converting proof burden directly into optimizer fact density.

Immediate-UB guards block speculation and pin code motion — every obligation discharged statically by a checked annotation deletes a runtime check and a motion barrier, the performance argument for maximal static discharge.
range metadata is a poison-backed semantic promise — writer-stated ranges (checked) are the only general source of such facts, since ranges beyond dataflow reach cannot be inferred.
inbounds GEP contracts are provenance-grade facts requiring frontend proof; checked writer-stated bounds facts discharge them, and statically-proven bounds remove residual runtime checks.

Alive2 validates IR transforms but not frontend truth — confirming that the truth of source facts is the language's burden, which check-then-emit uniquely satisfies; also precedent for llvm.assume-style preconditions as first-class proof artifacts.

Vectorizer legality consumes alias/dependence facts, reductions/inductions, trip counts — the concrete backend consumers of writer-stated loop invariants and disjointness proofs; also shows the runtime-check-plus-versioning cost that static discharge avoids.
Affine restriction shows restricting/declaring index structure makes dependence analysis tractable — precedent that writer-declared structure, not inference, is what buys loop-level facts.

(provisional, not adversarially verified) Type-level Send/Sync capabilities are the carded route to race-freedom facts; its negative scope — manual unchecked impls are the UB channel — is precisely the channel a checks-everything regime closes.

Names the payoff classes (register promotion, load/store elimination, reordering) that proven non-interference facts unlock; its negative scope (no magnitude quantification) is honestly conceded as the payoff-side gap.

All three positions converge that compiler-proven contracts are assumable in every build mode; proof-carrying source moves nearly all fact mass into that uncontested tier and shrinks the contested asserted-unproven middle tier toward the FFI wall; also records F006-burden objections as human-adoption costs.

Decided law already points this direction: verification spine (no third state), fully-elaborated stored artifact, checkable redundancy channels with mismatch-as-hard-error, contract-edit gating, machine-readable diagnostics — proof-carrying source is the completion of these commitments, and it dissolves the elaborator-codec open tension by making the writer author the elaborated form.

Establishes the no-free-third-state ruling and the decided explicit-boundary floor; frames prosecutor COUNT 2 (annotations as checked redundancy catching hallucination) as the strongest keep argument, with the correlated-wrongness rebuttal EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — and correlation is structurally weakest for semantically-richer facts (invariants, ranges, effects) where this position adds burden.

Every sound position surveyed runs through checked discharge (ownership machinery as common denominator); P3's verified-annotation discipline is 'exactly the emission rule memory-object-model.md mandates' — writer-verified annotations are the sanctioned emission route; also sources the SPARK-decidability-bought-by-bans concession.

concessions: Every writer-behavior claim in this position is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence cards, so the claims that an AI writer can emit SPARK-grade invariants at scale, that repair loops converge, and that annotation strength will not degrade are all unproven. SPARK's human evidence does not transfer, and even SPARK's own discharge rates and annotation densities are uncarded (round1-aliasing follow-up, still open).

The checker enforces truth, not strength: 'invariant true' everywhere passes every check. Maximal proof burden enables maximal fact density but does not guarantee it — the optimizer payoff is an upper bound, and enforcing annotation strength requires lint-class pressure whose effectiveness on AI writers is itself EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED (round2-synthesis flags exactly this open question).

Correlated wrongness (round2-type-inference defender horn b) is mechanistically plausible via autoregressive conditioning; I argue it is structurally weakest for invariants/ranges/effects, but that is an untested rate claim, and for plain interior type restatement the objection has real force — the never-infers absolutism is this position's weakest edge, and the mismatch-as-hard-error cross-check channel (round2-synthesis, already-mandated) achieves most of the redundancy while retaining checker derivation for facts the checker CAN derive. The core of the position survives that amendment (burden on underivable facts); the literal 'never infers' formulation may not.

Expressiveness frontier: SPARK's decidability is bought by bans (round1-aliasing, P3 weaknesses), and round-1 violation-semantics records that proof-gated regimes reject or deoptimize exactly the dynamic-aliasing, FFI-adjacent, pointer-rich code where the threat model says verification fails and payoff may be highest. The size of that frontier is unmeasured; if it is large, proof-everywhere forfeits performance exactly where D0 wants it most, and the conservative-fallback/FFI-wall residue grows.

Compile-loop latency and token economics are unpriced: the O(k^2)-style token-inflation argument is structurally sound and invariants/effect rows are strictly bigger than type annotations; closed-world compile-loop pricing is already flagged as the modules verdict's biggest unpriced cost and proof obligations multiply it. All admissible under D0, but 'admissible' is not 'free' — thresholds must be pre-committed and measured.

SMT-class discharge threatens the canonical-determinism doctrine: prover timeouts and version drift can make acceptance nondeterministic. The mitigation — a deterministic, resource-bounded checking procedure with writer-supplied proof hints stored as source artifacts — is undesigned machinery with zero corpus cards (no Dafny/Why3/F*/PCC exemplars exist in the corpus).

Annotation-vocabulary churn risk scales with annotation density (round2-type-inference, What must be true): this position maximizes density and therefore maximizes the systemic-rewrite risk if ownership/effect/mode vocabularies change after mass generation. Vocabulary stabilization is a hard sequencing precondition.
risks: Iterations-to-green explosion: if per-fact hallucination rates are nontrivial, proof failures multiply repair iterations, compounded by the trap=abort multi-process test-harness economics the error-handling verdict knowingly accepted — the loop-cost measurement could fail its threshold and force the regime back to boundary-only burden.

Repair-loop asymmetry on proof diagnostics: a failed verification condition ('invariant not preserved by loop body') is the hardest diagnostic class to act on; whether AI writers can repair failed VCs at acceptable rates is untested and is not currently in the round-2 experiment program at all.

Weakening-laundering: a writer that cannot prove a strong invariant will weaken it until it proves — the proof-burden analog of the contract-laundering problem contract-edit gating exists to stop. Gating interior invariants stalls repair loops; leaving them ungated permits silent fact-strength erosion. The gating policy for proof annotations is undesigned.

Checker TCB growth: this regime maximizes the obligation surface of the soundness-critical checker (invariant checking, range analysis, effect containment, ownership transfer). The corpus has zero cards on verifier defect history or implementation-soundness (round1-aliasing gap); a bug in a bigger proof kernel silently converts 'checked' facts into F003-class miscompiles — the exact D1 breach the regime exists to prevent.

One-way door: retrofitting a corpus annotated at maximal density after a vocabulary or checker-semantics change is the largest possible regeneration event; the decision is harder to reverse than any low-burden alternative.

Fact-density mirage: if the weak-annotation equilibrium dominates (writers emitting trivially-provable facts), the language pays full proof-regime costs while the optimizer receives near-boundary-only fact density — the worst point in the tradeoff space.
evidenceGaps: Zero AI-codegen evidence cards — the flagship gap; every writer-cost and writer-error claim on all sides of this debate is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED per the round-2 standing flag.

No proof-burden exemplar cards beyond F006: no SPARK proof-discharge-rate or annotation-density data, no Dafny/Why3/F*/proof-carrying-code family cards, and zero region/effect-verifier exemplars (Cyclone/MLKit/Koka/DPJ — flagged as blocking obligations in round2-synthesis), so the effect-row half of this position rests on an uncarded mechanism family.

No quantified performance payoff for any proven-fact density level (F002 negative scope; caveats.md: documentation evidence does not quantify magnitude) — the entire benefit side is payoff-in-kind, not payoff-in-magnitude.

No end-to-end card showing checked source facts survive lowering and measurably fire optimizations (F003 negative scope; N006 validates IR transforms, not frontend truth; round-1 follow-up on metadata survival through -O2/-O3 still open).

Load-bearing cards A005, C004, A001-A006 are direct extractions without adversarial verification votes; the runtime-check-elision and race-freedom legs of this argument must be re-checked when they are verified.

Missing experiments this position needs before it can pass any decision gate: (a) extend the redundancy-independence experiment from type annotations to invariant/range/effect/transfer annotations, measuring catch-rate vs correlated-wrongness vs friction; (b) an AI VC-repair-loop experiment (fix success rate and latency on proof-failure diagnostics — currently absent from the round-2 experiment program); (c) a proof-burden-ladder benchmark: proof-everywhere vs boundary-only vs runtime-check-residue regimes on the same kernels, measuring fact coverage delivered, tokens, iterations-to-green, and runtime delta; (d) a weak-annotation-equilibrium probe: do unattended writers emit strong or trivial invariants under lint pressure; (e) feasibility study of a deterministic, resource-bounded discharge procedure with source-carried proof hints compatible with canonical-form determinism.

Frontier sizing (round1-aliasing follow-up, still open): what fraction of performance-critical code cannot carry discharged proofs at all — the variable that decides whether proof-everywhere maximizes or forfeits performance under D0.

result.position: Solver-assisted obligations: the AI writer states facts (only the boundary-explicit intent facts the round-2 floors already mandate, plus optional checked hints); the compiler generates every residual proof obligation (bounds, overflow-freedom per declared numeric mode, value-dependent disjointness, contract-group laws); a deterministic, hard-budgeted SMT/prover pass discharges what it can, promoting facts into the compiler-proven tier; whatever remains undischarged falls to the round-3 check-policy ruling (A005-style runtime check + versioning where a check exists, sound fact-omission otherwise). The writer is never a trusted inference source; proof burden lands on toolchain compute — the one cost channel D0 explicitly prices as admissible.
result.coreArgument: Under the round-2 verification-spine law — "there is no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed" (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md, VERIFICATION SPINE; F003/N006) — the round-3 question "how much proof burden can be placed on the AI writer" is not a soundness dial at all: anything the writer emits is either checked or forbidden. Solver-assisted obligations is the position that takes this law literally and converts writer burden into an elastic, sound-at-every-setting tuning knob. The architecture is a monotone lattice. Tier 0: the ownership/capability core discharges D1 by construction (F001 safe-Rust exclusivity; C004 Send/Sync) — the solver never touches the D1 envelope, so the fixed constraint is structurally insulated from prover behavior. Tier 1: the writer states facts, exactly those the decided floors already require explicit at every boundary (round2-type-inference decided floors) — statement, not proof. Tier 2: the compiler generates residual obligations and the budgeted prover discharges what it can, growing the best-attested tier in the entire corpus — round1-violation-semantics' leading candidate already blesses "compiler-proven contracts assumable in every build mode" as the top tier, and notes the corpus's best-attested wins (F001/F005/F006) all live there. Tier 3: undischarged residue falls to the check-policy ruling, which is itself carded — A005 shows runtime pointer checks + loop versioning are LLVM vectorizers' standard legality strategy, a passed guard legitimizes the same contract on dominated paths (refuting "checking forfeits the payoff"), and every carded fact channel has a sound omission fallback (N002 unflagged, N004, N005 non-inbounds). Budget exhaustion therefore moves an obligation from proven to checked to omitted — always a performance event, never a soundness event. Three properties make this the strongest fit to the fixed constraints. (1) D0-alignment without behavioral bets: the corpus has zero AI-codegen evidence, and every rival position's load-bearing premise is an EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED writer-behavior claim — that writers can truthfully carry full annotation/proof burden at scale, or that marked assertions stay honest under performance pressure ("marking metastasis," flagged unsized in round1-violation-semantics). Solver-assisted obligations requires of the writer only what the floors already require; its soundness is invariant to writer quality because discharge is deterministic toolchain code with the corpus's only workflow-confirmed prover precedent (F006: SPARK prover-enforced anti-aliasing, ownership, side-effect-free expressions, 3-0 verified). Compile compute is explicitly admissible (D0; round2-synthesis names compile time "an admissible AI cost channel," D001/D004), and the canonical-form floor gives content-addressed memoization of proof results on canonical bytes essentially for free. (2) Trust-architecture alignment: round2-type-inference ruled that "under a least-trusted-writer charter, moving derived-fact production into the checker is a verification improvement" (C004 derived facts vs manual assertion as THE UB channel; F001 negative scope: restrict-like contracts depend on programmer truthfulness). Solver discharge IS checker-derived fact production, and the ir-strategy fact-provenance rule ("reject facts not stated-and-checked or checker-derived") admits it natively while rejecting trusted assertion natively. (3) The decided floors build the solver's runway without having been chosen for it: the fully-elaborated canonical artifact gives the prover a defaulting-free explicit input; zero implicit conversions plus one-mode-per-node (N002) make overflow obligations syntactically local; mono-only generics keep every obligation first-order and per-instantiation (round2-metaprogramming: "zero staging keeps every obligation first-order, per-site, decidable"); closed-world/no-interposition (D008 cut) means no fact is falsified by linking behind the prover's back; and the floor-set forecloses the fourth policy — J001/J002 speculate-and-deoptimize is incompatible with closed-world AOT, no-unwinding, trap=abort — so prove-or-check-or-omit exhausts the sound design space. Finally, elasticity answers the round-3 question directly: verbosity-is-free is honored as an accelerator, not a crutch — the writer MAY emit loop invariants and lemma hints as optional checked redundancy (generalizing round2-synthesis' "redundancy as hallucination checksum"), and because hints are themselves solver-checked they can only enlarge the proven set, never inject unsoundness. The writer can be handed arbitrarily much proof burden and must be handed almost none; burden becomes a coverage/performance parameter with a sound floor, not a trust decision. No rival position has that property.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: The corpus's only workflow-confirmed (3-0) exemplar of a prover in the trust path enforcing optimizer-grade facts: SPARK prover-enforced anti-aliasing, access-type ownership, side-effect-free expressions. Existence proof that prover-discharged obligations can meet the soundness bar.

Tier-0 by-construction discharge of D1 (safe Rust &mut exclusivity); its negative scope — restrict-like contracts 'depend on programmer truthfulness' — is precisely the dependency solver discharge eliminates for residual facts.

Compiler-DERIVED facts (auto traits) vs manual assertion as the UB channel: the trust architecture favoring checker-side fact production that solver discharge instantiates. Flag: C004 is a provisional direct extraction, not workflow-confirmed (per round2 correction).

Cards the tier-3 fallback: runtime alias checks + loop versioning are LLVM vectorizers' standard legality strategy; a passed guard legitimizes assume-grade contracts on dominated paths — so prover timeout degrades to a precedented mechanism, not a payoff cliff.

The refinement lattice gives a false contract unbounded silent blast radius — why trusted writer assertion is intolerable and why every level of my proven/checked/omitted lattice must be sound; also honestly prices the solver-bug risk (a false discharge inherits the same blast radius).

Per-operation nsw/nuw semantics with a sound unflagged fallback: overflow obligations are generated locally from the declared mode, and omission on budget exhaustion is always sound — the monotone-degradation property.
Range metadata as a checkable fact channel with sound omission — another rung where solver-discharged facts can be emitted or safely dropped.
inbounds vs non-inbounds GEP: sound omission fallback for pointer-arithmetic obligations, completing the claim that every carded channel degrades soundly.

noalias/alias.scope facts are UB-bearing promises 'only sound if frontend obligations are true' — establishes that obligation GENERATION is the real soundness surface, and that solver-discharged facts satisfy the stated-and-checked provenance rule.
Go race-halt is the only carded post-violation observability precedent; my tier-3 checked fallback under trap=abort inherits it, giving this position the observability story pure proof-gating lacks.

Speculate-and-deoptimize is the enumerated fourth policy; the decided floors (closed-world AOT, no unwinding, trap=abort) foreclose it, so prove-or-check-or-omit exhausts the sound envelope — my lattice is complete, not one option among four.

Mono-only generics make every obligation first-order and per-instantiation (no bound-solving in the trust path), directly improving prover tractability; D001/D004 also anchor compile time as an admissible AI cost channel.

Leading candidate (graduated obligations) already puts compiler-proven contracts as the top tier with the corpus's best-attested wins; names A005 the carded middle mechanism; its evidence-gap list (checked-tier mechanics, llvm.assume recovery, three-way benchmark) is exactly my gap list.

The verification-spine law (no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed), the fact-provenance rule, trap=abort floor, canonical artifact + content-addressed caching, and 'redundancy as hallucination checksum' — the decided substrate my position runs on.

Ruled that under a least-trusted-writer charter, moving derived-fact production into the checker is a verification improvement; decided floors make all boundary facts explicit — the writer-states-facts tier is already law, so my position adds zero writer-side mandates.
Checked ownership is the leading candidate and common denominator of every sound variant — my tier 0; its 'checker as trusted computing base' evidence gap honestly prices adding a prover to the TCB.

'Zero staging keeps every obligation first-order, per-site, decidable' — the floor-set was built in the prover's favor; also the precedent for routing burden to mandatory toolchain machinery rather than the trusted compiler or the writer.

concessions: F006 evidences prover-enforced RESTRICTION (SPARK bans hard cases to buy decidability), not general SMT discharge of arbitrary arithmetic/aliasing obligations; round1-aliasing-model explicitly notes SPARK's decidability is 'bought by the bans,' and F006's negative scope flags usability costs of mapping to a general-purpose performance language. My extrapolation from restriction-checking to budgeted SMT discharge is uncarded.

The solver joins the trusted computing base: a false discharge feeds F003/N002 UB channels with N001-unbounded silent blast radius. The corpus has zero cards on prover/borrow-checker implementation defect history (round1-aliasing-model gap), and the mitigation (proof-producing solver + small independent proof checker) is itself uncarded. This also runs against the grain of the metaprogramming verdict's restriction pattern, which argues against embedding large evaluators in the trusted compiler.

Hard time budgets introduce performance nondeterminism: the proven/checked partition can shift across solver versions, seeds, or machines. Deterministic resource limits (conflict/memory-based rather than wall-clock) and pinned solver versions mitigate but are unevidenced, and the instability collides with the unresolved charter question of predictable-cost-model vs guaranteed-optimization-floor (round2-synthesis open tensions).

D1 credit belongs entirely to the ownership/capability core (F001/C004), not the solver; this position governs the optimizer-fact envelope and the check-policy residue. I cannot claim the solver strengthens memory/thread safety — only that it is architecturally fenced off from weakening it.

For obligation classes that are both prover-hard and runtime-uncheckable — whole-execution noalias disjointness (A005 covers only loop-bounded pointer-range checks) and purity/law bundles (no dynamic-check or violation-semantics card exists; F006 is static-only) — my fallback is conservative fact-omission, i.e., I quietly concede exactly the payoffs Position-2-style proof gating concedes, on the classes where payoff may be highest.

Every claim about the writer-hint loop (that an AI writer, given a machine-readable undischarged-obligation diagnostic, supplies useful checked invariants) is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus has zero AI-codegen evidence cards, per the standing discipline.

The check-policy ruling my tier 3 falls back to is itself undecided (user-directives.md D1 delegates it to this round-3 debate); my position is parameterized by a sibling ruling and cannot be finalized independently of it.
risks: Budget cliff where it hurts most: the obligations hardest to discharge concentrate in exactly the dynamic-aliasing, value-dependent hot-loop code where the threat model says verification fails and payoff is highest (mirroring round1-violation-semantics' verified weakness of Position 2) — a timeout there puts a runtime check in the hottest loop or drops the fact entirely.

TCB enlargement: a solver soundness bug produces silent miscompiles indistinguishable from the trusted-assertion failure mode this position exists to eliminate; without a proof-checking architecture the position's central selling point degrades to 'trust a different large program.'

Reproducible-performance drift: solver upgrades silently reshuffle the proven/checked ledger across builds; the required countermeasures (pinned solver, discharge ledger recorded in the build artifact) create a new stored artifact the syntax verdict's second-spelling policing must govern.

Compile-loop latency compounds the closed-world cost the modules verdict already names its biggest unpriced item; AI writers regenerating whole functions may defeat content-addressed proof caching (low hit rates), putting the prover pass on the critical path of every iteration.

Opaque solver diagnostics: the corpus contains no diagnostics evidence at all (round2-interfaces), and SMT failure reports are notoriously non-actionable; if undischarged-obligation diagnostics are not machine-actionable, the AI repair loop stalls precisely where the design routes work to the writer-hint channel.

Obligation-generation completeness is the real soundness surface: a missed obligation class in the compiler's generator is a hole no prover can close (F003 negative scope — sound only if frontend obligations are true); the generator needs the same adversarial validation planned for lowering (N006/Alive2-style).

Hint-channel rot: if empirical discharge rates are low, the 'optional' hint channel becomes de-facto mandatory proof burden on the writer, reintroducing the annotation-burden/adoption failure mode F006's negative scope and round1-violation-semantics flag as historically limiting — the position must pre-commit a discharge-rate threshold below which it concedes to a rival regime.
evidenceGaps: No SPARK/gnatprove proof-discharge-rate or proof-time-distribution cards; missing-research-backlog.jsonl already lists SPARK discharge rates (aliasing follow-up and round2 memory-frontier item) as missing and blocking — the single number my coverage claims most depend on.

Zero cards on SMT-in-compile-loop engineering: budget determinism (rlimit vs wall-clock), incremental/content-addressed proof caching hit rates under regeneration-heavy edits, prover latency at whole-program scale.
Zero AI-codegen evidence anywhere in the corpus: writer response to obligation diagnostics, invariant/lemma hint quality, hint-loop iteration counts — all EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED and gating.
No prover/checker defect-history cards (round1-aliasing-model's 'checker as trusted computing base' gap), so paper soundness vs implementation soundness cannot be distinguished for the enlarged TCB.
Whether llvm.assume after a passed runtime check recovers assumption-grade optimization is uncarded (round1-violation-semantics gap) — this prices my entire tier-3 fallback.

The three-way benchmark named the 'single most decision-capable missing artifact' (UB-asserted vs runtime-checked+versioned vs proof-gated on aliasing/bounds/overflow kernels) directly prices my tier-2 vs tier-3 boundary; it does not exist.

Purity and quantified law obligations: no violation-semantics card, no dynamic-check card, and no card on SMT reach for quantified algebraic laws — the class where solver assistance is most needed has the least evidence.
Contract survival through lowering (F003/F009 negative scopes): solver-discharged facts must survive to the passes that consume them; no end-to-end preservation measurement exists.
Check-elision rates: how often a prover pass would statically elide A005-style guards is unmeasured, so the net runtime-check density under this position cannot be estimated.

result.position: Runtime barrier: every FFI call is a compiler-inserted optimization barrier — all optimizer facts about FFI-reachable state are conservatively invalidated across the call (C003-style discipline), memory that may be retained by foreign code is permanently demoted to an explicit foreign-shared storage contract, and optional sanitizer-instrumented dev builds provide the detection channel. Sound by construction: no trust in foreign behavioral contracts, no marshaling copies; the cost is optimization strength in FFI-adjacent code and on the escaped set only.
result.coreArgument: The wall does not verify the foreigner and does not check facts at the boundary — it WITHDRAWS them. External code cannot falsify internal proofs because, under this design, no internal proof's quantification domain includes any state external code can name. Falsification requires an asserted fact to falsify; the barrier makes the falsifiable state carry no facts and the fact-carrying state unreachable to the falsifier. Concretely, attenuation is three composed mechanisms. (1) PARTITION: the closed-world, whole-program floor (round-2 decided law) plus the explicit FFI extern-region declarations (round2-modules 'what must be true') let the compiler compute the exact FFI-reachable set: everything reachable from pointers passed to extern calls, from extern return values, and from the statically known, closed set of internal functions exported as callbacks. The no-interposition cut (D008) is what makes this set computable and final — in an open world the conservative barrier would have to invalidate everything; in the closed world it invalidates a bounded, known set. This is the load-bearing synergy: the modules verdict concentrated all boundary fact-loss at one wall (D004/D007/D008 boundary pathologies), and the barrier design is the mechanism that makes that concentration pay — F001/F002/F003-class non-interference payoffs run at FULL strength over the entire FFI-unreachable interior, which in a closed-world program is almost everything. (2) BARRIER: each extern call site is a full mod/ref-unknown barrier for the reachable set — no value caching across the call, no load/store motion across it, no introduced writes, re-derive facts only from post-call re-loads. This is not new machinery: C003 cards exactly this discipline as what every production compiler already does for possibly-shared memory and opaque calls, and F002 (contrapositive) confirms that withholding non-interference facts is the always-available sound default. Omission is always sound: F003's negative scope says UB-bearing metadata is 'only sound if frontend obligations are true' — the barrier simply never emits metadata whose truth conditions quantify over foreign behavior, so by the N001 lattice there is no false fact to propagate and the unbounded blast radius of a falsified contract is unrepresentable at this boundary. (3) DEMOTION: memory that escapes in a way foreign code may retain (stored pointers, foreign threads, callback captures) is permanently demoted — its pointer type loses the unshared/Send-Sync-class capability (C004 is the carded channel for making sharing a type-level fact) and acquires an explicit foreign-shared storage contract (fits the round-2 'explicit storage contracts' floor), with accesses lowered into C005's graded atomic vocabulary. Between calls the compiler treats demoted memory as concurrently mutable, so a foreigner who stashes a pointer and writes later from its own thread still cannot falsify anything: nothing stable was ever assumed about that memory. This position is also the only one consistent with law already on the books. The round-2 verification spine rules 'the verifier rejects any optimizer fact not originating from a stated-and-checked or checker-derived source... there is no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed' (round2-synthesis-minimal-core). A declared trust contract on an extern signature ('this C function only reads arg1, retains nothing') is precisely that forbidden third state, at the one boundary where the threat model's verify column reads a flat 'no' (unsound-optimization-threat-model, FFI-callbacks row); the same row's remedy column — 'boundary contracts / conservative fallback' — cards my position as the fallback branch. Round 1 already converged here trilaterally: all three violation-semantics positions, which agreed on almost nothing else, agreed that 'unverifiable boundaries get conservative fallback' (round1-violation-semantics rationale) — the barrier is that convergence point promoted to a design, needing no new debate to license it. For an AI writer the trust alternative is actively worse: round2-modules identifies coherent-pair hallucination as the failure mode declared contracts exist to catch, and at the FFI wall there is no foreign body to re-verify the declaration against, so contract-edit gating — the designated anti-laundering defense — has nothing to gate against; a hallucinated extern effect annotation is a silent, unbounded-blast-radius miscompile (N001) with no checker on either side. The barrier's failure asymmetry is the correct one under D1: partition/escape-analysis imprecision degrades OPTIMIZATION (over-invalidation), never soundness — compare declared contracts, where imprecision is a miscompile. Against copy-marshaling, the barrier preserves zero-copy interop with exactly the targets the interop dossier names (GPU/accelerator runtimes, system libraries) where shared buffers are the entire point, and copies do not even solve the problem — a copied-out buffer says nothing about callbacks or foreign threads mutating what was copied from. The control-flow half composes cleanly with decided floors: foreign unwinds abort at the boundary (round2-error-handling, provisional), trap=abort with machine-readable reports; the sanitizer-instrumented dev build (TSan/ASan-class interceptors on FFI edges) is the detection channel for violations of the residual ABI axiom, aligned with the corpus's only carded post-violation observability precedent (C002 Go race-detection halts). Costs land where no rival could soundly optimize anyway: code adjacent to the wall and the demoted set — code whose semantics genuinely depend on unverifiable foreign behavior. Mitigation is writer-side and D0-admissible: batching and hoisting FFI calls, staging into unshared buffers before hot loops — verbose mechanical rewrites the AI writer performs for free (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED that writers actually do this reliably).

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Cards the exact conservative discipline the barrier reuses: compilers must not introduce writes, must not re-load values (one read observing multiple values), must not cache possibly-shared values across barriers, and must treat fences as motion barriers. Shows barrier semantics is precedented production-compiler machinery, not novel soundness apparatus — the FFI wall applies an existing discipline at one declared boundary.

noalias/alias.scope facts are UB-bearing promises 'only sound if frontend obligations are true.' The barrier's soundness argument is the contrapositive: never emit any fact whose truth conditions quantify over foreign behavior, and there is no obligation to discharge and nothing for external code to falsify. Omission is the always-sound fallback.

The value lattice shows a single false fact has unbounded, silent, non-local blast radius. This is why checking-at-the-wall or trusting-at-the-wall are the wrong shapes: the only attenuation that bounds blast radius to zero is withdrawal — a fact never asserted across the wall cannot enter the lattice falsely.

Enumerates precisely what non-interference facts buy (register promotion, redundant-read elimination, dead-write elimination, reordering) — i.e., exactly what the barrier forfeits on the FFI-reachable set and preserves at full strength on the interior. Its negative scope ('does not quantify performance magnitude') is also my honest cost concession.

Rust Send/Sync + &mut exclusivity demonstrate that sharing is expressible as a type-level fact. This is the carded channel for permanent demotion: a pointer that crosses the wall loses its unshared capability in the type system, so 'escaped to FFI' is a checked, explicit fact rather than a per-callsite heuristic — integrating the wall with the D1 ownership/capability core.

LLVM's graded atomic orderings (and racing non-atomic loads as undef, not instant UB) provide the concrete lowering vocabulary for demoted foreign-shared memory: accesses to escaped state between FFI calls have defined, conservative IR semantics rather than UB, so a foreign thread mutating retained memory cannot manufacture UB in accepted internal code.

Go's race-detection halts are the corpus's only carded detect-and-abort observability precedent, supporting the sanitizer-instrumented dev-build half under the trap-with-report=abort floor. Its negative scope (race-semantics choice open, no performance quantification) also bounds what I may claim about demoted-memory semantics.

ELF semantic interposition blocking IPA/inlining is carded proof that linkage-level semantics silently falsify optimizer assumptions about 'statically known' code. The closed-world/no-interposition cut eliminates this channel internally and — critically — makes the exported-callback set computable, which is what gives the conservative barrier bounded rather than total invalidation.

Codegen-unit/LTO boundaries inhibit optimization with expensive buy-back — the carded boundary-pathology evidence that motivated confining all fact loss to a single wall. The barrier position is the design that cashes that confinement: one wall, one bounded loss region, interior untouched.

LTO/IPA/devirtualization recovery machinery is conditional, option-dependent, and revertible — evidence that trying to recover facts ACROSS boundaries is fragile and expensive, supporting the choice to accept bounded fact loss at the wall rather than build cross-wall fact-recovery machinery with no soundness basis.

HotSpot escape analysis is conditional and implementation-dependent (generalization confidence low). Cited honestly against my own position: wall-side escape/reachability analysis precision is not free, and imprecision inflates the invalidated set. Supports the failure-direction claim — imprecision here costs optimization, never soundness.

The interior payoff being protected: language-level non-aliasing/non-interference rules are the central optimizer enablers. The barrier exists so these proofs remain fully assumable everywhere inside the wall — the wall's purpose is to keep F001-class facts' quantification domains closed under foreign linking.

The FFI-callbacks row states facts breakable (effects/alias/lifetime), compiler-verifiable: 'no', remedy: 'boundary contracts / conservative fallback.' My position is the conservative-fallback branch — the only remedy in the threat model's own table that carries zero truth obligations on foreign code.

Records the tri-position convergence 'unverifiable boundaries (FFI, int-ptr casts) get conservative no-contract fallback' — the barrier is the already-agreed convergence point, not a new claim. Establishes that the single FFI wall now carries ALL boundary load, that fact-attenuation must be designed before the closed-world commitment hardens, and that contract-laundering / coherently-hallucinated declarations are the AI-writer failure mode — which at the FFI wall has no checkable foreign body to gate against, dooming the declared-trust alternative.

The provisional abort-at-boundary rule for foreign unwinds (explicitly flagged evidence-free) is the control-flow component of total attenuation; the barrier position composes with it into one coherent wall semantics: no data facts, no control-flow facts, no unwind propagation across the boundary.

The leading candidate (type-enforced data-race freedom, Send/Sync-class capabilities) is what the demotion mechanism plugs into; and its finding that non-interference proofs quantify over race-free executions is exactly why escaped memory must be permanently demoted rather than merely barriered at call sites — a foreign thread is a standing falsification threat between calls.

concessions: 'No trust' is bounded, not absolute: the barrier still rests on the universal ABI axiom — foreign code accesses only memory reachable from pointers it was legitimately given and honors the platform calling convention and synchronization primitives. A buggy C library performing wild out-of-reachability writes falsifies interior proofs under my design. But no rival language-level design (declared contracts, copies, verified bindings) discharges this axiom either; only process isolation does, and the round2-error-handling process-isolation doctrine is the composable escalation for genuinely untrusted libraries. Every position in this debate shares this floor assumption; mine just refuses to assume anything beyond it.

D1 scoping must be stated honestly: the barrier guarantees the optimizer never miscompiles on a foreign-falsified fact and that internal proofs never quantify over foreign behavior; it does NOT make foreign code memory-safe. Use-after-free of an allocation whose ownership was explicitly transferred out of the language is detectable only in sanitizer builds, not preventable. D1's 'impossible in accepted programs' holds for the language-checked interior; the envelope document must say exactly that rather than implying the wall extends D1 outward.

The position's headline 'costs optimization only in FFI-adjacent code' is only partially accurate: permanent demotion also costs on all escaped memory for the remainder of its lifetime, not just at call sites. Escape creep is a real hazard — if AI writers habitually pass large object graphs across the wall, the demoted set balloons and C003-class losses spread well beyond wall-adjacent code. A scoped-escape annotation ('callee does not retain') would recover this, but it is a lifetime trust contract — the first step onto exactly the slope this position exists to avoid; if ever adopted it must go through the round-1 marked-assertion machinery (per-fact, explicit, dev-checkable), never as a wall default.

The sanitizer-instrumented dev-build half of my position is entirely uncarded: round1-violation-semantics explicitly lists debug-mode dynamic checkability (Miri/UBSan/TSan-class) as an evidence gap, and caveats.md confirms no sanitizer coverage in the corpus. Practically, TSan/ASan see the foreign side only via interceptors unless the library is recompiled with instrumentation, so detection coverage of foreign violations is partial at best. I claim it as an optional channel, not a soundness component — soundness rests on the barrier alone.

Zero quantification anywhere: no card measures barrier cost, demoted-set access overhead (atomic-grade loads per C005), escaped-set sizes, or FFI-call frequency in performance-critical AI-generated code (caveats.md: documentation evidence never quantifies magnitude; F002 negative scope). Fine-grained FFI in hot loops — per-element calls into a native library — is the pathological workload where this design could lose badly to a declared-contract design, and I cannot currently bound that loss.

All claims that AI writers will mitigate barrier costs (batching calls, hoisting them out of loops, staging shared data into unshared buffers, keeping escaped sets small) are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED per the standing rule — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence.
risks: Performance-cliff pressure: if measurement shows FFI-adjacent and demoted-set code dominates real interop workloads (GPU runtimes, system libraries — the dossier's named targets), sustained pressure for declared trust contracts will mount. Without a pre-committed escalation path (the round-1 marked-assertion tier, per-fact and dev-checkable), the wall erodes ad hoc — the F005/Fortran-style 'unconfined promise' failure mode reintroduced at the worst possible boundary.

Demoted-memory semantics inherit the unresolved race-semantics residue: C002 marks bounded-vs-UB race semantics open and round1-concurrency-model is research_needed. If demoted accesses lower to C005 monotonic atomics the cost is C003-class optimization loss on all escaped memory; if anything weaker, foreign-thread races on demoted memory reopen a D1-spirit hole. The barrier position constrains but cannot close this; it must be decided jointly with the concurrency model.

Callback-set precision depends on the closed world staying closed: round2-modules names concurrency-driven runtime loading as its reopen trigger. If dynamic loading returns, the exported-callback set is no longer statically computable and the conservative barrier degrades toward invalidate-everything, gutting the cost story.

Wall-side reachability/escape analysis is uncarded as a mechanism and F007 warns that escape analysis precision is implementation-dependent; over-approximation directly converts to lost optimization across the program. The failure direction is safe (performance, not soundness), but an imprecise implementation could make the design uncompetitive and trigger the erosion risk above.

The sanitizer channel may not survive contact with reality (foreign libraries are rarely instrumentable), leaving this position with the weakest post-violation observability story of any candidate for foreign-side bugs — a real disadvantage against copy-marshaling designs, which at least localize corruption to the copy boundary.

challenges.challenge: The F006/SPARK precedent is overclaimed past its carded scope. The position's own headline — 'there is no source construct whose semantics is believe-me' — is falsified by its two retained constructs. FFI extern declarations with declared fact-attenuation and safe-signatured primitive imports ARE believe-me assertions present in accepted-program source; they differ from a hatch only in authorship policy (human, gated) not in semantic kind. Since the FFI wall is UNDESIGNED with zero primary-source cards, and P1 itself concedes writer-emitted FFI wrapping is the reroute channel, the claim reduces to 'no writer-emittable believe-me construct, conditional on an undesigned gate' — a much weaker and evaluable-only-later claim than the advertised total-envelope theorem.

Internal contradiction on frontier size: the core argument asserts the residue is 'small in kind, stable in form, and maximally reusable,' while the corpus (round1-aliasing evidence gaps, round2-memory-automation's reopening clause, the backlog frontier item) repeatedly names frontier size THE decisive unmeasured variable, and P1's own concession 2 admits it. A position may not both concede the decisive variable is unmeasured and premise its architecture on a favorable value of it. If the frontier measures large, the primitive set becomes the 'second, evidence-free language' round-2 already fears, and the audit-economics argument inverts.

Safe-signatured primitives smuggle a type-level hatch back into core surface. A mutex/atomic/channel primitive's safe signature must express mutation-through-shared-reference and Sync-capability assertions — UnsafeCell-class interior mutability — in the language's own type vocabulary. round1-aliasing flags interior mutability as puncturing shared-reference noalias facts (misleading-comparisons #2, uncarded tension), and round2-memory-automation explicitly bars admitting any shared-mutable/interior-mutability type before that tension is carded. Human-maintained primitives need an owner ruling against D0 that P1 never requests. D0 says all code is AI-written; P1's load-bearing authorship channel is a permanent human code-production pipeline for exactly the hottest, most defect-sensitive code. P3 at least requests the analogous boundary ruling for reviewers. P1 also concedes the human channel is a development-speed bottleneck in an AI-speed loop and only a policy fence — meaning the position's central guarantee degrades to Rust's status quo the moment the fence is breached, with no in-band detectability story.

The threat-model citation is tendentious: the remedy column's first row literally reads 'unsafe boundary / weaker metadata,' so 'the remedy column never requires in-language unsafe on any row' is true only under the reading that a slash-alternative always exists — by which reading the table equally 'never requires' prohibition. caveats.md rules implementation files are scaffolding that must not decide semantics. The manifest fails the very verification-spine rule it cites as forcing its form. Line 33 requires facts to be stated-AND-CHECKED or checker-derived, with 'no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed.' For the highest-value obligation classes — whole-execution noalias disjointness, purity, Send/Sync claims — no dynamic check is carded (round1-violation lines 48-49, 62-63, conceded), so a manifest entry there is stated-and-audited: the banned third state under a new name. 'Manifests are a checkable-redundancy channel of exactly the kind round 2 requires' contradicts round 2's own rationale for redundancy. Round-2 channels derive power from an INDEPENDENT checker (writer-stated facts cross-checked against inference; contracts re-verified against bodies every build). The manifest and body are authored by the same writer in the same pass — the precise 'same-pass output is error-correlated with itself' condition — and where no dynamic check exists there is no independent verifier to diff against. Likewise, consistency-layer mechanism 2 verifies deterministic-generator reproducibility by re-run-and-diff; it cannot re-derive or falsify an assertion's truth, so 'the manifest rides mechanism 2' overstates what the substrate does. Contract-edit gating also helps only on re-edits; initial coherent body+manifest theater passes untouched.

'Existence is already entailed; only the form is open' fails on document precedence and chronology. user-directives.md — the controlling constraint document, updated after the round-2 synthesis was written — explicitly delegates 'existence/form of any unsafe escape hatch' to this round; if round-2's 'unsafe regions' listings had settled existence, D1's delegation would be vacuous. The round-2/ir-strategy fact-boundary artifact is needed under any outcome precisely because it specifies where fact emission is SUPPRESSED — a no-hatch language needs the identical construct family for its FFI wall — so those citations cannot discriminate existence.

'Gate authorization restores two-state discipline' is a redefinition of 'checked.' The spine's dichotomy opposes checked obligations to TRUSTED ASSERTIONS; review — human or AI — is a trusted assertion with the trust relocated to the reviewer, not a mechanical check. P3's own concession 3 (the gate is toolchain policy, not a type-system invariant; a modified toolchain accepts the same artifact) and concession 4 (reviewer independence unresolved; same-model review shares the writer's correlated errors) concede the point the core argument denies. 'Stated-and-checked where the checker is the gated channel itself' is the third state wearing a badge.

The a-fortiori from contract-edit gating is disanalogous. Round-2's gating protects an EXISTING mechanical checker from laundering: the declaration is independently re-verified against the body every build, and the gate merely prevents the writer from moving the goalposts. A gated unsafe channel has no checker behind it for the obligations that matter — the gate SUBSTITUTES process for verification rather than protecting verification. 'If signature edits are gated, unsafe authorship must be at least as gated' borrows the decided mechanism's legitimacy without inheriting its structure; the honest claim is 'at least as restricted,' which does not privilege gating over prohibition (P1) or over proof-discharge requirements.

Quota-as-measurement is self-defeating: the core argument claims the gate ledger 'produces exactly' the missing frontier-sizing data as an operational byproduct, while concession 5 and the risks section say a quota-constrained writer contorts safe code, spams requests, or routes through the FFI wall. A capped, latency-priced channel measures demand under the cap, not the counterfactual frontier; the two claims cannot both hold. The frontier measurement remains a blocking prerequisite the gate does not discharge.

Structural convergence is larger than any advocate admits, and each position parks the D1 residue on its own largest evidence gap. All three reduce to: trusted assertions behind declared safe signatures, inaccessible to the steady-state writer, differing only in granularity and authorship channel. P1 concentrates residue at the FFI wall (zero primary-source cards, UNDESIGNED, named soundness precondition); P2 on an obligation ledger whose vocabulary is blocked by the undesigned memory-object model and on an uncarded debug tier; P3 on gating efficacy — itself an explicitly listed untested experiment (round2-modules follow-up d). Every position's decisive artifact is the least-carded thing in its own case, so the debate as argued cannot pass any decision gate; it can only rank which undesigned artifact to design first.

Content-addressed identity does not capture contextual validity. D1 quantifies over accepted programs; none of the positions defines acceptance as a stable, artifact-checkable predicate — only P1's pure-toolchain form even approaches one, and its FFI declarations reintroduce the same relativity.
result.sharpestTension: The round-2 verification spine — 'no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed' — is unsatisfiable as written at any trust boundary the language must have, and every position resolves the contradiction by vocabulary rather than mechanism: P1 relocates the third state outside the language and renames it 'TCB' (while its FFI wall, the zero-card, UNDESIGNED, verify='no' channel, becomes the pressure sink — strictly LESS checkable than in-language unsafe); P2 renames it 'stated-and-audited' (a manifest with no dynamic check is an unproven assertion with paperwork); P3 renames it 'gate-checked' (review is a trusted assertion with the trust moved). Which of these violates D1 therefore turns entirely on an undecided normative prior: whether D1's 'impossible in accepted programs' means an unconditional theorem over all accepted source (only P1 even approximates this, and only modulo an unbounded-growth primitive set plus an undesigned wall) or the conditional guarantee its own named reference bar actually provides (safe fragment unconditional, confined fragment obligation-conditional — which admits P2/P3). The corpus already demands exactly this owner ruling (missing-research-backlog numeric-semantics item: the meaning of 'soundness may not be sacrificed', with its aside that any unchecked mode 'must be unsafe/proof-gated'), yet no position treats the ruling as blocking, and each silently assumes the reading that crowns itself. Until the owner rules on D1's quantifier — and until the FFI wall and debug-checkability gaps are designed/carded so the three relocations can be compared on evidence rather than naming — this debate is three architectures for the same unavoidable residue, distinguished mainly by where each hides the spine violation it accuses the others of committing.

challenges.challenge: The 'compiler never infers optimizer-consumable facts' pillar contradicts DECIDED LAW, not merely the spine's letter: the metaprogramming verdict mandates the opposite polarity for an entire fact class — 'Optimizer facts about constant data MUST be compiler-derived by inspection... Writer-asserted facts about constants are forbidden' (round2-metaprogramming.md What-must-be-true; round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md COMPILE-TIME COMPUTATION). Proof-carrying source is not 'the completion of the round-2 commitments'; at constant data it is expressly outlawed, and the concession that 'the literal never-infers formulation may not survive' understates this as an amendable edge rather than a floor violation.

The headline claim that F006's failure mode 'dissolves under the charter's own cost model' overclaims: F006's negative scope prices 'usability costs' ambiguously, and round1-aliasing-model.md records that SPARK's decidability is 'bought by the bans' — an EXPRESSIVENESS restriction, which under D0 is an admissible performance-forfeiture cost, not inadmissible human ergonomics. The position's own concession 4 admits the frontier; argument (2) and concession 4 cannot both stand as written. Additionally, rejection rates and prover latency hit the machine repair loop directly — a D0-admissible cost the position defers to unpriced thresholds.

Unraised structural defect: proof annotations break the canonical-form floor. 'One spelling per semantic construct' (round2-synthesis SURFACE) is unenforceable for invariants/ranges/VCs — logically equivalent invariants have unboundedly many spellings and deciding their equivalence is undecidable, so proof-carrying source imports a non-canonicalizable surface into the byte-canonical artifact. The claim that it 'dissolves the elaborator-codec second-spelling tension' trades one codec problem for a worse one, and regeneration noise in invariant spelling defeats the content-addressed caching the position claims as a synergy. Relatedly, 'the writer authors what the store already requires' equivocates on 'contract': the elaborated artifact requires the DECIDED vocabulary (types/modes/effects/storage contracts), not SPARK-grade invariants — a much larger surface smuggled under a decided word.

Proof-of-consistency is not proof-of-intent: a checker verifies a stated invariant against the code as written, so correlated code+invariant wrongness passes the prover exactly as correlated code+annotation wrongness typechecks. The 'second derivation rather than an echo' argument assumes the invariant originates from an independent reasoning process, but a writer optimizing iterations-to-green will select invariants it knows are dischargeable from the same plan that produced the code — the weakening-laundering risk it lists is the equilibrium, not the tail case. The checksum value therefore rests on the same untested independence assumption the position credits itself for escaping.

challenges.targetPosition: Rust-division of proof burden (intent-only writer, compiler derivation)
challenges.challenge: The central dissolution move — 'writer proof burden can NEVER buy trust; it can only buy redundancy' — is a non sequitur for undecidable fact classes. For facts the checker cannot derive unaided (loop invariants, value-dependent disjointness, trip counts: exactly A005's consumer list), a writer statement is not redundant with anything; it is the enabling input that converts an underivable fact into a checkable one, because verification is cheaper than synthesis. The spine's 'stated-and-checked' arm exists precisely because statement ADDS facts. Under this regime the reachable fact set is permanently capped by deterministic-analysis decidability — a D0-performance forfeiture the argument prices at zero, conceding only 'restructure loops' (concession 7) without admitting the fact-density asymmetry.

challenges.targetPosition: Rust-division of proof burden (intent-only writer, compiler derivation)
challenges.challenge: The laundering argument (COUNT 3) conflates soundness-laundering with strength-erosion: under the fixed D1 envelope with checked discharge, weakening an interior invariant can never launder a wrong body past a caller — it is a performance-only event invisible to soundness. Interior proof obligations therefore do NOT multiply 'the laundering channel gating exists to close'; they multiply a softer, different channel (fact-strength erosion) that Position 1 correctly distinguishes. The strongest anti-interior-burden argument in the case rests on this category error.

challenges.targetPosition: Rust-division of proof burden (intent-only writer, compiler derivation)

Meanwhile the position's own determinism claim for borrow-checking-class analysis is a design-property assertion with zero implementation cards (conceded), and its pre-commitment to ambiguity-rejection is admitted to degrade if frontier rejection rates force flow-sensitive precision — the same slope it indicts.

challenges.challenge: The independence asymmetry doing the mandatory-vs-optional work — 'caller and callee are generated in different contexts, so signature-vs-body checking catches cross-context hallucination' — is itself an EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED writer-behavior claim: same model, plausibly same context window, plausibly one coherent hallucinated plan spanning both. The position flags behavioral claims generically but exempts this one while resting its boundary/interior split on it. Also stale evidence: D001 is now CONFIRMED and C004 CONFIRMED_WITH_AMENDED_SOURCES per notes/card-verification-2026-07-02.jsonl, so concession 5 overstates its own weaknesses — while D009, the load-bearing silent-degradation exhibit for the boundary mandate, remains genuinely unverified and is not on the confirmed list.

'The solver never touches the D1 envelope' is internally contradicted. The position's own evidence-gap list prices 'check-elision rates' as payoff — but if solver-discharged bounds/lifetime facts ever elide safety-relevant runtime checks, a solver soundness bug yields out-of-bounds writes, memory corruption, and transitively D1-class breaches (its own concession admits 'a false discharge feeds F003/N002 UB channels with N001-unbounded blast radius'). Either solver facts are forbidden from eliding safety checks (forfeiting the claimed payoff and leaving the tier-3 check density high) or the solver IS in the D1 trust path. The 'architectural fence' is asserted, undesigned, and incompatible with the payoff claim; nobody, including the position itself, resolves this.

The optional hint channel creates exactly the forbidden third state. A writer-stated hint the solver fails to discharge within budget is neither checked-redundant, nor unsound-assumed, nor a 'mismatch': if undischarged hints are hard errors, budget nondeterminism makes ACCEPTANCE nondeterministic across solver versions/machines — breaking canonical determinism and content-addressed memoization (a decided substrate); if they are silently dropped, the round-2 rule 'writer-stated inferable facts are cross-checked with mismatch as a HARD ERROR' acquires an unlegislated exception and the spine's no-third-state law is violated. 'Hints can only enlarge the proven set, never inject unsoundness' is true for soundness but false as a claim that the channel is design-complete.

'Prove-or-check-or-omit exhausts the sound design space' overclaims on two fronts: (a) A005 runtime-check-plus-versioning IS static speculation-with-fallback, so the J002-foreclosure argument blurs its own tier-3 boundary rather than eliminating the fourth policy; (b) round-2 explicitly keeps an offline-profile channel alive (trap reports 'doubling as the offline-profile channel for static-vs-profile', round2-synthesis Additions) — profile-guided static fact placement is a live fourth fact source the lattice does not cover, and static-vs-profile is research_needed, not foreclosed. The completeness claim should read 'exhausts the carded envelope', which is much weaker.
