missingConsiderations: The 2026-07-02 adversarial card-verification file (notes/card-verification-2026-07-02.jsonl): confirms ten previously-provisional cards including C004 (with sharpened scope) and D001; all three positions argue from the pre-verification state, and the still-unverified set (A005, A003, D009, D010, J001/J002, C002, C005) is exactly the load-bearing set for this debate — grounds for the same stale-evidence remedy round-1 violation-semantics imposed.

Monotone coupling with the escape-hatch sibling topic (D1 open list): higher mandatory burden (P1), higher conservative-rejection rates (P2), or budget cliffs (P3) all raise pressure into whatever unsafe/FFI region round-3 legislates, relocating facts from checked to trusted exactly where payoff is highest ('marking metastasis', unsized). Deciding proof burden and escape-hatch friction independently can produce the worst composite: maximal burden + cheap escape reverses the intended fact-density gain. No position models the joint decision.

FFI fact-attenuation versus the no-third-state law: attenuated FFI facts are by definition neither stated-and-checked nor checker-derived, so the spine taken literally makes the (undesigned, soundness-precondition) FFI wall's declared attenuation a contradiction in terms. Every position cites both the spine and the wall without noticing the proof-burden ruling must either legislate an exception fact-class or attenuate to zero facts crossing — which reprices everyone's 'shrinks toward the FFI wall' rhetoric.

Canonicalizability of proof annotations: invariant equivalence is undecidable, so 'one spelling per semantic construct' cannot extend to a proof-carrying surface; equivalent-but-differently-spelled invariants are distinct canonical bytes, breaking both the drift-diff machinery's semantics and content-addressed proof caching under regeneration-heavy writers.

Obligation-generation completeness as the SHARED soundness surface: P3 names it for itself (a missed obligation class is a hole no prover closes), but it applies identically to P1 (VC generation from writer annotations) and P2 (the borrow checker's implicit obligation set). It is the common uncarded TCB core dominating all three soundness stories, and only one position mentions it, for itself only.

Contract-edit gating scope for proof annotations: gating currently covers declarations (signatures, law bundles, lifetime contracts). Whether interior invariants are gated is undesigned in all positions — gate them and repair loops stall (the writer cannot edit the invariant it failed to prove); leave them open and strength erosion is silent. P1 names the dilemma as a risk; nobody proposes the policy, and the answer differs across all three regimes.

False-REJECTION economics (checker incompleteness, not unsoundness): all three regimes reject some correct programs (undischargeable VCs, borrow-checker conservatism, budget exhaustion escalated to errors). The 'checker says no, code is correct' repair workflow — restructure vs hint vs weaken — may dominate iteration cost under an unattended writer, is a different diagnostic class from any experiment currently in the round-2 program, and no position designs it.

Per-fact-class decomposition of proof burden: round-1 already ruled that uniform violation-semantics across noalias/bounds/overflow/purity 'may be a category error'; the same classes differ in derivability, statability, runtime-checkability and discharge difficulty, so the round-3 answer is plausibly a per-class burden table (e.g., bounds→check, disjointness→writer-hint+solver, types→checker) rather than a single regime — none of the three cases is framed to permit that outcome.

Threshold pre-commitment discipline: the round-2 pattern is pre-committed reopen thresholds before experiments; only P3 gestures at one (discharge-rate floor). No position states the shared decision function mapping experiment outcomes (catch rates, iterations-to-green, fact coverage, runtime delta) to a burden level, which is what would make this round adjudicable rather than re-arguable.

The amended C004 scope (2026-07-02): data races prevented, general race conditions NOT preventable and not memory-unsafe alone in safe code — benign for D1 as worded, but positions citing C004 as tier-0/D1 discharge should carry the amended scope, and the amendment is itself evidence the verification process changes card content, strengthening the case for verifying A005/D009 before any proof-burden ruling.
result.sharpestTension: A single trilemma at the high-value fact frontier, crystallized in the P1-vs-P2 head-on collision: is a writer-stated, checker-verified fact fact-CREATION (the only route past the checker's decidability cap — P1) or trust-free redundancy whose error surface should be minimized (P2)? Both positions derive their answer from the SAME decided law — the verification spine's 'stated-and-checked or checker-derived, no third state' — and the law is silent, because 'stated-and-checked' is simultaneously the mechanism P1 maximizes and the channel P2 caps at boundaries. The stakes are asymmetric in a way no evidence can currently price: the optimizer facts worth the most under D0 (dependence-freedom, value-dependent disjointness, whole-execution noalias, purity) are precisely those that (i) checker derivation cannot reach — so P2's ceiling is a PERMANENT performance forfeiture, (ii) writer statement cannot be shown to supply strongly — correlated fallibility plus the weaken-until-provable equilibrium make P1's fact-density gain an unproven upper bound purchased with the largest one-way door (non-canonicalizable proof surface, vocabulary churn, maximal regeneration event), and (iii) solver discharge and runtime checks fail exactly there — budget cliffs concentrate in the hot loops, and A005 checks cover only loop-bounded disjointness, so P3 quietly omits the same payoffs while enlarging the TCB and leaving its hint channel in a spine-forbidden third state. All three regimes are D1-sound by construction, so the debate cannot be settled by soundness argument at all; it reduces to fact-density-per-unit-of-writer-competence, where the deciding variables (AI invariant quality and repair rates, SPARK-class discharge rates, frontier size, payoff magnitude) have literally zero cards. The unresolved core: proof burden is the one dial that chooses WHICH failure currency the project pays under unknown writer error-rate — recoverable compile-loop explosion (P1), permanent conservative forfeiture (P2), or trusted-computing-base growth with nondeterministic performance (P3) — and the corpus, by every position's own concession, cannot rank those currencies today.

challenges.challenge: The headline theorem — 'the fact-attenuation function is identically zero, its domain is empty' — is falsified by the position's own machinery, not just its concessions. Three LangHeap-adjacent channels remain: (a) copy buffers: the foreign callee must receive a POINTER to whatever buffer holds the by-value copy; if that buffer is LangHeap, the callee can retain it and a later free makes use-after-free representable (D1 violation outright), so buffers must be arena-resident, which means every 'cheap' crossing is actually LangHeap→arena copy, call, arena→LangHeap copy — a double-copy discipline nowhere stated or priced; (b) exported callbacks: under the round-2 closures floor, function values are env-structs, and callback envs live in LangHeap unless a new arena-env restriction is invented — so either LangHeap is reachable through the callback trampoline/handle table or callbacks may only capture arena state, a severe unpriced restriction; (c) the handle table itself is a language-side shared mutable root. 'There are no survivors' is therefore an overclaim; the survivors are the wall's own infrastructure.

Quarantine quarantines DATA, not CONTROL: a foreign thread invoking a language callback executes language code that touches LangHeap by construction. At that moment 'foreign threads can only touch the arena' is false, C004 Send/Sync predicates (which the card scopes to Rust's own threading discipline, with incorrect impls being UB) have no defined meaning for a thread the runtime did not create, and 'D1's race-freedom needs no foreign happens-before modeling' collapses. The position must either ban foreign-thread callbacks, add a runtime thread-identity check at every callback entry (unpriced, uncarded), or model foreign happens-before after all — and it chooses none. The claim that C001/C004 'quantify over language threads only' is design stipulation asserted as if it were card content; neither card says this.

The arena is not actually 'fact-free' in the way the argument needs, on two counts. First, D1 applies to language-side code too: two LANGUAGE threads racing through arena accessors is a data race the fixed constraint says must be impossible, so accessor ops need atomic-grade lowering or an ownership discipline over handles — i.e., the arena grows exactly the checked-access vocabulary quarantine claims to have eliminated; the concession admits relocating the OPTIMIZATION tradeoff but not the SAFETY machinery. Second, the round-2 memory floor requires a 'writer-stated, compiler-checked storage/lifetime contract' on every allocation; for arena residents the compiler cannot check lifetime (foreign code controls it), so the floor's checked component is vacuous there and the residency 'contract field' is a trusted assertion — the very artifact class the position attacks Position 2 for.

Citation defect: 'F001's negative scope flags programmer-truthfulness dependence' attributes a structured negative-scope field to a card that has none — F1 in verified-findings.md carries confidence/vote/evidence/sources only; the truthfulness caveat comes from caveats.md and round-1's treatment of F005. Separately, the accelerator/device-memory precedent is honestly flagged uncarded yet is load-bearing: the arena-residency mitigation is the position's ONLY answer to its own charter-collision risk, and it rests entirely on an admitted corpus gap (GPU/heterogeneous). If that precedent fails to card out, the position's answer to 'hot interop data' is 'pay prohibitive copies,' full stop.

'The wall adds exactly one SANCTIONED third class — stated-and-trusted' is unsupported: no verdict sanctions it, and the verification spine says verbatim there is no third state. The position's real textual basis is the contradiction inside round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md itself (line 23 'declared fact-attenuation' vs line 33 'no third state'), and intellectual honesty requires arguing that the spine must be AMENDED at the wall, not claiming the amendment already exists. Worse, the position repurposes round2-modules line 25 while deleting its content: that line's mechanism is contracts 're-verified against BODIES on every build' — the redundant check IS the point; 'same vocabulary, discharge switched to declared-trusted since no body exists' preserves the syntax and discards the property the line was written to guarantee.

The position fails the admission test of the very round-1 candidate it cites as licensing it. The round-1 leading candidate permits human-asserted contracts 'only in per-fact explicitly marked contexts, DYNAMICALLY CHECKABLE IN DEBUG BUILDS' — and the position's own evidence-gaps list concedes that whole-execution wall properties (escape, thread-publication) 'have no carded runtime-check mechanism... even in debug builds' and that no sanitizer cards exist at all. So wall declarations are marked assertions that structurally cannot meet the checkability condition of the marked-assertion tier. The 'fuzz under sanitizer builds, lint against foreign headers' testability story is asserted against the position's own admitted gaps.

'Pure conservatism without declarations is inexpressible under the ownership floor' is false, and it is the position's load-bearing forcing argument. A uniform post-state rule — every reference passed to extern is treated as escaped, with the allocation required to have been declared foreign-shared at its allocation site — types every call with zero per-function trust; that is exactly Position 3's demotion and Position 1's arena residency. What the ownership floor forces is a POST-STATE RULE, not per-function trusted effect/escape declarations. The design freedom the position claims is compelled is precisely the thing under debate.

The auditability claim — 'list the declarations whose taint reaches this code, a computable set' — has no mechanism under it. What round-2 mandates is provenance for DIAGNOSTICS and a fact-SOURCE rule at the verifier; transitive taint of facts through LLVM's pass pipeline is a different and uncarded artifact, and the corpus warns against exactly this assumption: misleading-comparisons #5 (IR metadata is a conditional carrier), the flagged-uncarded Rust noalias enable/disable/miscompile history, and the round1-ir-strategy gap on representing where facts do NOT hold. Combined with N001 (a false fact has unbounded, silent, NON-LOCAL blast radius — meaning the taint frontier is the whole reachable optimization horizon), 'auditable residual' may in practice degenerate to 'the audit set is every wall declaration in the program.'

'Declared trust contracts are illegal under decided law, leaving withdrawal as the UNIQUE lawful attenuation' is the mirror-image overclaim of Position 2's 'sanctioned.' The verification spine is a synthesis core-sketch item coupled to ir-strategy (itself research_needed), it is not among the enumerated round-2 floors, the SAME synthesis document specifies the wall as 'declared fact-attenuation' (line 23), and the round-1 leading candidate the position invokes for its conservative fallback ALSO contains a marked-assertion tier. At most there is a genuine conflict of decided texts; there is no law in the barrier's favor. The position's own concessions then quietly reintroduce the forbidden mechanism ('scoped-escape annotation... must go through the round-1 marked-assertion machinery'), reducing the disagreement with Position 2 to defaults and gating — not legality.

Permanent demotion, as specified, violates the floors the position invokes: it is a compiler-INFERRED, call-site-triggered rewrite of a writer-stated storage contract, colliding with the explicit-storage-contract floor, the zero-cross-boundary-inference floor (TYPES AND FACTS line: no inference of intent-bearing facts across signatures), and contract-edit gating (a contract silently rewritten by the compiler is worse than one laundered by the writer). It is also ownership-incoherent: a caller holding only &T cannot legally have the OWNER's allocation contract rewritten by its borrow crossing the wall. Fixing this forces foreign-shared to be pre-declared at the allocation site — at which point demotion converges with Position 1's arena residency and Position 2's escape dimension, and the barrier's claimed differentiation shrinks to the semantics of transient crossings.

The reachable-set partition is defeated by a threat the position never mentions: integer-pointer laundering. The partition is computed from pointers PASSED, but the threat model's int-ptr row says provenance verification is 'often no' — a LangHeap address laundered through an integer (in either direction) escapes both the barrier's invalidation set and the demotion trigger. Position 1 explicitly concedes this and couples itself to the unresolved provenance questions in memory-object-model.md; Position 3, whose soundness story depends on the computed set being COMPLETE (imprecision is claimed to only ever cost optimization, never soundness), silently inherits the same coupling. Without a provenance/int-ptr ban, 'imprecision degrades optimization, never soundness' is false — under-approximation of reachability is a soundness hole.

C005 is overread and the cost headline contradicts the concessions. 'A foreign thread mutating retained memory cannot manufacture UB in accepted internal code' overstates the card: racing non-atomic loads are undef rather than instant UB, but USES of undef/poison (branching, addressing) can still be UB downstream, C005's negative scope conditions everything on correct frontend lowering, and C003's negative scope warns IR semantics are not source semantics; moreover D1 bans data races in the composed program outright, so demoted accesses MUST lower atomic-grade (the risks section admits what the core argument denies). And 'costs land where no rival could soundly optimize anyway' is refuted by the position's own concession: a TRUE declared contract soundly recovers optimization on exactly the fine-grained-FFI code where the barrier 'could lose badly,' while the recommended mitigation — staging into unshared buffers — IS copying, eroding the claimed zero-copy advantage over quarantine.

All three answers to the debate question are, at bottom, the same answer, and none says so plainly: the wall does NOT prevent in-process foreign code from falsifying internal proofs — every position rests identically on the unverifiable ABI axiom (foreign code touches only what it was given), differing only in how much OPTIMIZATION is recovered and how the trusted surface is bookkept. The honest D1 envelope statement is identical across positions ('impossible, conditional on ABI-well-behaved foreign code — Rust's shipped envelope'), so advertising D1-composition advantages (P1) or D1-soundness-by-construction (P3) as differentiators is category error; the actual decision variables are unquantified performance (zero cost cards, per caveats.md) and audit-surface shape (uncarded in all directions). The only design that strengthens the D1 claim itself — process/MPK/CHERI isolation — has zero cards and only Position 2 even names it, as a bottom tier.

unsupportedClaims: P1: 'the fact-attenuation function is identically zero — its domain is empty' — contradicted by the position's own concessions (callback reentrancy, ABI axiom, arena-resident data) and by its unexamined infrastructure (copy-buffer residency, callback envs, handle table).

P1: 'C001 DRF-SC and C004 Send/Sync-class non-sharing facts quantify over language threads only' — neither card contains any language-thread scoping; this is design stipulation presented as card content, and it fails at foreign-thread-executed callbacks.

P1: 'round1-concurrency's residual FFI-survivor policing objection is discharged — there are no survivors' — exported callback envs, copy buffers, and the handle table are survivors; the objection is narrowed, not discharged.
P1: 'F001's negative scope flags programmer-truthfulness dependence' — F-cards in verified-findings.md have no negative-scope fields; the caveat belongs to caveats.md and round-1's F005 discussion.

P1: arena residency 'matches the shape of the highest-performance interop in actual existence' — self-flagged uncarded, yet it is the sole load-bearing answer to the position's own charter-collision risk (GPU/heterogeneous is an admitted corpus gap).

P2: 'the wall adds exactly one SANCTIONED third class — stated-and-trusted' — nothing sanctions it; the verification spine verbatim denies a third state; the position needs an amendment it claims already exists.

P2: wall declarations are 'small, testable propositions' an AI toolchain can 'fuzz under sanitizer builds' — the position's own evidence gaps concede zero sanitizer cards and no carded runtime check for whole-execution properties (escape, thread publication) even in debug builds.

P2: 'the audit for any suspected miscompile is a computable set' via provenance taint — round-2 mandates diagnostics provenance and a fact-source rule, not transitive taint through optimization passes; misleading-comparisons #5 and the uncarded Rust-noalias incident history warn directly against assuming declaration-derived metadata stays attributable through lowering.

P2: 'pure conservatism without declarations is inexpressible under the ownership floor' — a uniform conservative post-state rule (treat crossings as escapes to pre-declared foreign-shared storage) types every call with no per-function trust; P3 and P1 both instantiate it.

P3: 'trusted FFI contracts are illegal under decided law... withdrawal is the unique lawful attenuation' — the spine is not an enumerated round-2 floor, the same synthesis specifies 'declared fact-attenuation' at the wall (line 23), and the round-1 leading candidate includes a marked-assertion tier.

P3: 'a foreign thread mutating retained memory cannot manufacture UB in accepted internal code' (via C005) — undef uses can be UB downstream, C005/C003 negative scopes condition IR semantics on lowering and distinguish IR from source models, and D1 requires atomic-grade demoted access regardless.

P3: 'partition/escape-analysis imprecision degrades OPTIMIZATION, never soundness' — only true if the reachable set is a guaranteed over-approximation, which requires the int-ptr/provenance ban the position never mentions (threat-model row 2: verification 'often no').

P3: 'costs land where no rival could soundly optimize anyway' — contradicted by its own concession that a true declared contract soundly recovers optimization on fine-grained hot-loop FFI, the workload where the barrier 'could lose badly.'
missingConsiderations: The INBOUND direction is undesigned by all three: foreign-supplied values (callback arguments, return buffers, out-params) can be uninitialized, invalid-representation (out-of-range enums, non-boolean bools, null where non-null is claimed), or aliasing-contradictory. D1 bans reads of uninitialized memory, and language-side marshalling code copying FROM a foreign buffer the foreigner never wrote is exactly such a read. Every position argues outbound fact-attenuation; none designs boundary validity checking, and the threat model has no row for foreign-supplied values.

Foreign threads executing language callbacks cross the wall in CONTROL, defeating all three data-centric mechanisms: what capability state does a runtime-foreign thread carry, is there a thread-identity check at callback entry, and what do C004-class predicates mean for threads the runtime did not create? P3 gap-lists it; P1's core claim silently depends on its absence; nobody designs it. This is also precisely round2-modules' named closed-world reopen trigger arriving through the wall rather than through dynamic loading.

Coupling to the sibling round-3 topic (unsafe escape hatch): round2-modules line 42 groups 'unsafe regions, FFI extern regions, reflection capability grants' as ONE fact-boundary construct family needing a home. Whatever attenuation vocabulary the wall gets will be claimed by internal unsafe regions — if the escape-hatch debate admits marked internal assertions, P3's 'unique lawful attenuation' and P1's 'no trusted annotations anywhere' are both decided out from under them; if it doesn't, P2's wall declarations become the language's ONLY trusted surface and will absorb all escape-hatch pressure. Neither debate procedurally blocks on the other — the same defect the synthesis flags for the periphery generally.

The TCB is never delimited: the allocator, scheduler/runtime, and OS syscalls are foreign code under every position, sit BENEATH LangHeap (the OS reaches the whole address space), and are not crossings of 'the single wall.' The ABI axiom every position leans on is really a TCB declaration that must name the runtime explicitly, or P1's 'Reach_foreign ∩ LangHeap = ∅ by construction' is ill-founded at its root.

D1 applies to language-side access of arena/demoted memory too: two LANGUAGE threads racing through P1's accessors or on P3's demoted set is a banned data race, so the 'fact-free' region still needs checked access discipline (atomics or handle ownership) — the safety vocabulary cannot be zero anywhere, only the optimizer vocabulary can. C002's open UB-vs-bounded question is thus not merely 'confined'; it must be decided for core-language semantics regardless of wall design.

Post-violation attribution under trap=abort: when the ABI axiom is violated (foreign scribble on LangHeap), the failure manifests later as a trap in innocent internal code, and the machine-readable trap report — the corpus's ONLY carded observability channel (C002-adjacent) — will systematically mis-attribute root cause. P2's taint audit covers declared facts, not axiom violations; P1 and P3 offer only partially-effective sanitizer stories they concede are uncarded. Nobody designs wall-crossing logging/telemetry as the attribution channel.

Handle-table/accessor infrastructure requires shared mutable internals whose soundness story is the uncarded 'interior-mutability vs shared-reference-noalias tension' backlog item — quarantine's own machinery depends on an unresolved INTERNAL soundness question, independent of any FFI card.

Copy-buffer residency under quarantine (where does the callee-visible pointer point?) and the resulting double-copy-through-arena discipline — a cost and D1 question sitting inside P1's cheapest crossing mode, unstated by advocate and rivals alike.

The three mechanisms may be one design with different defaults: once P3's demotion is repaired to respect the explicit-storage-contract floor (pre-declared foreign-shared allocations) it equals P1's arena residency, and P2's conservative default equals both; the genuine residual disagreements are only (a) may non-shared LangHeap pointers transiently cross, and (b) may a writer relax the default per-function and at what checkability tier. Framing the round-3 decision as three rival mechanisms rather than two orthogonal knobs invites a false trilemma.

Extern signatures under the mono-only-generics and Result floors: extern functions cannot be generic (no witness tables, no IR-level generic ABI) and foreign failure modes (errno, status codes) need a mandated Result-mapping at the wall under the Result-vs-trap classification rule — small, but core-surface design none of the positions or verdicts assigns.
result.sharpestTension: The corpus's own decided texts both demand and forbid the same artifact at the wall, and every position is built on reading only one of the two lines: round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md line 23 makes the core's compilation model 'exactly one explicit FFI wall with DECLARED fact-attenuation,' while line 33's verification spine rules 'there is no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed' — declared-but-uncheckable wall facts are precisely such a third state, since (per Position 2's own evidence gaps) whole-execution foreign properties have no carded dynamic check even in debug builds, failing the round-1 marked-assertion tier's admission condition. Position 2 resolves the contradiction by pretending the third state is 'sanctioned'; Position 3 resolves it by pretending the spine is decided law and line 23 isn't; Position 1 escapes it only by making the trusted-fact domain empty — at the cost of surrendering F001/F002/F003 payoffs on exactly the hot interop data (device buffers, in-place kernels) that D0's extreme-performance charter exists for, with zero quantified copy/barrier/contract costs anywhere in the corpus to price that surrender. And the deadlock is procedural as well as substantive: round2-modules line 24 requires the attenuation design BEFORE the closed-world commitment hardens, while the caveats.md standing rule plus ffi_abi_runtime (missing_primary_source, priority-high, backlog line 6) forbid promoting ANY of these designs until primary sources exist. Something decided must yield — the spine's no-third-state rule, the declared-attenuation wording, D0's performance expectation at the wall, or the design-before-hardening ordering — and no verdict currently owns that choice.

positionAssessments.position: P1 — Always-check with aggressive elimination (mandatory runtime check for every unproven checkable fact in all build modes; elimination only by proof; identical debug/release semantics)
positionAssessments.strengths: For D1-critical fact classes its semantics is the only one derivable from decided law without amendment: D1 (non-debatable) plus the verification spine's two-source rule plus trap=abort jointly force 'unproven bounds/alias fact => check or prove or reject, never ambient assume', and P1's compare+branch-to-abort lowering is exactly the branch-based shape round2-error-handling blessed. Tested-artifact=shipped-artifact is the strongest predictability property on offer for a machine writer, and trap-with-report is the corpus's only carded post-violation observability story (C002-adjacent, round-2 mandated). Its burden-of-proof posture matches caveats.md's standing rule and N006's evidence that LLVM applies maximal ceremony to poison-generating relaxations. Its concessions were the most complete in the debate (overflow precedent cuts against it, elimination half-carded, relaxed trap ordering unspecified).
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The critic's attacks land. 'Forced by D1' overclaims: D1 forces check-OR-prove-OR-reject and explicitly delegates the escape-hatch question, so always-check is a policy choice whose D0 admissibility turns on the absent three-way benchmark — P1 concedes this while claiming 'forced'. The 'no third state' trichotomy is wrong for overflow: N002's unflagged wrap is a sound no-check-no-assume fallback the spine fully permits (the spine forbids assuming unstated facts, not declining to state them), and the only real-world precedent (Rust release-wrap) cuts against always-check there — though that precedent is itself uncarded and inadmissible as deployed. Its performance-recovery mechanism rides A005 read past its text: the card is unverified (absent from the 2026-07-02 verification set), says only that runtime checks 'influence' vectorization, was already discounted by round1-arrays-loops as vectorizer-internal speculation, and its loop-invariant pointer-range guard shape does not transfer to per-element bounds/overflow checks where P1's cost concentrates. 'Byte-identical semantics in all modes' is not yet a defined claim: it presupposes a sound relaxed-trap-ordering spec (absent; without it checked inner loops face a vectorization cliff under strict trap ordering) and concurrent-trap semantics (tasked to this debate, unanswered). Elimination rates are unmeasured and its fuel (range types) is an undecided, uncarded feature. It has no composition story for a sanctioned unsafe hatch if the safety-envelope debate lands one.

positionAssessments.position: P2 — Graduated T0-T3 (proven assumed everywhere; marked+proof-attempt-logged assume trap-free in release, debug-checked; unmarked unproven facts checked in release; conservative fallback at unverifiable boundaries)
positionAssessments.strengths: Only position that expresses per-fact-class decomposition, which the evidence says is mandatory (bounds, overflow, and alias disjointness have different checkability, fallbacks, and D1 criticality — the round-1 verdict's 'category error' point). Its T3 default coincides with the forced floor (unmarked unproven => checked in release), T1/T0 are shared and sound, and it degrades gracefully: if the escape-hatch ruling is zero-hatch, it collapses cleanly to proven-else-checked. The proof-attempt-and-log gate is the debate's one genuine design contribution to D1's delegated 'proof burden on AI writers' question — attempt-required/success-not-required is a coherent point between Rust's bare unsafe and SPARK's proof-or-reject, and D0 makes its ceremony admissible. Its concessions were candid (spine conflict, uncarded T2 for alias, attempt-gate unprecedented).
positionAssessments.weaknesses: T2 as written is illegal under decided law: the verification spine admits exactly two fact sources and says there is no third state; P2 concedes needing a formal amendment while its core argument asserts the log already IS lawful provenance — resolution by fiat, as the critic charged. It also pre-decides the escape-hatch question D1 delegated to the sibling safety-envelope debate. Its alias ladder exceeds the round-1 leading candidate it claims to instantiate: the amended candidate restricted the asserted tier to facts dynamically checkable in debug builds and explicitly held general scoped-noalias to proven-or-conservative; for that class T2 is logged-assume-with-no-check — a log audits, it does not test, so the 'ships contracts testing never executed' failure is relocated, not eliminated (no Miri/UBSan/TSan-class card exists for the debug leg). The overflow default binding is silently left open ('checked modes' parenthetical), so the policy is incomplete on one of the topic's three classes. Governance holes are real: adding an assume mark is not a contract edit, so contract-edit gating does not police the metastasis path, and prover-version churn makes the T2 residue unstable across toolchains. 'T1 sound without further rulings' is conditional on provisional C004, the unrun aliasing round 2, and the undesigned FFI wall.

positionAssessments.position: P3 — Prove-or-reject with explicit checked ops as the only opt-in (no compiler-inserted checks anywhere; residuals must be writer-spelled checked operations lowering to trap=abort)
positionAssessments.strengths: Maximizes the proven tier, the only region of the corpus with well-attested wins (F001, F006), and its truthful-lowering discipline discharges the F003/N002/N005 frontend obligations by construction rather than by promise — the cleanest satisfaction of the verification spine. It is correct that check visibility in the re-read artifact matters for a machine writer, correct that D0 genuinely reprices the historical SPARK usability objection, and honest that it hardens beyond the recorded round-1 lean. For overflow, round-2's one-mode-per-node law does most of its work: a checked node as the visible opt-in and proof-gated assume is nearly decided law already.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Its central entailment argument is refuted by decided law itself: the round-2 MEMORY floor makes deallocation compiler-DERIVED but surfaced as explicit IR drop ops plus machine-readable reports — decided law's own template for compiler-inserted, artifact-honest operations. Canonical form therefore entails 'checks visible in the elaborated artifact' (which P1/P2 can satisfy), not 'no compiler-inserted check exists'. Combined with its own claim (1) that the accepted-program set equals the implicit rival's, the entire differentiator reduces to an EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED writer-behavior bet (restructure-toward-provable vs checked-op spam), with the workload census absent and discharge rates uncarded — under D0, where spelling is free, it is P1 with the elaborator's pen handed to the writer plus a psychology wager. Evidence hygiene is worse than acknowledged: N004 cited without its confirmed load/call-only scope limit, A003/A008 unverified, range types undecided. An SMT-grade prover in the trust path cuts against round-2's closed-decidable-checking posture, whole-program provability makes rejection nonmonotonic under distant edits (undesigned repair-loop divergence), and it is silent on both debts assigned to this debate (concurrent-trap semantics, FFI-boundary fact policy).

keyTradeoffs: Tested=shipped fidelity vs release performance: retaining every unproven check in release buys identical semantics, attributed aborts, and the only carded observability story, at a runtime cost that is unmeasured; any trap-free tier buys an unmeasured speedup at the cost of shipping IR contracts testing never executed. Both sides of the delta are unquantified — the three-way benchmark is the arbitrating artifact and does not exist.

Third-state legality is a normative prerequisite, not an evidence question: the verification spine ('no third state between checked-redundant and unsound-assumed') reads as forbidding any trap-free unproven assume, while the same synthesis tasks this debate to 'design graduated obligations' and D1 delegates the escape hatch to the safety-envelope debate. Each position resolved this contradiction by fiat (P1 abolishes, P2 quarantines-by-assertion, P3 declares unnecessary); it must be ruled, not argued.

Fact-class heterogeneity forbids a uniform policy: bounds violations ARE memory unsafety with no sound fallback (check, prove, or reject — D1-forced); overflow has a sound spine-compliant no-check fallback (unflagged wrap, no nsw), making its check a correctness/performance policy, not a D1 obligation; alias disjointness is runtime-checkable only in A005's loop-bounded pointer-range form, so its general form admits only proven-or-conservative. One policy per class, not one policy for the topic.

Who spells the check: the MEMORY drop-op template makes compiler-derived-but-artifact-surfaced operations lawful, collapsing most of P1-vs-P3 into a near-free spelling choice under D0; the surviving difference is a writer-behavior experiment (rejection-pressure restructure vs checked-op metastasis vs silent insertion), not a semantics question.

Elimination-dependent performance vs predictability: every position's admissibility under D0 leans on check elision or proof discharge whose rates no card measures, and optimizer-dependent elision collides with the unresolved predictable-cost-model vs guaranteed-optimization-floor charter question — a reportable, guaranteed elision floor is the only route consistent with the dispatch verdict's hostility to silent optimizer-dependence.

Universal A005 dependence: all three positions reconcile checking with performance via one unverified provisional card whose text covers only loop-invariant pointer-range guards in vectorizer-internal speculation, already discounted by round1-arrays-loops; additionally, strict trap ordering under decided trap=abort makes vectorization of per-element checked loops semantically illegal absent a relaxed-ordering spec that exists nowhere — a cliff no position priced.
evidenceGaps: The three-way benchmark (UB-asserted vs runtime-checked+versioned vs proof-gated) on bounds/overflow/aliasing kernels — speed, code size, check-elision rates under closed-world + mono floors; named by round 1 as the single most decision-capable missing artifact and load-bearing for every position's D0 admissibility.

A005 verification and scope: the card carries no adversarial verification vote, its text says checks 'influence' vectorization (not that a passed guard legitimizes assume-grade facts), and whether guard-then-assume exists at all outside loop-invariant pointer-range checks is uncarded — prerequisite to every position's performance story.

Sound relaxed-trap-ordering specification against LLVM's speculation/side-effect model, and concurrent-trap semantics (abort ordering, report atomicity, FFI-introduced threads) — the latter explicitly tasked to this debate by round-2 and answered by no position.

Post-check fact recovery (whether llvm.assume or dominating checks recover nsw/inbounds-grade optimization; N004 confirmed inapplicable to arithmetic results) and the compiler-inference fourth channel — either could reprice the entire debate.

Language-precedent cards: Rust release bounds-check policy and get_unchecked density/incident history, Swift trap-always costs, Ada/GNAT overflow-check overhead, SPARK proof-discharge rates — currently deployed as load-bearing support by P1 and P2 despite being absent, which caveats.md renders inadmissible.

Debug-mode dynamic checker coverage (Miri/UBSan/TSan class) for noalias/inbounds/nsw assumes — decides whether any marked-assume tier has a debug-check leg for the high-value fact classes; without it T2 has no middle rung where it matters.

result.leadingCandidate: P3 amended — a single gated fact-boundary construct family, absorbing P2's manifest form and P1's writer invariant, with prover-gated discharge as the preferred mechanism.
